title: Positive Experience
review_text: Overall, the home buying process was seamless. The KB home team was very professional and communicated with me throughout the entire process. I have encountered some minor issues where some has been addressed due to contractor errors. There are some that I have encountered and are awaiting resolution. In comparison to some of the horror stories that I have heard I am thankful for only minor problems. I do get a third party inspection and will get another closer to my one year mark.

title: Great buying experience, great team, overall great house
review_text: A Mostly Wonderful Home Buying Journey with Room for Improvement Overall, my home buying experience was very good, largely thanks to the excellent people involved, though the final product's workmanship left a bit to be desired. The human element of this process was truly exceptional. The entire team made the journey enjoyable and manageable: Brad was outstanding in sales. He was knowledgeable, attentive, and made us feel comfortable and confident from our very first visit. His professionalism set a high standard for the entire process. Heather was a fantastic liaison. She was incrediblyorganized, responsive, and always ready to help navigate the many moving parts of the build. She was a true advocate for us! Rachel at the Design Studio was a pleasure to work with. Her expertise and guidance made the selection process fun, and the materials we chose for the home were of very good quality and exactly what we wanted. While the team and the materials were excellent, the workmanship fell a little short. We had a few problems that were somewhat frustrating to deal with after we moved in, ranging from minor cosmetic flaws to a couple of more significant items that needed fixing. While the problems were eventually addressed, the need for these fixes detracted from the otherwise stellar experience. It's a shame that the high-quality materials and excellent design studio selections weren't matched by equally high-quality execution in every detail of the build. I would absolutely recommend this builder based on the exceptional sales experience, the high-quality materials, and the outstanding support from key team members like Brad, Heather, and Rachel. They are a massive asset. However, future buyers should be prepared to be diligent with their inspections, as the workmanship quality needs a little improvement to match the rest of the fantastic home-buying experience.

review_text: This is my 2nd time buying KB home and have no regrets. Got a home that perfectly matches my requirements. Overall, I am happy with the end result. I appreciate the hard work and professionalism of the team. From sales, lending and construction I have received all necesssary support, attention and communication. One area of improvement would be attention to detail on outside vendors work. There were some corrections needed after I moved in and KB home team very diligently worked with these vendors to resolve issues. I understand that it's normal to encounter some issues after moving in, butpaying closer attention to vendors' work during the early stages could greatly enhance the overall experience for homebuyers. I am grateful to Severo, Michelle, Ray, and Bryan for ensuring a smooth process throughout my home buying, closing, move-in, and settling in stages.

Too thin coated paint makes it very flimsy and leave mark on the wall in case hit by even any soft bag. Few on-line video about how to complete warranty documentations, whom to contact (there are many parties involved--manufacturer, installer, KB reps) will help the home owners and reduce the numberof phone calls to KB home CS.

title: Great experience but………
review_text: My home buying experience was great and seamless. I didn’t have any issues during the process, which was great. Everyone was incredibly helpful during the process and I greatly appreciate everyone’s assistance along the way. Sandra (mortgage originator), John (home sales agent), and Isabel (loan underwriter) are awesome employees and pivotal people during my home buying experience. This is my first time purchasing a home, new construction and I’m glad I purchased with KB Home.. My only complaint at this time has been the lack of understanding and empathy by the Warranty person in my area. Iofficially moved into my home about 3 weeks ago. Primarily, I work from home so, I need internet. I contacted Comcast to install cable and internet prior to moving in. A Comcast Technician came by and said the conduit is jammed and a cable line needs to be dropped. I thought the work was completed after a ticket was submitted; however, another technician came out and said the same thing. At this point, I’ve had 0 service for 2 weeks and I’m completing work duties using the mobile hotspot feature on my work phone. Of course, that’s extremely frustrating and upsetting. Anyways, I had been calling Comcast several times, inquiring about the status. Subsequently, I reached out to the Warranty guy and asked him about the issue and wondered who is responsible for what’s transpiring here? Because I don’t have service. He said Comcast and the conduit isn’t jammed. He can’t send any of his guys there because other homeowners had the same issue in my neighborhood but whenever he sent his guys, they never identified an issue. He told me the technicians need different tools. So, I asked him I’m supposed to continue going back and forth with Comcast just to get some service and resolve this issue I’m having? He said yes, it’s not their problem and he’s sorry. At that point, I was furious and told him directly that this is ridiculous, no one cares, and thanks! No response from him. Where was the empathy or compassion? Imagine still having no service for 3-4 weeks now and you’re a federal government employee, working on your employer’s work phone mobile hot spot. Who wouldn’t be upset? And to even assume without verifying is absurd. Basing prior experiences to what I’m experiencing is very foolish. Although Comcast is set to install next week, the Warranty guy has left a bad taste in my mouth. Yes, I submitted my initial review with phenomenal ratings but I’m extremely disappointed in the lack of care and compassion from the warranty person.

title: Our experience with KB Homes has been nothing short of exceptional.
review_text: From the moment we stepped into the KB model home office and met Marbelia, our experience with KB Homes has been exceptional. Marbelia patiently walked us through every detail, answering our questions and making us feel confident in our decision to purchase one of their houses that was ready to move in. Navigating the financing process can often be a source of stress and uncertainty, but thanks to Anthony and Tara from KB Home finance, our experience was remarkably smooth and efficient. They worked tirelessly to ensure our financing went through quickly, with minimal back-and-forthcommunication. I was pleasantly surprised at how seamless the process was, with only two clarifications needed on our part. KB Finance truly sets the standard for professionalism and efficiency. During the home inspection, we encountered a few minor issues, as expected. However, Cristian from KB Homes was incredibly responsive and addressed most of these concerns promptly. He also provided clear explanations when certain issues were within state code regulations. It's evident that KB Homes takes pride in their commitment to quality and customer satisfaction. Speaking of quality, we couldn't be happier with the craftsmanship of our new home. The attention to detail and the overall build quality truly exceed our expectations, making it clear that our investment was well worth it. In summary, our experience with KB Homes has been nothing short of exceptional. From the initial consultation with Marbelia to the efficient financing process led by Anthony and Tara, and the responsive customer service provided by Cristian, KB Homes has proven themselves to be a reputable and trustworthy builder. Without hesitation, we highly recommend KB Homes to anyone in the market for a new home.

title: Lily Attached Condo Community: Lot 70
review_text: My sales associate Wendy was such a sweetheart. I genuinely felt like she had my best interest at heart. She went over and beyond for me and my family. She was answering my questions even on her days off. She went the extra mile to get me into the home with the bigger lot at a lower price point. When I had any issues she made me feel heard and validated. There was transparency throughout the process of buying our new home. Every word promised was kept from the builder to the lender. As with any home buying experience, it can be very stressful and the KB team was extremely patient andunderstanding. I had some initial issues with the first lender representative they paired me with. They quickly assigned me another agent that was able to thoroughly answer my questions and address all my concerns. Juan was exceptional. I am an over thinker and I’m sure I drove him insane, but he remained respectful and professional explaining in detail certain loan details that I did not understand. The craftsmendship is the best I’ve seen. I shopped around and looked at other builders final home builds, and KBs quality surpassed all others. KBs customer service after purchase is amazing as well. I’ve had no issues reporting small issues and getting the issues resolved within a day or two. They are prompt and the reps they send out are friendly and professional. As with any builder, they have a lot of homes to get through so There’s always room for improvement with the quality of work being done on the homes. The finishing on the brand new tubs they installed were cracked in multiple places. The finish appeared scratched and scuffed. They have someone coming out to refinish the tub, but it’s still a place for initial improvement. The carpet was lifting and nails or staples were poking our feet when we moved in. Again, KB remediated the issue quickly, but the over all quality of the carpet/vinyl flooring install wasn’t the greatest. I had to scrape paint off the vinyl flooring and the base boards were poor quality. They had tons of dents and bumps. But I’m sure the average person wouldn’t have noticed. Overall I was very pleased and happy with what KB offered and continues to offer our family with our new home. I would recommend to family and friends.

title: Overall a great experience buying our first new build
review_text: We had a wonderful experience in our community with our sales team, and construction team. I hear your experience really depends on these teams. If you ever get Braxton, Jason, Diane or Lindsay you are in excellent hands. Our Superintendents stayed on top of the crews to put together a quality, and sturdy home. The home is incredibly well insulated, and blocks out external noise very effectively. This is likely because of the Energy Star certification. Since it's a new construction, you need to set your expectations appropriately: no home is perfect. There will be minor flaws andimperfections. You could spend millions on a completely custom home and still wind up with imperfections and things that were done weird. But you can focus on the big things (e.g. concrete, electrical, plumbing, the guts of the house, etc) and ask questions about the little things (e.g. Should the transfer grill rattle when closing the door? Does the grout get sealed?). We are very happy with our home! It was a great experience building with KB Home, and we look forward to raising our family in this beautiful home.

title: Salerno - Hallmark’s single story house - great floor plan
review_text: I have never thought of purchasing a KH Home for myself. I work in the real estate industry and have heard a lot about how KH homes being built with lower quality, cheap and required a lot of upgrades. Knowing this, but a few things that interested me about KB homes was the community is big, mostly located in good convenience location, house square foot are large, allow buyers to chose structural and interior design upgrades that must of the average builders don’t allow especially during the Covid19 time. I have chosen a lot and a one story plan building it from scratch. Overall the buildingprocess took 8-9 months which I was expected and told by the builder. Went to select option upgrades and our house turn out very nice the way we imagined it was going to. We did find a few problems with the AC leakage, and a few other small issues after moved in, but KB has a wonderful warranty team, the warranty manager who scheduled appts and managed the vendors to come out and repaired all of the items we called out within the first two weeks after we moved in. Overall, it was a great experience and I do recommend buyers to look into buy KB homes today and plan to spend $40-$60k+ upgrade to make your house looks really nice on top of the base price given (was still great and average cost per sqt) to start.

title: Helpful Knowledgeable Responsive Professional Efficient KB Team
review_text: To start, KB was the only builder that didn't join the "bidding game" among all the builders out there. They went with "first come first serve", and I was able to sign up and start the home buying process immediately. Alex in the design studio is awesome! She made the entire design process so enjoyable. Our design appointment went over 3 hours and it felt like a breeze. Alex deserves a raise for sure. The construction process was overall good. There was a small hiccup toward the end, but the team was able to fix the issue rather swiftly. Sarah from the KB Home Loan team was alsosuper helpful. She walked us through the loan process with knowledge and worked hard to give us the best rate and pricing possible. It's been a tough market, but the KB team helped us ease through the entire home buying process. I'm grateful. I'm not able to give much input on the house quality as of yet since I just moved in. If given the opportunity, I can give a more informed review of the house quality.

A crack in the cement pad on the driveway noted prior to closing and photographed. Still not fixed. Damage to siding in three places is still not fixed.

title: My Experience
review_text: Though buying a brand new house and waiting for it to be finally ready can be stressful due to not having a firm completion date, my overall experience has been positive. All involved were professional, knowledgable, and responsive in a timely fashion. I was kept up on the progress and asked frequently if I had questions or concerns. I am enjoying my home and look forward to spending many years here.

title: Okay. Happy to still be in California
review_text: The administrative side of things (studio visit and sales agents) we're great. Couldn't ask for an easier experience! Sales agents and our loan officer were absolutely FANTASTIC. KB is a reputable company but I was disappointed in the quality of the home. The longer we're here, the more we see paint that doesn't match, drywall repairs that were never fully repaired, and walls or base that isn't/aren't straight. Paint isn't a huge issue...I have almost ten years of experience in the paint world that I can fix these issues myself, they're just cumbersome. The nails and trash in ourbackyard, I'll never understand. We had to pick them all up ourselves and luckily we didn't step on one. They were EVERYWHERE as if workers threw them carelessly. I will say, when our hood was collapsing over our stove, someone fixed it right away. The super and all those working for KB have been great. I can't stress that enough. I understand these homes are built fast and efficiently, but I guess we were just expecting better quality. The shower rod that was placed in the guest bath wasn't actually on properly and was missing hardware. We purchased a new one ourselves. Overall, we're happy to just own a home in California. And do want to thank KB for their professionalism throughout the buying process.

title: KB Home - We recently moved from San Jose to Gilroy and I would like to
review_text: We recently moved from San Jose to Gilroy and I would like to take this opportunity to thank everyone at KB for making this a memorable experience. We were very excited and a little apprehensive since it was a big move. Christine from sales was so friendly and helped us find the perfect plan. She was always available for questions and ideas! Becky from the design center was amazing at keeping us focused and on track-since it can be quiet overwhelming. She let us take our time with the picks and also helped us when we were stuck. Tim and Peter from the loan processing worked really hard tomake sure we had everything and ready to go when it was time. The process is very complicated and they tried to keep us as stress free as possible ( although loan applications and approvals are really really stressful!) Ryan from customer service is a RockStar! He brings so much energy and gets you pumped up for the big move and beyond! He has worked with us on all the little fixes and even helped out during our appliance delivery. The construction crew and their project manager Serena are awesome! We had a couple of things that needed to be redone and they were on it! My husband and I really appreciate everything you’re team has done and our family is so happy to have moved into this beautiful new home and an amazing neighborhood! Minu & Hari

title: Seamless
review_text: My overall experience from start until now has been amazing! Our first day driving in the torrential rain to see the properties, we met Christine who was very nice and professional. Then we were introduced the Blanca who we loved and connected with very well. She was very transparent and keep us informed. More importantly, she made us feel that she wanted what was best for our situation and not made to feel like a paycheck. Then we met Ed, great guy and down earth. We could have talked for hours each time we met for our construction meeting. Let's not forget Duc and team. They were veryhelpful and persistent with sending weekly house progress reports. We weren't able to come into town as often as we had liked so we couldn't stalk the build of our home so the pictures and status updates were helpful. Lastly, on the final walk through we had a great walk with Don and Mike. They were very nice, friendly and made sure we knew that we were in their good hands moving forward. Thank you KB Gilroy for making this stressful decision easier to cope with and giving us the confidence to know that we are in good hands.

title: Overall pleasant experience
review_text: I found a lot of mixed reviews on KB Homes and was initially very skeptical. Now being in our home for 2.5 months, it was the best choice for us at the time. All of the KB Homes employees we worked with and continue to work with are wonderful. Many things happened during our journey that were out of their control, mainly worker shortage and supply chain issue due to the pandemic. We understand all of this so choose to judge based on the quality of care and information we got from the KB team. The strengths of the team we worked with are communication, friendliness, informative, and patient.KB Homes isn’t going to build you a custom home with every single little detail you could ever want. The contractors they use are probably local and could be hit or miss. We accepted this and we’re luckily able to be close enough to our new build that we went to see it multiple times per week, taking hundred of photos. During the building process, we found several items that needed to be corrected and KB worked with the contractors right away to fix them. There will be bumps in the road as with any project of this magnitude. Overall, we have a brand new home for a great price considering the area we live in. We are pleased with the experience and general quality of the home.

title: Overall, I’m happy.
review_text: As a first time home buyer the experience was easy. The foreman was responsive to my complaints/questions as well as the salesperson. The studio was an easy and smooth experience. As a prior construction worker, some things stood out to me. The structure of the home is solid, the finishing not so much. There were missing paint spots, short cut baseboards that were filled with caulk, mismatched door trim, extra caulk left in the corners where it should have been smoothed out, painters closed doors before paint was dry, etc. The backyard was not leveled properly which caused all the neighborswater to drain and stay stagnant in our yard. This killed our tree and grass. All of these items can be fixed, and the foreman made sure they were fixed. If you are looking for an absolutely perfect house to move into, ensure you have given yourself at least a week or more beyond the forecasted closing date for the builder to fix these problems. The one thing I have suggested to KB is the need for a quality assurance rep to walk the home before the customer does the final walkthrough. I feel this would mitigate customer findings and expedite the closing process. I did have an inspector walk the home, he did not point out the finishing issues I have mentioned. Most of his findings were of little to no concern.

title: Great construction
review_text:
Watching (and learning) through the process was a fantastic experience.  I noticed that inspectors would catch little flaws that I would never have seen. The house turned out wonderful and 
  I did have an issue with corporate policy that only targets people using Veteran's Benefits.  These policies ended up delaying closing by 6 weeks and costing me thousands of dollars only because management would not discuss issues with the customer (only the salesperson).
  Also, after moving in, I noticed a few patches in the stucco when the walls were wet.  These patches would dry to the same lightgray color as the house but turn dark brown when wet.  When wetting down the entire exterior, there were hundreds of these patches.  Rather than a proper paint job on the entire house, they splotch painted all the patches.   I would think a proper final coat of paint would have been more professional, but it might end up looking just fine.

title: Amie is the Best.
review_text: So as far as quality of materials and workmanship the only reason I am giving it a 4 stars instead of five is for three reasons. I really wish you had an upgrade option for sheathing to be real osb (t-ply was used). I am a builder I know it would be very easy to cut a hole in hardie and get into an unoccupied house. The second would be the paint. Painters are one of the hardest trades to get right imo. Runs, holidays, and drips happen I get it double checking these is extremely important and correcting them as well. The third would be drywall. I am just getting use to the knockdown drywallthat is so prevalent in texas offer a level 4 drywall as an upgrade option. It makes all the hard work show in your homes. Our superintendent was very professional and knowledgeable. He worked tirelessly to make sure my family and I were satisfied. Caesar did a great job accommodating me and I will definitely refer my friends and family to you guys from now on. Amie was great she absolutely made sure through the whole process we were updated and informed she is a valued assets to your company. I've met alot of the team and have enjoyed working with them thoroughly. Cheers KB.

title: KB built a great home.
review_text: We had very few issues with our home build. The local sales office, our loan representative and most of all, our building contractor, Gilbert, made all the difference. They were very responsive and went above and beyond the call. We did experience a few issues with the main corporate office and the "personal touch" could've been better. Paperwork was lost, including our earnest money check. The corporate office said they found our earnest check, but we're still waiting for that refund because we had to come up with extra cash at closing to make up for that. But the home is very wellbuilt and small issues like a misaligned cabinet door have been addressed. I would also note that the Studio process of selecting options could be a little more streamlined, less convoluted and easier to navigate. Pricing can be confusing and some options like lighting can be more expensive than just purchasing your own lights and installing them yourself. I would have no reservation in recommending a KB Home to those looking to build. Just be diligent and involved with the process and you should be fine.

The only problems I have are: 1. standing water on the sides of the house due to poor grading or the fact that the houses are too close to drain well. 2. noticed that my lawn grass was not rooted properly either due to the builder not watering daily for the first 30 days or that it was already dried out when placed on the lot. This has caused my grass to shriveland die in places unless we have continuous rain. 3. The worker who laid the tile left small pieces of concrete all over the floor instead of cleaning up after his work. This was disappointing.

The electric power for bedroom outlets is only 15 Amps and this electrical rating is not supporting enough for my portable electric heater. Also, there is one electric breaker is handling for 3 rooms such as bedrooms 2 & 3 and a bathroom 2. I am not very happy with this issue. When I complained, KB authorized person said it's conformed with city code. There is nothing they can help. I want to split or separate the power for those rooms into individual power or circuitbreaker. Each room should have its own circuit breaker. Now if one bedroom uses the electric heater, then the other room can't. Overall, I am very happy with the model I chose.

Since we’ve just been in home 2 months, we are still going through the house and finding small things to fix or improve upon. Some may be location specific to AZ climate as we are from the Midwest. Overall, very satisfied and would recommend.

title: Tweeks were addressed, Lots of nice little things and energy efficient
review_text: First off I dont like the pre-engineered lumber in the ceiling space, but all new homes are built this way. It prevents storing or walking in attic space. Second with all new homes there are tweeks that need to be addressed Travis and Ishedro made sure to take care of all these items. Third nice little features like the side fences go near the front of the house and options to add extra outlets and lights (like in the garage) were nice inexpensive options. Fourth our house (built 2019) is in an all electric neighborhood and our new energy star certified home lived up to its certification. Ourelectric bill was half what our combined gas and electric bills were for the same month last year at our old home (built 2017).

title: KB Homes Value
review_text: KB homes lived up to their promise and delivered a beautiful home. They promptly addressed any issues with repairs and touch-ups, though issues were minimal. I came from a house built in the 1940's where the walls were made of plaster (rock hard cement) and were basically bulletproof and soundproof. Bumping into the walls at the new house always leaves a gouge. I guess that is the new normal, but you might want to consider sound proofing especially if you install speakers in the downstairs ceiling.

title: Mr Tommy
review_text: The one thing that I don't like is, the utility workers do not notify you when they will need to access your property. We already moved into our home and we had our side gate locked and just made it home from shopping, and my wife was in the bedroom changing and two guys jumped my back fence, she ran into the living room and told me she was changing and these guys came over the fence and I ran to the back of the house and I saw that they were digging in my backyard.They had no name tags or uniforms and I didn't know who they were and they didn't speak English, and with all the crime today italmost ended with me pulling my weapon One then was able to say KB HOMES AT&T and I figured out that they were contracted out to KB Homes for at&t digging cable lines. They should have put a note on my door or something because it could have ended very bad. Please make your workers identifiable and start knocking and putting notes on your door and letting you know when they have to go into your back yard, so they won't have to jump your back fence.

title: Overall experience
review_text: My husband and I bought our first home with KB Home and it turned out better than we expected. Alexandria from the Home studio was very patient with us and allowed us to take our time and picking out the designs. Jayson did most of our financial information and explained/even extended himself to explain things. My one critique with the financing department is for them to allow buyers more time i.e a week at least to lock in rates or any other important decisions when purchasing a home, I did not appreciate being rushed into a decision. Lastly our super Joe, overall Joe did a very good jobwith his knowledge on the materials/making of the home. I can’t fully put this on Joe but we did feel rushed to closing even though it was quite a few important things not completely done with our home. We still have exterior items needing to be fixed and have not heard of an ETA on those fixings. Another thing I would like to add which really disappointed us, is the day we closed on our home the keys given to us by Joe to our house did not work. We planned on moving into the home the very next day and not being able to get into the home the night prior or not receiving any response after hours was disappointing. Despite these faults we love our home and our process with KB Homes.

review_text: Overall, the sales experience was a delight and I can't speak highly enough of Tanya and other members of the KB crew I have had dealings with. The floor plan is perfect for my needs and it's a wonderful area, neighborhood and neighbors. While I understand the current labor and supply issues have taken their toll and caused delays, there has been considerable post-signing lack of communication and getting issues resolved that - IMHO - shouldn't be that major of an issue. I keep hearing "we're working on it," "I'll get back to you." Yet they don't. Yada, yada, yada. My advice? Don'tclose until all is finished to your satisfaction. title: Overall Positive Experience.
review_text: The sales side of the transaction was very good. The mortgage staff was helpful, the realtor that sold us the home was helpful at getting us a fair price, and informing us of deals. That was a very positive experience. Downside was samsung products. A month after moving in the oven broke and there's no warranty to cover it. I contacted KBHomes customer service and they said a warranty was available through Samsung but Samsung said no it is only available 1 year from date of purchase. Either way I was caught in the middle and had to pay $2k for a new oven. Not good when you buy a brand newhome and use a stove 1 time. There was some painting issue that we pointed out to the community rep and he still hasn't given us the paint name or come by with the paint (like he said he would) to fix the paint issues. Overall, the home buying experience was smooth and easy. The move in experience leaves a lot to be desired. Customer service once the home is purchased also leaves a lot to be desired.

title: Electric panel terribly inadequate
review_text: There are no 20A circuits where we can run our treadmill or water distiller. Kicks out the GFI every time. I asked the electrical contractor who wired the house for a quote on putting in a single 20A circuit. $1,000. You’d think that a $720,000 home could have had a 20A circuit for a treadmill. Lots of people have treadmills. I’m not sure whether a 15A circuit would even power a skill saw under load. Every person we’ve dealt with through our building process have been extraordinary, wonderful people. We love our new home, but had no idea about the power deficiency. Hope KB Homerealizes what a big mistake this is.

Jill at the design studio is well versed in what options are available, as well has has a talent for interior decorating. The finish work on the house has been a struggle. Alot of imperfections thru out the finish work on the house. Mostly the paint. Painting over dirt on the baseboards one example. Overpaint onhardware and flooring. The upgraded cabinets needs work, still after 60 days. The willingness of the staff to continue to work with me and fix things around the house has brought some ease. Finally, the house was not thoroughly cleaned before receiving. In my mind, I cannot understand the reasoning of building a house and not doing a finish cleanse after all work is performed. These house are built fast, so with all the workers it is probably a bit like herding cats. Still presentation is like first impressions. There is only one time to impress. title: A Surprisingly Pleasant Home Buying Experience
review_text: Overall I had a pleasant experience with KB Homes. From the time I first set foot in the sales office, everyone was very welcoming and kind. You would think that is only because they want your business and while that is a big part of it, I did sense that it went beyond that. Every single person from sales to the lender to the builder on site took care of me. I really did feel like part of the local KB family. I have had new homes before and it was never as warm and welcoming as this was. It was a treat to go into the design studio and choose my decor (although I do wish there were morechoices). And when I changed my mind it was not a big deal, they happily changed it. Meeting the team who actually built the home was great too. The construction foreman and workers never minded my questions and openly showed me their work. I never felt that they were being sneaky or just slapping up another house. Was there things that could have been done better- of course but they are minor cosmetic things. The loan process was the most stressful but even then, the team handled it with professionalism, finding a loan term that fit my budget. I will say the only blemish on this review is KB corporate. They are in the business of selling houses no matter what it takes and it shows. They rushed deadlines and got pushy with even their workers whom they viewed as dispensable and for that I would knock down a few stars for. In review - the local KB Team was great, Corporate KB not so much. I love my house and I hope it holds up for many years to come.

We were given a $15k closing cost credit to assist with buying down the rate and the entire $15k had to be used to get anywhere near other loan companies with no buy down. Materials on the house were also decent but it seems as if the house was made in a rush. Attention to detail was not given on a good amount of items in the house. During the first 30 days we had an individual in the community to assist with anything that was wrong before we had to use the actual warrantyprocess and that individual was over almost everyday to fix small things in the house.

title: What we wished we knew
review_text: When buying a new home get ready to go from 0 to 100 mph in a moment. Be on hand to watch you house being built if possible. Write down or take good notes and ask questions a lot of questions. Our sales represent David did and awesome job keeping us informed and communicating with the builder of our concerns, working with our relator when it came to crunch time and always being available on his days off. Heather did a great job ensuring the project managers addressed our concerns. It is important that you pay great attention to the details of the construction process, take plenty ofpictures before the house is closed in. Look at the crafts work and remember they or only working with the materials provided by the builder. Not every board is perfect this will be more evident when the project is near completion i.e. floors may not be solid when walked on, the walls may not be perfect and have bows, the windows when dry walled may not be true and will need to be floated in to appear square and if measuring for window blinds learn the correct way to measure them because the width at the top won't be the same as the middle or bottom, remember quality of materials. Watch for the quality of paint used the crafts are only as good as the product they are provided and the preparation of the materials. You are very lucking of have the yard fenced and grass installed. That being said, look at the quality of land if it is rocky with little dirt the build will use the minimal amount of soil 4" and install sod on top of that. The company that provides our turf recommended up 6" of soil with a fertilizer worked in before the turf was to be installed because of our soil condition. You will need to apply fertilizer within a month of moving in see the turf supplier's recommendation. Prior to closing have your house inspected by a third party it's worth the money remember these are new homes being built by the crafts and managed by project managers build multiple homes at one time things will get missed. After moving in you will identify find items needing to be addressed follow the process it works.

title: KB Home Review Plumas Lake
review_text: There were a number of issues with the home and most of them were addressed quickly.

title: Still waiting on a few things promised
review_text: There were a few minor things which weee identified when I did the final walk through which needed attention. I was promised that everything would be made right. I can see now that promise was only good until I completed the homeboy survey sent out. Tony had made sure get a few things fixed before the survey got to me. However, since the survey was submitted, all other things on the list have been quickly forgotten about. As a result, my front door leaked water during a recent heavy storm because the weather stripping was not correctly installed on the front or back doors. Had I not beenhome at the time of the storm, my entire living room would have been flooded. Please note, part of the carpet did in fact get soaked and I had a huge amount of water to mop up at the entry. I notified Tony about this and he stated that the previous installed of the weather stripping was no longer being used by KB, but he has yet to send anyone to correct this problem. I was also promised a set of blinds for the master bathtub window. The company who was supposed to install them has not delivered yet. There was paint drops on the kitchen cabinets and no one has come to clean them or the sticky glue on the vinyl flooring. I see now that my mistake was in completing the big survey they hounded me about shortly after I closed. If that was all it took for my home’s problems to be ignored so quickly it makes me wonder what will happen if I ever need to file a warrant claim. I don’t like to be constantly calling to make complaints and it should not require such rude behavior to get things made right. It’s don’t know it Tony will even see this review, but this is my last chance to voice my concerns and at least get a message out to other buyers do not be quick to submit your home buyer survey if you have anything thing which was not completed or don’t incorrectly during the construction of your home. Once that survey is in, they will forget you fast

title: Experience

title: Positive experience
review_text: We recently completed a home purchase with KB, and overall, we are pleased with the results. The builder was notably flexible during the purchase process, accommodating our requests and making the experience smoother. While the project did take longer than anticipated, they were diligent about providing updates to our realtor, which we greatly appreciated. However, we were somewhat disappointed with the classification of certain features as upgrades. For instance, items we felt were essential for a modern home, such as LED lighting in the rooms, were considered upgrades. We believe suchfeatures should be standard in today's housing market. Despite these points, the quality of the construction and the team's responsiveness were good. We would recommend to anyone looking for a flexible and communicative builder, but suggest discussing what is included as standard versus upgrade early in the process.

title: Overall, a positive experience

Even though Liberty and Cadence are both KB home developments, there appears to be an unofficial class system with Cadence appearing to have more benefits. The response to the final punch list appears to be a little delayed relative to earlier interactions with the team. May be due to availability of contractors and not related to KB management of the process.

review_text: Overall it was a okay buying process. The beginning with the sales rep, was very hectic, and unprofessional. After the home was purchased Scott has been great with reaching out to me and making sure everything is good in the home. The only negative part was during the actual buying process. title: On top of things
review_text: I really appreciated all the emails in the building process. Workmanship/Materials: I wish somethings would have been said about the time not being sealed prior to move in. I wish it was also said (if it is said then emphasized more) that the paint will be a matte as well. Both the sealing and the paint should at least be things that people can choose to upgrade through KB. I know everything can't be perfect but I do feel that the painting could have been done better (lots of little dots on my time). Also anything that I asked to be touched up was done, however now it is just asobvious. Trustworthiness: I also feel that it should be explained that if you don't have a realtor to represent you, then KB is just representing themselves which with any company that is not what is best for the customer. Obviously this would take money away from them so it won't happen with any builder. I had a lot of confusion when dealing with KB financial and feel they were talking around my questions to halfway answer so the outcome was best for them. Value I gave 4 because they are very expensive houses. I understand that is the going rate but I feel the sale price is nowhere close to the actual cost to build it. I feel KB definitely does not use the best products (paint company they use says that they water down the cheapest paint that they buy from them). This might be standard with all builders but does not make it "Great" quality. Responsiveness: Great responses prior and once purchased. I haven't really had an issue except for sealing time and paint quality which I had an outside company do. I hope i continue to get great responsive after this review is complete as well.

title: The sale personels, broker, and the management team, all great.
review_text: The builders need to pay more attentions to the fine details. The use of some known brands seems attractive but not practical. double handle instead of single handle on faucets. Drain handle hide behind and set way too low for good looking but you need long and slender fingers to pull it up. missing screws etc. defects on the quartz counter top, punch thru brat nail tips at the base board almost cut my finger, toilet seat fastener cover automatic pop open due to engineering defects, hot and cold water outlet from wall to the pedestal can be seen while sitting at your toilet. we do not expectevery single cement block be perfect but paying half a million dollar home, a few corner chipped cement block is not that expensive to pick a good looking one and safe the defective one for your home art works. front walkway bricks came off as you walk and step on it, near falling disaster on my mover. over all, KB has done an excellent job. Builders need to pay more attentions to detail, fine details; that is what I call workmanship or craftmanship. Top brand names may not mean top quality craftmanship. Enjoy.

title: Love our new KB Home!
review_text: Overall, we had a decent experience. My husband is a contractor and can confirm the majority of the workmanship and materials is excellent. We did experience issues with shortcuts being taken on finish work and it is my advice to be VERY involved, take LOTS of pictures and communicate concerns in writing the minute they occur. I do wish we had insisted on power to the home for the final walk through - we could have avoided things like appliances not working correctly and outlets that were not connected. The design studio is fabulous! BUT - know your prices - there are things they offer(e.g. front door lock upgrades) that are inflated 300% - those things you can save $ and do yourself. Also - read everything - and I mean everything very carefully - we had to send back our design plan 4 times for corrections. The communication of the entire team - ALL people we interacted with throughout the buuying process was stellar! The sales team, job supt., finance dept, etc. all made sure we knew what to do. The fact is - with a new build there WILL be issues - count on it and know this. The key is to stay on top of post closing communication. I have learned that clear, kind and consistent communication and accountability helps get issues resolved. I do wish they were available for warranty work on weekends - we have had to miss a ton of work to deal with post closing issues. Plan accordingly. Overall - we would definitely work with KB homes again - I have family and friends who also had very good situations and experiences. I just want to make sure my review reflects the true reality of what to expect!

No communication throughout the process unless I initiated the conversation but still took forever to respond if I even got a response. Did not educate me through the process so I felt I made poor decisions at the beginning. Didn’t explain things throughly. Towards the end of the process, Tanya took over and justified the wrongs. title: I'd do it again
review_text: The initial shock of what the home doesnt come with was difficult to accept after visiting the design center. By that time they have your deposit, so theres no going back of you dont want to give away money. The sales people at the actual neighborhood site are amazing and bend over backwards for you. That is for sure! But the KB Home finance was a true horror show. Every conversation, every interaction was like pulling teeth and nothing was conducted the way it usually is with a finance group. Oh the nightmare. What a bunch of newbs. The title office, on the other hand, was amazing! Sowhat is NOT included with the house, (that we think should be without being charged extra) is: cabinets in the laundry room, nickel plumbing fixtures, nickel shower enclosure in master, luxury vinyl in the living room (instead of carpet), hardware on kitchen cabinets, elongated toilets, pendant lights at kitchen island, fans in living and master. I will say that we are super impressed with the home insulation. Its by far the best insulation we've had...and we've lived in a lot of homes. The person who was supposed to check in on us after 30 days from movein asked my husband how things were after 5 or 6 days. I havent met the guy and wish I had cuz we need him now...nowhere to be seen and its been almost 2 months. Wish i had his number. You cant check on people's houses when theyre not even unpacked or moved in yet. Not great customer service after the fact.

title: KB home was fine, KBHS was a nightmare
review_text: This was not our first rodeo. This was our third home and we knew exactly what we wanted and knew how the process should work. The process with KB home and its employees was fine. A few things here and there could’ve been improved upon, like clear, timely and concise communication, but we handled it as best we could. However KBHS home loans was a nightmare. We dealt with unprofessionalism when we were called by a seemingly intoxicated agent at 9:30pm on a Sunday. Then we got a new agent who was much better than the first, but still couldn’t answer our questions which lead us to making fourdifferent trips to the bank to get a Gift Letter four different ways because every time we were told it was okay, then for it to come back and us be told it needed something else. Even the bank was blown away and said they’d never had so many issues doing a Gift Letter in the past. It was such a mess and really made our experience negative at the end when we should’ve been the most excited. The house is gorgeous, not sure of the quality of the materials and of course everything in the design center is way overpriced, but there are options for all budgets which we thought was really nice. We hate that there is an HOA that seems very overbearing at this point. One lady in the neighborhood said when she was buying, they told her there was no HOA, then once she was in, one was formed. Make sure you know for sure or get something in writing if that’s important to you. All in all, every neighborhood has positives and negatives, you just have to do your research and decide if the negatives are something you can live with. We love our home, and hope to be here for many years!

title: Had good experience in buying the house process but not satisfied with materials and quality of the house.
review_text: I recently purchased a new home, and while there are some positive aspects, I must highlight a few concerns regarding the walls, paint, and structural issues. 1. Uneven Walls: One noticeable issue with the house is the unevenness of the walls. Upon closer inspection, I noticed that some walls have slight bulges or dips, which affects the overall appearance and makes it challenging to hang artwork or shelves. 2. Paint Issues: There are several paint-related problems throughout the house. I observed uneven paint application in certain areas, with visible brush strokes and inconsistentcoverages. While the new home has its positive aspects, such as its overall design and layout, the presence of uneven walls, paint issues, and structural concerns is disappointing. These flaws affect the overall quality and satisfaction of the purchase to an extent.

title: KB Home: Great Process and Product But Be Vigilant of Excution
review_text: I recently had the experience of building a home with KB Home, and overall, I was very happy with the process. The company was professional and efficient, and they kept me updated on the progress of my home throughout the construction process. I also appreciated the fact that KB Home offered a variety of customization options, so I was able to make my home exactly what I wanted it to be. However, there were a few areas where I felt that KB Home could improve. For example, the execution of some of the finishing details was not as good as I would have liked. There were a few small mistakes,such as bad paint jobs, lack of attention to details, roof leaks and cabinets that lacked proper painting. Overall, I would still recommend KB Home to anyone who is looking to build a new home. However, I would advise them to be vigilant of the execution and finishing details. If you are willing to do that, you will be very happy with your KB Home purchase. Here are some tips for being vigilant of the execution and finishing details during your KB Home building experience: Be present during the construction process as much as possible. This will give you a chance to see the work being done and make sure that everything is being done to your satisfaction. Ask questions if you see anything that you are not sure about. The construction team should be happy to answer your questions and explain the process to you. Keep a detailed record of any problems or concerns that you have. This will help you if you need to file a warranty claim later on. By following these tips, you can help ensure that your KB Home home is built to your exact specifications and that you are happy with the finished product.

title: Good Experience
review_text: Overall, the home building experience was pretty seamless and I am satisfied with the final product. Working with Juliet of KBHS Home Loans was a good experience but I did not agree with some of their policies when it came to locking interest rates and floating down rates. I had two mortgage brokers question the legality of their pay back policy if I were to switch lenders. I also missed out on the opportunity to float down my rate to 5.75% because I was just outside of my window for eligibility. For the most part, the builders were good but there were some instances where I pointedout issues or concerns and they were brushed off or made to seem unimportant. They eventually were able to correct all of the issues but it took a lot of follow up to ensure things were done proper. The design studio was great and there were a lot of excellent options to make selections from. My only gripe is the strict cutoff time for the end of your appointment. I was able to take my time on everything but my external selections. I had to rush to pick brick and color scheme and left questioning my decisions.

title: KB HOMES Review - Orlando
review_text: My experience has been good with the salesperson, the studio personnel, the Superintendent and the original Home Loan representative, during the absence of the original Home Loan rep, things started to get a little off color and NOT as organized as inception, The coordinator was great at collecting the info and handing it off to the proper associates, from there on is where the loan fell apart and the Underwriter was a total dictator and my loan was put together with incompetence. A blind man could have done a better job structuring this loan, I even gave them 100k in advance, 6 months priorto closing to secure the loan and still could NOT get the loan organized in the proper fashion, I would strongly advise anyone who decided to get a loan from KB Homes to be very aware of who they deal with as some people have the structure and experience to do things but I am marveled at how others even have jobs within the Mortgage industry. They should focus more on hiring people with the skill level of accomplishing things instead of people who continuously apologize, and nothing gets done. I terminated the Home Loan with KB Homes and went elsewhere, got what I wanted and expected. I am extremely happy with the Superintendent and the salesperson; they have great professional conduct. Very responsive with a get it done mentality. Everything outside the Home Loan has been a good experience. Purchasing a home is the largest investment a person can make and should always be a good experience.

title: Good quality. A bit disappointed with KB (read review)
review_text: The build is of good quality. There were some minor issues when moving in but were resolved promptly. With exception to an expensive water bill accrued because the irrigation valve was faulty, causing a small but expensive leak in the sprinkler system. I was hit with a $350 bill, that I have no choice to pay with the water district. Customer service was contacted and chose not to say say yes to doing the right thing. Apparently the warranty covers inside the home only. Working in the hospitality industry, we would have eaten the cost in order to preserve our reputation.However I believe KB is not entirely in accordance with its branding message “#1 in Customer Satisfaction”. KB and its lender will gain approx $800k between purchasing the home and interest accrued over 30 year loan. I would think that $275 would be something that could be written off, but I guess KB is solely interested in maximizing profit margins. At the expense of its customers. This is very unfortunate because the staff members I’ve worked with are wonderful dedicated professionals: Amber (realtor, who was very patient and kept us updated regularly ), Gina & Traci (loan officers, who were very patient in keeping us appraised) Paul (Superintendent, his easy going demeanor and passion for magic) & especially to Raymond (Asst Superintendent, whose gone above and beyond because he is Cleaner *read Relentless by Dwayne Grover) have been very professional and easy to work with. They made the homebuying process more manageable & helped lower our stress levels.

title: Juan is empathetic and goes out of his way to take any issue and correct it.
review_text: The build was a slow process understandably due to environmental restrictions (COVID). The studio options process was smooth, with the designer's assistance. During the build process, issues were identified but corrected slowly. The quality of workmanship up to the drywall process was great. As the finer details came into install, things started to slip. Tile staining edges were not stained but painted over; floor planks had uneven cuts and pencil marking are left at the corner cuts. Caulking joints were left until after closing. Besides all the finer details on the house, it's a greatlooking house, and we really enjoy the floor plan/elevation. I've heard of horrible stories of the home building process, but I feel that this is definitely not one of them. I think KB Edgebrook did a good job with my home.

title: KB Homes New Building Experience
review_text: The homebuilding process was stressful as many new home buyers know. The loan process was the most stressful part. I felt like a lot of the documents we were giving were being lost and had to be redone a lot of the times. And the communication between the sales agent and loan officer was not well. I understand having to keep giving documents in order to continue the process for the loan, but to keep being told that documents were never received despite the email confirmation was frustrating. Jared, our sales agent was amazing. He kept us updated every week and met our needs whenever weneeded. Jason, the superintendent of our home was also super helpful. He worked fast and completed the tasks that needed to be done ASAP. Overall, the homebuying experience was great, despite the loan process.

title: I had a great experience overall
review_text: My building supervisor, Nick is great. He has a great personality and is very patient. He listens to all my "concerns" and respond promptly. He even picks up his cell phone on weekend when I have a blown fuse in my kitchen and gave me advice. He had accommodate and assist me to change my initial walk through date when I had some oral surgery done in NY. I had a condo in NY LI area and my experience with KB home and Nick opens my eyes on how excellent builders respond to their customer after the sales. KB could write the text book on customer care. They truly earn their motto on "relationshipbuilding". I am impressed that a lot of employees purchase KB homes in the vicinity. This substantiate the quality of their work. An "insider" knows true quality. Overall my experience is extremely positive. It makes my 3000 miles move form LI to NV very worthwhile. I also want to mention Venessa who is my sales representative. She had kept me inform all the way through the building phase. She is an asset in the sales team. Keep up the good work.

review_text: Overall the experience was good. Took longer than we would have liked, but it's understandable given the world situation right now. title: Attention to detail
review_text: I love my new home. However, it seems that in an effort to complete construction by deadlines (if there are any), corners were cut. There wasn't much attention to detail. Specifically, there were, and still are, MANY carpet staples/tacks sticking out resulting in numerous minor injuries. One light fixture was missing. A few spring door stops were also missing. There were small paint spots on the flooring. Irrigation in the front yard leaked. Most of these issues were remedied after notifying customer service. There may be other issues that are yet to be discovered (hopefully not). All inall, nice. I'm just worried about how the recent heat wave will affect the workers and in turn the quality of construction on current builds.

title: Worth the Wait!
review_text: We love our new home and are very happy with it. There were some delays along the way that could have been handled much better or avoided all together. Considering the climate of the economy being upfront, honest, and fully transparent about the time it will take to complete the home is essential. 15-18 months is a realistic timeline and knowing this upfront would have saved us a lot of money and stress. For those buying once you are in your home it is all worth it, but prepare for it to take over a year to complete and you won’t be disappointed.

title: Opportunities for improvement to better homebuying experience overall
review_text: The initial or first few interactions with each employee encountered was a fairly pleasant experience. During the build of 8 months and 3 weeks as long as there was nothing to have any concerns about, things were great. Most of the time it took to build the house went smoothly. However, there are a handful of opportunities that need definite improvement to maintain customer satisfaction. SALES (Jourdan): First to begin, the sales rep. After signing the initial documentation and expressing our interest in purchasing a home from KB (earnest money), the sales rep was no longer to be seen.Weekly email status updates were days sometimes weeks behind of what the true status of the house build. We would often know what was happening with the house by going by each week ourselves and walking into the house. The simple drive-by photos seemed like the same photo for weeks at a time sent via email with minimal updates included. DESIGN CENTER (Sarah): Overall the staff at the design center were friendly and helpful, but there were moments during the process that just were not relevant to our design choosing process. One example is the rep’s venting about the hose bib placement on their own build. This did not help provide relevant information to our own home. Had the selection been focused on our own build, I would have opted to add on a third hose bib on the opposite side of the front of the house closer to the front door. Additionally, the laminate wood flooring that we chose for our home was praised as being durable and long-lasting; however, in the short time we have lived in our home we have encountered many instances where the floor needed to be repaired from work done during the final stages of the home build. There are divots in the floor and scuffs that are noticeably visible. Point is, the quality of the floor is not living up to the praise that was given by the design center which is unfortunate because the flooring was an upgrade. BUILD PROCESS: We are very fortunate homeowners when it came to the build process of the home. It may be slightly due to our multiple visits during the week, but I would like to think it really is because of the workers on the site are skilled masterfully in their craft. There were minimal complications during the build of our home. Although, it was a little unnerving to have gone through four different construction managers during the 9-month process. The initial construction manager was late in scheduling our first construction appointment to explain the process of the build. Our first meeting with Garrett was held at the one of the model homes to discuss the foundation and look over the approval from the city of the foundation. We did not go to the site to see and have the foundation/land explained. Foundation was built and laid (land clearing, wood structure for foundation outline, bagging, pipe placement, cement pour) between November 13, 2021 through December 21, 2021. While I do not take it lightly to receive the explanation on how the foundation of the home was designed and built for our specific home, there was lag time from completing the design selections on October 30, 2021 to the first construction meeting January 13, 2022. Upon Garrett’s departure from KB he did notify us that February 11, 2022 would be his last day with the company and provided us the contact information for our new point of contact. The following construction manager(s) that were assigned to the Dove Heights community communicated and responded within a reasonable timeframe when questions were asked about the home build. The second construction meeting was scheduled for the frame walkthrough with David on April 8, 2022. David met us at the homesite and walked through the entire home explaining the frame design, build, and structure. The process was thorough and explained well in terms that a homeowner could understand and appreciate. While David was overseeing the construction of our home, he was communicative and responsive. We had a great experience in working with David for the short time he was assigned to our home. Our next construction manager whom was assigned to our home has been the most professional employee of our experience at KB. Duane has graciously walked us through the final build of our home and sought out to make sure any necessary repairs that were needed from the construction of our home were addressed timely and accordingly. Duane’s service and attention to detail did not go unnoticed. He was never bothered in providing us information on how to take care of our home as a new homeowner. As a new homeowner, it was through his customer service and leadership that really bettered our home buying experience with KB. There were many instances through the process that we had considered going elsewhere due to the lack of service or unprofessionalism that we experienced throughout. There were however some key employees that really improved our experience and helped drive home our decision in staying with KB. We strongly believe in giving honor where honor is due. We would personally like to thank the following individuals for their professionalism, integrity, honesty, attention to detail, and most importantly their service provided to new homeowners. Loan Officer Terri who I believe is no longer with the company. Loan Processor Faran – Faran! Thank you a thousand times more for your patience with us and your mad skills in loan processing. That was not an easy feat and you made the process run smooth like a well-oiled machine! Construction Managers – David, even though our time was short, thank you for your expertise and support! Most importantly, Duane! My husband and I truly appreciate all that you do! You are a huge asset to this company and if it truly was not for you and Faran I think we would have walked away months ago. You understand the importance of a person investing in a home purchase which is no simple task on all accounts. Thank you for your cus

title: Could be better
review_text: The experience was fairly easy and yes, the value of the house is unmatched as it is a new build. However, communication could have been better regarding a realistic timeline. We were originally told our move in date would be Oct - mid Nov but it could be delayed. Then, we were told it was delayed to December, then to beginning January, then to late January or early February. We finally closed at the very end of February. However, the originally timing of events communicated to us such as the pre carpet orientation / final walkthrough were all extremely rushed. The pre carpet orientation wassupposed to be around 45 days before closing. However, this was only 2 weeks before closing. A couple days after, we were doing the final walkthrough. This happened because we had gotten word that we needed to close by the last week in February but this wasn't communicated to us until the beginning of February after having asked for weeks on end.

title: Good production homes
review_text: Generally a positive experience. They will try to get you to bundle your mortgage and insurance with their in-house products/services but you can go with your own. Extremely hard to get any customized features on newer technologies, such as internet and solar systems. Such as structured wiring (ethernet, cable, etc) cabinet HAD to be in the master bedroom closet, and no option to go with larger solar system. However, ironically, super easy to customize "old tech" such as paint, fixtures and appliances. I got a good value for a good home, but I think if there's ever a next time, I'd gowith a home builder who's much smaller and hence, responsive to what I am wanting in my home.

title: Mixed Review
review_text: While I recognize that we did not purchase a custom-built house, however, for the price we paid ( half million or so)we expected more…this was our second KB home. We had great experience on our first thus we decided to get another KB home. I said,, mixed as there were quite a couple of people that stood out. Steve was great during our meetings with him from the footing/ foundation to the final walk through. Respected our customs and allowed us to do what we wanted us to do. The only issue, up to this day we couldn’t figure Out how to program our coach lights. A bit complicated even with themanual re the latitude and longitude thing. The loan processors Dan Murphy and Patricia were on top of things. Justin Grasmick was very responsive and quick to solve our issues. We have a few issues here and there… but what surprised us , why won’t people do things right the first time ( carpets were not fully stretched and tucked just to name a few) I understand we are on a time crunch esp during these covid times but that doesn’t mean we cut corners. I wish I could give a “ 10” as coached by some of the agents, but surveys are completed with transparency so that things will be fixed! I do not thrive on giving 5 or 10 stars knowing at the back of my Mind it was not. We still have a few things that needed to fixed here and there…. And I hope the same responsiveness will continue regardless of this review. There is always room for improvement. I just wanted to mention, in the past the builder would leave a small pint of paint to retouch just in case the paint was chipped during moving in, caulk to retouch or so, we have not seen that this time… thus for every little thing we call. It bothers me to see even little flaws as The idea that we bought a new house , there should be no flaws. I might be wrong but that’s my opinion. Thank you.

title: Mostly happy. Ups and Downs
review_text: We really liked Bud. He was our initial contact with KB and unlike other builders we went to he was very down to earth and genuinely friendly. The Design studio needs to learn some things from him. Our experience with them was one that felt rushed and inconvenient to them after we were pushed a month because it snowed and they did not open for just that particular morning of our initial appointment. You should not get to treat someone as they have inconvenience you when you guys closed. Regardless of the reason. As for the actual construction process we felt it went as good as it couldgo with all the issues of COVID and shortages. However we did feel like the subcontractors got careless and lazy to finish out the house. I understand they were trying to get us finished up to meet our closing (which got pushed anyway) and maybe it is because of the year long warranty. The finish out just felt rushed and very much like the feeling "just get it done and if we have to come back during the warranty period we will". We get that and have been using it. But it felt like a lack of caring. Now that being said Kevin and Derek have rocked getting anything we need done. They both have been awesome and you should do what you can to keep them happy. Thank you, Bryan

This cost me several thousand dollars on the start with no going back, despite using their preferred agents. The building process was good and relatively quick despite the early stages of the pandemic. The builder overseeing my home was outstanding and fixed everything I requested, within the bounds of KB's very tight leash. The design center had plenty of options despite being highly overpriced, I performed the sameupgrades myself at 25% of the cost. title: Despite shortfalls, they did come through in the end.
review_text: I bought my house during COVID-19, when things were crazy and uncertain. They did their best to deliver the full customer service experience given the social distancing limitations. My sales consultant was very accommodating and quick to respond to questions/emails. She also sent periodic pictures of the progress. On the financing/paperwork side, I was a little confused and disappointed. Both of my loan experts got changed on me mid-purchase, which led to a lot of re-sending of things already sent, confusion, and frustration. My final down payment amount changed 3 times in one day (after Ihad already secured the cashier’s check). There was a shortage of appliances during this time and I had ordered a refrigerator for the house from the design studio. The fridge wouldn’t be ready in time for closing so they provided me with a loaner fridge, which I was then able to keep. There was then a lot of confusion and miscommunication about delivery of my other fridge. I was given a delivery date by one construction manager which we had planned for Thanksgiving week when I was off of work. When the day came, it turns out that the fridge had been sent back and the construction manager had left the company without communicating the fridge issue with anyone else. I spoke with the warranty guy who got right onto the problem. He put me in touch with the regional construction manager who then tried to troubleshoot the delivery of the fridge. The problem was that it could only be delivered the following week when I would be back at work. The regional construction manager was very accommodating about that. The fridge was actually delivered at about 9 that morning. Apparently it was guarded all day while it sat in my driveway. When I got home from work at 4, there were about 5 guys here ready to move the other fridge and install the new one! I was very impressed and pleased with willingness of everyone to go above and beyond to right something that was essentially beyond their control! Definitely put a silver lining on a situation that had become quite stressful!

title: Amazing customer service !
review_text: I appreciate customer service the most and Bill was amazing throughout the process. He always promptly responded to my questions and was on top of everything. I knew everything that was going on with the house building process. The house itself is also beautiful. The only negative thing I have to say is that there were definitely some things that could have been done better as far as painting and cleaning up better but even with that, they were very good at coming back and fixing those issues which I was very happy about. I can’t speak for all KB homes but I can say that my community and myexperience overall was a great one and I’m glad I chose KB to build my first home.

title: KB Home - During the building process, everyone was communicating and making
review_text: During the building process, everyone was communicating and making things happen to get us into our home. There were few bumps, but nothing major. 2 months after we moved in communication went down the drain and my “POC” stopped replying to my messages for issues that were going on with my irrigation system. Then we get a package from KB about our warranty, and guess what, our irrigation system’s warranty expired over a month ago, which we didn’t know until after the fact. That’s when everything made sense as to why my KB rep stopped replying to my messages concerning the issue. Now I have togo find someone to fix my irrigation system. Take away from this, make sure they turn on the sprinkler system during your inspection so you can see that they work before you close on the house.

title: Chaatic
review_text: Bought a newly built home from KB. There are 3 steps involved. First of ,the construction process was great. The superintendent and the construction manager kept us up to date and were receptive to our complaints and concerns. The home was delivered with minor cosmetic flaws which were fixed. So, we are definitely happy with that. However, the sales person who sold us the lot and floorplan was either not knowledgeable or just plain deceptive. We were told things that proved to be false. I requested several times if there were any model homes for our floorplan at other KB locations for us tovisit since this particular subdivision at the time has no model homes to show for. We received no help there. Seems like the sale was in the bag and we're left on our own. We had to look for ourselves few presentations were made to us and our realtor proved to be false. Next step, the design studio. The person at the KB design studio who took care of the home upgrades was omitting critical info. For instance, we upgraded our bathroom which included a huge clear window. But this was not relayed to us. Who wants a big clear window in the bathroom??? So, the decisions we made was based on incomplete info. Make sure to ask what other changes come with every upgrade!

review_text: I need to say 100% satisfied with the service of the construction manager, New Home Sales Counselor, and Loan Department. The materials are good but could be better. The Responsiveness to issues is fast but not necessary the results are as we asked. The construction workers don't have enough attention to the details. Issues that I want to share with no resolution at our favor.: Asking for a better more capacity AC unit ( I will pay the cost difference), a lot of construction debris over already installed appliances (we asked for microwave replacement) and very bad quality grass. Atthe end, I'm finally happy with my house. Also, until now, the Warranty department is awesome. Let's see how is the warranty service during these next 2 years.

title: Fine Details
review_text: It's the little things in life that matter most and the finish carpentry was extremely poor. Walls not plumb, messy grout lines, dings & nicks everywhere (interior & exterior). I also need to mention KBH's framing means & methods. KBH doesn't cover the whole exterior shell of their homes in plywood sheathing or board of any kind because their homes have been "engineered" and the sheathing is only needed in "certain locations" to meet the shear strength requirements. So KBH uses 25 to 50% less sheathing per home, saving them a ton of money but not necessarily the consumer. From abusiness standpoint I get it, but from a consumers standpoint KBH just seems cheap. Another small rub I had was the 24 inch on center framing (in lieu of 16" standard) for interior walls that aren't consider load barrier.
