title: Our KB Home Experience
review_text: Overall, the experience was subpar. Everything was going great in the process up until the point we signed our purchase agreement. After that, we went to studio and picked out all of our upgrades. Options were mispoken for, for ex: sprinkler system/gutters not included and were told they were. We upgraded to all gas appliances and upgraded appliance options and somehow they mistakenly did not include gas drop ins for our most important appliances such as the dryer and double connvection oven. We paid an additional $6700 for upgraded vinyl flooring that was terribly placed in some sections(youcan feel the vinyl move.) we paid an additional $3400 for our countertops and they were misplaced and uneven with completely obvious and terrible placement seams. Our kitchen cabinetry was not flush with the wall and our cabinet sticks out from the too of the cabinet and retreats into the wall about half ways in. We have cracks in evidently every room downstairs as well as some upstairs cracks as well. Our upstairs tub was likely not plum becsuse it drains extremely slower than all of our other tubs. Our sod/plants were dead upon recieving our home and were told they'd be replaced as soon as we moved in, but a month later we were still waiting for them to get estimates to replace it and not guarenteed that they'd fix it after they told us they would. We ultimately decided to come out of pocket to replace the dead sod and install a sprinkler system. We've been told straight up that the new drywall contractors will not come fix the last drywall contractors mistakes and that they're too busy to fix our issues. We were told all settlement cracks however the size are always addressed at the 10 month mark and not immediately. The date we tried to move in we noticed our furnace was not turning on and had to wait FOUR days for the "emergency line" to dispatch out an HVAC company to repair the furnace. The issue was diagnosed as a miswired unit, so it was impossible for KB Home to ever inspect and evaluate if the unit was even working before we were given the home. The walk through was fairly rushed so we could close on the property and to be frank the walk through was more just a highlight of the basic features of the home. We got bounced back from person to person and superintendent to superintendent until someone finally took the phase and finished it. Even so, we had an 18 month build. We purchased at the end of April 2022 and Closed Mid- October 2022. We didnt recieve the guarenteed closing date until two weeks prior. Getting them to schedule people accordingly to come make repairs has been a struggle because they never show on dates we are told. There is an underwhelming determination to help us fix the issues and it's almost like we are bothering them to fix issues that should have never existed and then persisted. It has been extremely frustrating to be told there is no time to help us with all these issues because they are too busy with other jobs. Its been over a month and many issues are still unresolved. We like our house, but we are just extrememly dissatisfied and disappointed in the worksmanship. Everywhere we look, there is something wrong. I dont think any new home owner would appreciate that. KB Finance was okay. They were attentive and tried to help to the best of their abilities.

title: Do it right the first time
review_text: So many things wrong with the house. Sloppy workmanship on cabinets, baseboard, patio door and carpet. Getting so many appointments scheduled to fix things that should have been done right the first time is cutting into my work schedule and making it hard to get to doctors appointments. So I’m just done. I’m so tired of trying to schedule and reschedule these appointments and have people interfere with my workday that I will likely just leave many items as they are instead of having them fixed.

review_text: The home is wonderful and an awesome value. The only issue ws the mortgage process, never been so felt raked over the coals, and we have very good credit. But the team got us through it, and we couldn't be happier!

title: Overall it was very educational, but long.
review_text: Dahlia and Joey were great to work with. However, the underwriter(s) were terrible to work with. They made me feel I had to prove I was giving honest answers to anything and everything. Took them so long to do things that I needed to pay extra to get more time to close

title: Slow Start, then Fast After
review_text: This could've just been the whole housing market in 2021. Four months after signing, our home was still a dirt pile. But weeks later, foundation was poured, the structure went up fast & filled in soon after. The default paint may scrape off easily in places, but everything else has held up well. Maintenance staff has been helpful around the house too.

title: Great home and process but very poor Sales team.
review_text: The home buying process was a highlight but the information and professionalism from KB Home sales rep was frustrating with misinformation after misinformation. To customers: do your homework and make sure all information provided by KB Home sales rep is accurate or written on official documentations.

title: Sells vs Construction
review_text: When it comes to KB, workmanship is up to par. The PM team is very knowledgeable and attentive to your building needs. KB sells team and relationship building needs work. They are not transparent and are not very personable. They are thier to sell you a product, and thats it. Recommend working with a broker outside KB and expect to be on your own.

title: Initially quick and easy
review_text: Everything was fine until we had to redo the Loan the day before closing. Other than that things were fine and as expected

title: Mixed feelings
review_text: The process with Kb Home has been interesting. On one hand, there are very nice floorplans and lots of options to semi customize your home. However, my experience was a bit challenging because no one shared details with me of the KB Home process. There were disconcerting moments with the sales department, design center, and with the loan processing department, in particular. I have had several challenges getting a response on some issues and currently even with the title company that KB Home uses.

title: Happy with house despite issues
review_text: There were issues not accurate represented before or during the final walkthrough. Primarily, there is a significant termite infestation throughout the entire neighborhood. Another issue is the water meter was completely broken and is still not fixed. KBHome never even informed me of this issue, I learned this when the water utility company told me they can't setup an account until it's fixed. With that said, I am still happy with the home. You generally get what you pay for. Important too is that KBHome is significantly better than other production home builders in the area.

title: Anthony
review_text: All has been pleasant till now’

title: Quality
review_text: I was expecting a higher quality on materials as well as working. It seems to me Model houses were better

title: Don't use KB Lending ,Lender does not know how to properly calculate.. I moved on and got my own lender.
review_text: Do not use Kb lender , they don't know how to calculate mortgage payments ,certain people don't calculate correctly and even told when there wrong. This was also addressed to Nick the manager and still the issue is occurring.

title: All of my issues were with there loan division KBHS Loans
review_text: As with any escrow there were some minor issues that came up. The big problem was the closing. Everyone at KB knew from day one what my drop dead move out date was for the home I sold and I was told no problem. Then I was told on the last weekday before closing that if I provided the closing statement and full down payment by Monday that I would be able to close late Monday and pick up the keys. I provided what thy asked for before 9am on Monday and was told everything was going smoothly. A couple of hours later I was told I may not be able to get the keys until Tuesday, which I couldlive with. We had taken Monday off in order to be available to sign paperwork and pick up the keys. Once they confirmed that we wouldn't get the keys on Monday we went into work. Shortly after I arrived I received a call saying that things worked out and we could sign and get the keys, so I headed home so we could drive up to Corona (an almost 2 hour drive for us), then the calls started coming in about every half an hour to 45 minutes changing from we can to we can't and back to we can again. This continued until about 2pm when I finally decided we had better start driving just in case we can complete everything. When we were about 15 minutes from the escrow company I received a call from them saying that they had been copied on all of the emails from earlier in the day and that they had no clue why they were telling me it would close as they still hadn't received the paperwork and nobody was communicating with them about it. I was told that the earliest they could do it was Tuesday and that we wouldn't be able to pick up keys until Thursday (Thursday is 2 days after I was to turn possession of my home over the the new owners). We turned around and started heading home while also calling KB who told us that we were definitely closing today and to ignore what escrow was telling me. So once again we turned around and started heading for escrow. Just as we parked in their parking lot I received a call from KB saying that we can sign the paperwork, but won't receive the keys until Thursday. Had we know this we would not have driven to Corona to sign as the original plan was for KB's notary to come to our home to sign everything. It was a little before 4pm when we arrived at escrow who closes at 5pm. I then had phone calls with the KB home loan rep, her direct supervisor and a couple of other people from KB who all refused to turn keys over as promised. I had been told the reason I couldn't get them on Tues was because it was too late and that I couldn't get them on Weds because it was a holiday. I found out later that the real reason was because the sales agent had already gone home for the day and that Weds was her day off and apparently nobody else at KB knows how to turn keys over) Finally the manager of the escrow company offered to call KB corporate office and get someone who could help as I was refusing to sign and was ready to back out of the deal altogether if I wasn't getting keys as promised. Went back and forth with that person for a while who was also refusing to guarantee I could get the keys. About 4:45pm I told him that I was walking out the door unless he sent me an email stating that if I signed the paperwork that I could pick up the keys and he said that I had to sign before he would. I then told him that if I signed that I lost any leverage I had of getting the keys and he said I already had lost my leverage as it was almost 5pm. I then reminded him that this particular home had already fallen out of escrow once before we made an offer and that while I realized I would loose my earnest money that I would still have plenty of money from the sale of my current home to purhase one from one of their competitors. Within in minutes I had the email guaranteeing me the keys that night and we finally closed.

title: First time home Buyer
review_text: This was our first time purchasing a home and to say it was stressful would be an understatement. Nevertheless, the team here at KB had our back especially the builder Jeff.

title: Homeowner
review_text: Buyer beware. House was great, great building superindent ( very important). Land services were shoddy. Landscaping was terrible. Lot not level, large drop off but on my property.

title: Closing ruined the whole process
review_text: The whole process of purchasing this house was a 9 out of 10. Until we started recieving threats to close the house or encure financial penalties, even tho the house was not ready. KB Homes wanted the closing quater to look strong, so deemed it necessary to harrass us to close before the end of February. Prior to this encounter we were activitly talking about but the house next door to add to out portfolio. Due to this experience we will not be buying from KB Homes ever again.

title: The builder was good.
review_text: We need a four wheel drive to get into the house for the driveway is to deep. Jesse was very good.

title: As expected
review_text: Everything during the home buying experience was great in the beginning I didn’t have any issues with the selling agent at all she was very responsive to any questions or concerns I had, I can’t say the same thing for my loan officer and I have tried my best to put myself in her position as far as having the amount of buyers to deal with at once towards the end of the process I wasn’t getting a response like I was initially many times my text would go for a day or two before receiving a response phone calls sometimes would go straight to voicemail, my number one concern around this time wasthe interest rate was starting to go up and there was no discussion on locking in or a plan to talk when we will long story short I ended up locking in on a random phone call at the height of the interest rates, I felt ignored at times. I know my experience will be different than others but having to settle for an interest rates at its height really bothered me and made my experience very poor. Every other department did their job as expected.

title: Terrible Experience With KB Lending
review_text: KB Lending is the worst experience I have ever had dealing with a bank. Questions were asked of me that were truly degrading, and Tracy Bisig, the SR Processor, was the most unprofessional person I have worked with in my life. She NEVER called me throughout the process to introduce herself, and I kept sending the same information because she was not organized. I was never told I could purchase this property from a different lender because I did not meet KD Lending's requirements. I was forced to use my life savings to buy this property because I had no time to seek outside funding. I havebeen in staffing for over 35 years and have never dealt with an uncaring, rude, lazy, and flat-out disrespectful human as Tracy Bisiq. I pray that you speak with this woman regarding how she treats future home buyers and not string them along only to crush their dreams. I would not refer KB Home Lending to Horse Crapping Contest. The realtor, Jeff, was excellent, and building the team was just as great. Future KB HOMEOWNERS, you don't have to use KB HOME LENDING when buying a KB HOME and research your leading offers.

title: Good experience, but sometimes communication was lacked.
review_text: Big thanks to Jeff, he was amazing! Thank you, Grace, for your input and patience. Rick, always on point and knowledgeable, Mark was good.

title: Closing
review_text: My experience was great until it came down to closing. The title person handling my home was a pain. She wouldn't get on the portal that my lender had setup for all paperwork to go to one place. She was uncooperative on that part. Certain things that could have and should have been paid at closing they wanted paid up front. Anything that KB Homes tell you as far what they provide. Be sure to get it in writing

title: Over all positive
review_text: Angelina, the sale person is an angel, very knowledgeable and helpful. Treat my buying like hers, very kind and caring. House is good, wish there is more bathroom compare to amount of bedroom. WILL NOT buy through KBHS loan -very pleasant in the beginning but very dishonest. Make you to sign and says she will fix but continue to drag with same price. NEVER EVER sign something that you didn't agree.

title: Construction
review_text: Overall experience was good. The construction team is excellent and responsive

title: The sales team, Michelle , Beth
review_text: I believe since my community was brand new, I experience unusual circumstances. My move-in was delayed by 2 1/2 months due to now power available to the community when I finally moved in no cable or Internet was available for a month and a half however the sales team was amazing so I can’t penalize them for any mishaps I feel next time, the builder should communicate with the city, and make sure everyone is on the same page so that all the utilities and amenities are available

title: Homeowner
review_text: I was so excited to be a new homeowner at Valencia Fivepoints. My realtor Rolanda did an amazing job helping us find our new home. She went above and beyond to make sure our deal got closed. I just wish some to the KB representatives made us feel the same way as Rolanda did. She made us feel like we belong in this community and our goal of being a homeowner was valued. Rolanda is definitely a gem for KB Home.

title: The four individuals mentioned in my review made this buy a pleasant experience in the end.
review_text: Jeff, Daniel , Paula and Belinda turned a frustrating buy into a very good buy due to there professionalism and caring attitude towardsour needs. They completely saved the day when they took over our loan. I"ve never had anything but good things to say about these four individuals.

title: KB Home Buyer
review_text: Loan process was extremely repetitive, asking for the same documents submitted by more than 10 times. Even though everything was uploaded in KB portal, most individuals didn’t bother to look and asked for my personal/Bank/W2/Loan documents via email. KB portal seemed to have no purpose, even though we were made to believe to upload all our documents in. Design studio was great and so was Steve who worked with us after our home was done.

title: Tommy
review_text: Thomas was great to work with and very responsive to our needs and concerns Others should know that build time did not meet times that were promised

title: Good experience overall
review_text: Overall good experience. I love my home.

title: Thank you
review_text: Please ask questions! Do not hold back

title: Thoughts regarding my home purchase
review_text: The biggest dismay I had was in the time stated between closing date and when it actually occurred, which was about 4 1/2 months later than was stated to me. This of course lead to unforseen expenses both in storage and lodging for the unexpected amount of time which passed. Closing dates were moved 4 times before it actually happened. Hotel expenses are high. Also, better quality control upon completion of home is desired, i.e. cleaning up paint on floors where it bled over, making sure there are no cracks in light switch hardware, tighten bolts as needed to avoid repairwork within a shorttime after closing date. Also, window screens were missing and put in after move-in date by the home buyer, these kind of finishing touches were missing and desired by new homeowners

title: KB needs to evaluate their customer service experience
review_text: We went into contract February 2022 with a promise of a completed home in May or June. It took until the end of September until our home was delivered and only after us calling and going to the managerial staff. We still have repair issues that are unresolved. Also KB puts such a low fence in the backyard that you can see into your neighbors windows. We are disappointed that a privacy fence was not part of our package. This is a $800,000 plus home that should have included that out of respect for the homeowners privacy. We love the house but are very disappointed. The only shininglight was our salesperson who was incredible.

title: Inspect options before closing
review_text: Our house did not have the upgrades we selected at the studio. We realized that only after closing. They haven't fixed it yet and is delayed for ever. Rest everything is good so far.

title: Dennis
review_text: Sales person and building superintendent were great to work with.! The VA lending process was hard! Lending officer’s were helpful in straightening that out!! Thank you to all.

title: Kasey has made sure the things that needed to be fix was schedule to be repaired in a timely fashion.
review_text: I knew purchasing a new home will come with a few bumps and bruises and roadblocks, but all in all, it was good. A few minor things that needed to be worked on but Kasey has been on it to make sure it has or will be repaired.

title: KB Home - Make sure you view construction carefully.
review_text: Make sure you view construction carefully. A lot of issues still with house. Some areas are not even. It’s been a month and still trying to fix issues.

title: KB Home - Build times are longer these days.
review_text: Build times are longer these days. So plan on at least 9-10 months. The construction was done well and to our specifications.

title: KB Staff can be great and contractor quality workmanship can be bad
review_text: Sales rep was prompt on lot and model availabilities. Design Center appointment was exciting and handled professionally, but responses after final appointment were not responded to. KB Home Loan team was great to work with and received prompt feedback. Superintendents resolve issues as fast as they can but at the mercy of the contractors. Many contractor quality and workmanship issues found during the build and after closing (aren't these supposed to be caught and fixed prior to closing!?). Unfortunately, from observations and experience so far, if you rate internal KB surveys with avgor low scores you put yourself at risk for slower to no responses to issues during your warranty period.

title: Sales Team
review_text: Sales team was very attentive in answering all of my questions. Appreciate the knowledge and professionalism.

title: Good price vs quality ratio
review_text: I'm very satisfied with my home.

title: the Agent Cindy and the after market helper Norm
review_text: Be ready to wait with all the C-19 issues

title: Love the floor plan
review_text: Communication with sales rep was difficult. Due to shortage and winter storm pushed back move in dates. Great Experience with everyone else, on the KB team. We love our home

title: Great design, good people, questionable craftsmanship
review_text: Working with KB staff was great. Every person from sales, to studio, to construction was great. In fact, I had no complaints at all until we moved in. There are several cosmetic issues in our home which could/should have been taken care of before we closed but we were on our 7th closing date and just couldn't wait any longer. Annoying things like paint on the carpet to more significant things like large gaps between the siding and top of the windows which I've already had to address personally. The only thing keeping me from a 5 star review is the lack to attention to making every detailperfect.

title: KB Home - The home build itself is wonderful and what we asked for.
review_text: The home build itself is wonderful and what we asked for. The process of buying the home was the hassle. There was major communication issues with every aspect, KB reps, finance personnel, approval office, etc. There needs to be better communication to make the home buying experience with KB Home a wonderful process and experience.

title: Taking responsibility and actions
review_text: Design studio being worst. Sales office and site manager and danny are the best .

title: Missing Features
review_text: When we signed to buy our home, we were told verbally that our slider would be a foot shorter and the downstairs bathroom would no have a window. However, we were not told the gutter on the back of the building would be eliminated. You may say that the salesperson didn’t tell us. Fact is, it should have been in writing all the things the models and already built homes had which would be eliminated. Nothing. We could actually sue for the shorter slider and the window and gutters if we wanted because we were buying what appeared in the model and the already completed homes.

title: KB Home - Sales rep is very transparent.
review_text: Sales rep is very transparent. Building team is very attentive.

title: KB Home - My overall experience with KB Home was great, they are very helpful,
review_text: My overall experience with KB Home was great, they are very helpful, and they were always there to answer any questions or concerns !

title: KB Home - the building team was and is amazing.
review_text: the building team was and is amazing. they are the reason I recommended kb homes in the state of florida.

title: Double check design and lot options!
review_text: Overall, the quality of the build is great. Because the house is so basic, you're inclined to upgrade a bit. Decided to upgrade light fixtures in bathrooms and dining room. Big mistake. The bathroom fixtures were not centered to the sink and are closer to the wall. So much for having a design element. In addition, We were stuck getting a Lot that we didn't want, but agreed to it because we were told we could build a certain design on that lot (Farmhouse), which is the design we wanted out of the four choices. Two days later, we were told that we could no longer have the Farmhouse and that wehad to have a certain style (Italian) because supposedly the plans were already submitted to the City. I feel like the plans would have had to be submitted much earlier if that were the case and that the Sales Agent must have known it was an Italian lot before telling us we could get a Farmhouse. Unfortunately, not being able to choose the style of home really messed with our experience as it's an option that is used to entice buyers to purchase with KB. The home wasn't 100% ready when we moved in, but the Superintendents (I went through 3 in the span of the 6 month build/purchase process) did follow up to make sure our concerns/questions were answered after moving in. Overall, happy with the home, not with the process. There's definitely lot of room for improvement.

title: KB Home - As a first time buyer, KB streamlined the process.
review_text: As a first time buyer, KB streamlined the process.

title: Privacy
review_text: KB built our neighbors home after ours was built. They built the same model as ours with their windows all mirroring ours. The fence is lower then the windows, therefore we can see into each others homes. We have NO privacy!

title: KB Home - My experience with the builder and Sarah was awesome.
review_text: My experience with the builder and Sarah was awesome. They answered every question I had and was able to assist me when needed. My 3 star rating is due to the loan officer, Sylma. As a loan officer she was not knowledgeable and very incompetent. She would give closure dates and not make her deadlines. Instead of her being honest with the changes in the closure dates she would put blame on others. I was told my closure date was changed due to waiting on meters to be installed. Once the meters were installed my closure date was changed again because she was waiting on GSFA to approve me. When Icontact GSFA I was told my application was not received. Immediately after I contacted Sylma and told her what was said and who my point of contact was and only at this time she admitted to me she was not familiar with the program and had to get educated on it. Her apologies were not accepted because I had asked her before I went with KB Loan if she was knowledgeable to the program and was told yes. I forgot to mention I had given my landlord my notice to move, so I was displaced for three weeks and had stressed this to Sylma and her supervisor Steve. Both of them promised to get the paperwork done ASAP, but that didn’t happen. It took GSFA a few hours to approve my application, but it took them over three weeks to process and submit a few pages. Unacceptable!

title: KB Home - Pretty good so far.
review_text: Pretty good so far. Time will tell.

title: KB Home - We love our new home.
review_text: We love our new home.

title: KB Home - Overall experience was great.
review_text: Overall experience was great.

title: The construction manager (Joey) was the highlight of our experience.
review_text: Marcell, our salesman was nice in person but at times not very responsive and seemed to lack knowledge of products and building procedures. He was also rude to our agent a couple times. Once we finished that part of the process working with Joey was a far better experience. He was super responsive and went put of his way to help us and explain anything that needed to be explained. He was flexible with scheduling and didn't mknd going the extra mile.

title: KB Home - Pretty good experience so far.
review_text: Pretty good experience so far.

title: Materials quality could be better,
review_text: You guys can do better. KB is a big corporation. I was expecting great service and quality. I am disappointed with the whole experience. This is not my first new home I purchased.

title: The home is great! The finishing work was less than expected
review_text: The quality of the home overall is great! The finishing work (painting, trim work) and overall cleaning was very messy! We did a lot of work on our own to get the place in tip/top shape. As a new build we expected to move in with most everything ready to go. Our KB sales rep was very responsive and understanding.

title: Only 3 glitches
review_text: Our down payment wire was lost by KB mortgage for 5 days, living room texturing very poor quality and roofing subcontractor performance was way below par extending our closing by a month. Other than that, they were great overall.

title: Interesting home to move into
review_text: The responsiveness has been amazing. They've had challenges given I moved in just as the city was going into the "safer-at-home" measures. Having said that, I'm finding so many nail holes not filled in. I see what looks like flakes on the walls, but when I touch the flakes, they are actually poorly covered nail holes that when touched flake off to show the underlying drywall and nail. I have a shower stall in the master bath, but the tile cuts are uneven making it look like the work was not done by a professional. It was a difficult decision to buy a house in this price range - I think Iexpected the few upgrades that were done to be impeccably done.

title: Jury is still out.
review_text: I have a few issues that have been identified, but it's not fair to comment due to the covid-19 state that we are in. I've been advised that only emergency issues will be attended to. That being said, I can't honestly comment on what they can't do. Until I know what they would do with my outstanding concerns. These are only preliminary responses, but not final.

title: Great Service and process, mediocre materials and workmanship
review_text: In short, good experience with KB, the service, responsiveness. Would recommend the team and ease of doing business anytime. From a value and workmanship perspective, I think they are mediocre; some paper-thin walls (sound), rushed finishes, carpet tags and bolts missing (though again, good service to fix). I think value (price) is ok for the Bay Area. But for the $ I would expect better materials.

title: overall great home - difficult getting response and follow up for issues
review_text: Sales great, home is build well good value for price. However, coordinating follow up to resolve issues needs attention. Coordinator for this project does not communicate of complete followup timely. This can be very frustrating.

title: KB Home - I was given a couple of closing dates.
review_text: I was given a couple of closing dates. May be a lack of communication

title: Responds to Customer Service Requests
review_text: Have had multiple customer service requests to be fixed after closing, however KB seems to be very responsive to those requests to get them resolved. Windows replaced. Still waiting call from company for Microwave to be replaced, stove clips to be installed (Customer service turned it over to appliance company as of 2 days ago), cabinet door replacement and a few door/drawer fixes scheduled Dec 31.

title: Quick build, quick close, your new home will soon be a construction site
review_text: They build the houses in less than 5 months, and close them. Then all the issues show up once the homeowner is living in the property… So get used to living in a construction zone while they repair what should have been done right from the beginning…

title: House Review
review_text: We expected much more from the quality of the KB Home. With the price and presentation, we thought that we were purchasing an excellent house. However once in, we realize that the quality is much lower than our expectations. Exapmples - a) the insulation is terrible - the wall between the office and master bath seems like there is NO insulation - when the shower door is closed - it reverberates loudly in the office - that should NEVER be the case. Ralph comes from a builder family and knows what quality is. He is also an Interior Designer/Architect and knows the business - thus we have beenexceedingly disappointed in our purchase. b)Also the windows appear to have be deficient - they hold moisture and are wet at the base .c) The stair going into the garage does not appear to be the appropriate width - we have nearly fallen down the step several times. c) We also notice that several neighbors had "french drains" installed under their houses. We have been told that we also have free water pooling under our house. Do we need to have a french drain installed? When walking over the floors - things on the counters "rattle" due to the vibration. We have never lived in a house where this happens. Thank you for allowing input

title: Poor communication
review_text: The builders seem to have pressure to mass produce homes which then results in a product that is rushed at the last minute to close. The pre closing walkthrough date will get moved without notification to you so your PTO you requested will feel wasted. Your “10-day” walkthrough after closing will actually be a month and a half later and there will be at least another month that passes before those issues get corrected as well. The house seems to be good expect lots of headaches caused by a lack of communication throughout the process.

title: The quality of home itself and materials are very good. There are however many items that should have been addressed during walk thru
review_text: The home was built too quickly IMHO due to numerous delays to get started. There was too many houses going up at the same time. I believe this resulted in the issues we noted during our walk through. The items we noted during walk thru honestly should have been noted prior and fixed prior to our walk thru. Here we are 5 months later and still have open issues. I haven't been able to get responses from warranty and superintendent.

title: Water leaching to crawl space, air conditioning unit vibrating and frame
review_text: 1.)Upon walkthrough notice drain and crawl space, ask what's that for was told for emergencies. Now upon inspections and crawlspace, moisture and water underwater vapor. So waters coming in under the drainage system at this time causing issues with mold possibly growing. 2.) serious noise issue with air conditioning unit outside of master bedroom. The piping is run through the framing and not properly insulated that it makes a vibration noise and keeps us awake most of the night. It started to cause health issues. Bob's heating has come inspected and says the unit is running properly. Theissue is not the unit running it's where the pipe is run through the framing up to the heater causing loud vibration noises all night and day. We have contacted customer service several times in reference this matter. The response is that's the only place I can put the unit and the way needs to adjust our temperature higher so the air conditioner doesn't run. Why do I have to absorb the cost of running more heat than necessary? Thank you for your attention in this matter

title: James
review_text: Don’t close until the home is completed to your satisfaction. Great superintendent

title: NC
review_text: The entire process was quite stressful as communication between the KB Home corporate office and the title company (First American Title) was terrible. They couldn’t coordinate the closing date and it kept changing. KB kept wanting to close on my new house without having me do a final walkthrough. Quite a poor experience.

title: Trying to settle in our new home
review_text: As this is our second star rating survey that I am receiving. The list of issues that haven't been fixed and new issues continue to pop up. My family and I are trying to enjoy our new home, it turns out there's something new popping up that needs to be fixed and other issues have to wait because we were told there will be a 1 year walk through for items to be looked at or fixed. Giving just two examples. Example One would be: drywall, texture and paint. Second example would be: the AC unit out side is dented and broken in which something had fallen on it when the roof was being installed). Alot of our items were brought up before the NHO and on NHO appointment and still haven't been fixed or were told they can't be corrected. Items were passed on to the next person, ignored or moved path from our concerns.

title: Lack of Good Faith
review_text: Lack of knowledge by sales personnel regarding valid issues.

title: Great Purchasing Experience, Terrible Delivery Experience
review_text: The Sales Team throughout the KB Network were amazing. When we landed on Mystic Vista as the community with our preferred home, we received exceptional service from the Sales Team (Heidi was amazing) and the lending team (Ioana & Roxanna were fantastic), but once we signed and took the keys, we had problem after problem. Starting with a laundry list of poor finish work and culminating with multiple vendor issues and communication difficulties with the post-sale team. Not to mention, KB pushed us to download their app, but 45 days after taking the keys, the app still says we haven't closed onescrow or gotten the keys or moved in. The whole post-sale process has felt very much like we've moved from valued customer to just a homesite number. If you are willing to stay on top of their team and diligently tackle the multitude of repairs or problems that will come along with the purchase, then this could be the right neighborhood builder for you.

title: Too many documents. Problems not identified by Kbs and our inspector not. Are being addressed by Kbs but the timeframe will reveal how they are handled. It would be beneficial if trash and Hotwire prearranged with a fee included in closure.
review_text: Responsive promptly to minor problems. Excellent customer service

title: Not a terrible experience, but not amazing either
review_text: For the most part it was a pleasant experience. The days leading up to closing were incredibly stressful, though. It seemed as though there was no communicaiton between KB homes, KB home loans, the lawyer's office, and my realtor/myself. No one was on the same page, the loan office was unhelpful and condesending, my house wasn't completed/cleaned before closing. And it was just overall an incredibly stressful experience.

title: Bad Work
review_text: Horrible drywall finishing as you can see where seams are everywhere. Robert is a horrible project manager and missed many steps and requests on my home after discussing with him personally several times.

title: Not So satisfied
review_text: Not so good, uncomfortable experience , slow to response, I won’t buy from the builder any more

title: Not totally pleased
review_text: Noticed crack in bedroom window. Informed salesperson, who stated the replacement window was on order. During homeowner orientation, noticed blinds were placed over broken window. The Superintendent of construction was not aware of the issue. So, who ordered the replacement window? Was not made aware of non-delivery of mail. Was informed by different salesperson, 2 weeks after closing of mail delivery issue only when I inquired about time of delivery. This information should have been disclosed at closing. Two months after closing, still have to go into post office to retrieve mail. Suchan inconvenience. Overall, good price for value paid.

title: Honest Review
review_text: I have no problems with the sales representative and the home buying experience but I do have a problems with the materials/equipment of the house. My family and I just moved in 1st week of February 2025 and we already encountered problems with the dishwasher on our 2nd week and now, our garage door.

title: malo el servicio postventa
review_text: Cuando vas a comprar todos son amables, pero luego cuando tienes dudas o consultas ni te responden, y si lo hacen es de mala manera.

title: Before construction, the supervisor said that he is going to give a weekly update but sometimes does not give info unless he is called.
review_text: Take note of all the mistakes and errors during the construction and supervise as much as possible for they are not going to correct this issues unless brought upon to the supervisor and take pictures as evidence.

title: Not a good first homebuyer experience
review_text: Buying a new-build home was more frustrating than expected due to poor workmanship, in-perfection painting, and rushed finishes. While the warranty service has been great and responsive, it’s frustrating that so many repairs were needed in the first place. The unfinished landscaping and ongoing construction noise have also been disappointing. If you’re considering a new build, research the builder thoroughly and be prepared for potential quality issues.

title: Jose
review_text: Hire a third party inspector. AC unit behind master bedroom, is noisy (classic 1675).Toilet water level is very low. Openings in parts of the fence, pets can get out of the yard. Light switches throughout the home are at different levels.

title: House
review_text: The Loan process was a nightmare

title: Not so great Not so bad
review_text: Its not so bad experience with KB. The sales, mortgage teams could be better with communication and providing more options on home loans. The design center experience is good. Most of the options we choose are basic and which can be included with the home by default thereby adding more value to the purchase

title: Buying experience is ok.
review_text: You need to just follow up with them to be able to know the status on where you at.

title: Had 3 Different Building Supervisors .
review_text: 5 Different closing dates.Not a good experience.

title: Not responsive to house inspection issues or warranty issues
review_text: Be careful when buying one of these houses. The KB reps will do everything in their power to let you know that issues discovered during a house inspection is typical and not something they can fix. There warranty phone number and email is also not reliable and they say they will respond in 24 hours but that has not happened on multiple occasions. In fact, it requires multiple time to call and follow-up with them. Buyers beware.

title: Moved into a new build
review_text: The process was pretty simple and the timeline that was provided for our new bill to be finished was accurate. The sales office was always accommodating and welcoming, and honest about different questions we had including upgrades. Once the final walk-through was complete and we moved in, that’s when he realize the quality of work and craftsmanship left much to be desired. Overall, we felt slightly disappointed after moving into our new home. However, these are usually single-family starter homes so somewhat to be expected.

title: KB Homes
review_text: Good job! Keep up the good work!

title: Low quality material and work; bad design of interior, landscape/yard
review_text: 1. Make sure you look at the model / plan before purchase. This detached home come with neighborhood's air-condition sit inside your back yard and the air blow into your living room sidedoor when the AC is on. 2. They do an unnecessary plant/tree on the sideway to backyard where a paveway is needed instead for trash can to pull into backyard. Because of the plant/tree, there is $200 cost to submit to HOA for review just to remove the plant/tree and add out of pocket to add paveway. 3. Don't sign any closing document until they fix all the issues during walkthrough. Also, make sure it isall clean. I just realized how dusty the cabinet, closet, and floor is until I move it. The window is dirty from the paint as well. Overall, it is not a good experience especially for new home where you expect everything is good when you move it.

title: Not 100% Satisfied.
review_text: We have several ongoing issues in addition to the many, some resolved, experienced. In no particular order: 1) We purchased two ceiling fan presides that were suppose to work off of two wall switches. However, only one switch works and the other is dead (times two). KB electrician blamed us for getting a ceiling fan that he said was not compatible with two switches. That is nonsense as ALL ceiling fans come wired liked the ones we bought. So we paid for something that doesn’t function. 2) We were told that the homes on either side of us and the three behind us would not have thesame color scheme. The house directly behind us is identical in color to ours and the one next to that is very similar in color to ours. Not happy with the talk and no enforcement. And it looks stupid with two homes exactly the same color next to each other. Very upset. 3) The sod on the right side of the house as you look at it didn’t take and is dead and looks bad 1 1/2 months in. No effort was made to initially lay the sod on flat ground as the lumps in the sod are everywhere. You should roll over the sod to flatten it and the ground at the time of installation. 4) It has been 1 1/2 months and the coating is coming off the black cabinet handles and you can see silver. That coating is so thin and cheap. 5) The cement has cracked on the sidewalk already. 6) The interior primary bathroom door is difficult to close as it is not aligned properly. 7) The primary bathroom shower drip pan is not centered on the plumbing opening and you can feel the screws from the protective plate with your feet because they are screwed in to nothing. 8) The area to put the modem and router is so incredibly inconvenient. The devices do not fit in the opening and closing the door is difficult. 9) There are chips and holes in many places in the foundation thinset covering. Bugs, moisture, etc., can easily penetrate and get into the foundation. 10) The floor appears to be unlevel in spots as we are finding that we have to adjust chair and furniture legs that we did not have to do in our prior house. Quite frankly, I have another ten issues at least but am tired of typing. And I haven’t mentioned the bad attitude our first builder, Terry, had that really spoiled this whole experience. We are not 100% satisfied and don’t believe that is the KB goal. Sounds nice though huh?

title: Kb experience
review_text: Made me pay my upgrades before house closing at design studio after I was already locked into a contract with an earnest deposit. I think kb could’ve done a better job at making that clear ahead of signing contract. Typically including on my prior kb purchase if your purchase amount is higher than your prequal you come in with the difference at closing not before closing on your home. Also, Has a lot of issues with minor things that were not addressed promptly and still waiting on certain things to be addressed in the home.

title: My experience
review_text: KB needs to improve pre contracting briefs for perspective clients concerning the loan process (impacts because of fannie mae and freddie mac requirements). Sub contractors neee to take their time and build a quality product, clean up job sites and no alcohol should be permitted on job sites. Our home had many flaws that required correction prior to closing.

title: Plumbing issue
review_text: It sounds like you're experiencing issues with low-quality materials and inadequate plumbing in your new home. The small pipes leading to low water pressure can be a significant inconvenience. the sales team is being helpful and help in the buys process which I greatly appreciated.

title: Often good but with some significant hiccups!
review_text: Hard to rank value. There are many things we love about the home but they all came at a price. Construction seems to be solid. Appliances were not installed well. Some big issues going on now with infrastructure in the neighborhood which is unnerving. We will see how it all works out.

title: Barely acceptable cookie cutter housing
review_text: Sales team were courteous, polite and responsive. Design Center was ok. Construction Superintendent was fair, at best. I was a cash buyer. No special interest rate, incentive or promotional allowance for me even though most buyers got something. I was promised an $8000 design center credit for paying cash but it was taken away less than 24 hours later because you guys had an overnight price increase and I switched from financing to cash the very next day after signing my purchase agreement. It was handled wrong by your staff. Your sales agent suggested we write the purchase agreement forfinancing as I was undecided as I could always switch to cash if I decided to. The very next day I decided I’d pay cash and take the $8000 studio credit…. And all the sudden that credit was no longer available. That was wrong and I will not recommend KB to anyone because of it. Some design center choices were very limited and the option prices were excessive. But, it was convenient one-stop shopping. The inability to even have my closet shelves installed a little higher or lower was irritating. Not having input on what $5 plants got stuck in my front yard was disappointing. KB has a successful formula and cranks out thousands of homes with their system. But, homeowners would probably like to see a little more flexibility from the builder. The finished neighborhood is not that nice. Perhaps KB should charge an extra $500 but provide nicer front yard landscaping. Optional pavers, side yard and backyard landscaping packages would provide a more consistent look and a nicer neighborhood thus making for a better finished product.

title: Sonia
review_text: Not to happy with my purchase I been ask to give you a great review and not so happy about my purchase Still issues aren’t resolved

title: Builder Supervisor Makes Promises But No Follow Up
review_text: Having asthma and allergies to pollen I had in my Sales Contract not to plant any trees or shrubs that produce pollen. Construction supervisor has continually promised o supply me with the plants he had landscaper plant in my front yard months ago. I have continually requested this info to no avail. He keeps saying he’ll request it and forward to me. I need the info to verify they don’t produce pollen, and also to care and maintain the plants. He never follows up on my numerous requests and I’m really frustrated with his non-responsiveness.

title: Bribed
review_text: The new home was reviewed & things pointed out that needed fixed. Painting not completed, saw marks in siding, floors not clean & needed acid washed after moving in. Screws missing out of hinges. Furnace filter up side down. Over all not cleaned. Light dim & flickering in bathroom. So while I was gone getting more of our belongings the superintendent RJ bribed my wife with giving him 10’s across the board & he would give us a glass front door. When I did not give all 10’s he would not give her the door. He did nothing for us except to fix the things that was needed & their responsibility. Theonly thing RJ did was painted our porchs. The other guy (Superintendent) that was here during our guy gone had our fans place in & placed garage door openers in do to the mix up with not able to sign while we were there in Florida. Bribery is not the way to do business when you want a bouns from your company because of an Evaluation, that only had fact in it!!

title: Great sales experience but not great at the end
review_text: Everything was great initially, but the financial part could have been better. The people from KBHS (Loan) worked like they didn't know exactly what they were doing, and in the end, I lost money and time because of them. When I asked for help because I had problems because of them, the manager answered, "It is what it is, and I had to see how to solve my problems." when we finished closing, all the promises made by the project manager of a second walk after the 30 days, nothing, I am still waiting, there are some details in the house that I had reported, and I don't receive any answer. Inconclusion, everything was excellent in the sales part, but in the financial, you better save some money and work with other lenders and don't believe in all the promises by the construction part because at the end, when you are done with the closing, they forget about you.

title: Homeowner
review_text: Shoddy construction and materials issues

title: KB Home new construction purchased in 2024.
review_text: I am happy to finally be in our home. KB Homes, you may have over promised and under delivered. The sales reps are great. The home studio was nice but did not give all details of available options. When we received the final product (our house), there were several construction issues that we were expected to identify. It's clear to see that KB Homes uses random contractors to complete their builds. This is an area that I would caution buyers to be aware of when dealing with KB Homes.

title: Business before Customer
review_text: I like my home and most of the process was good. However, when an issue arose, that KB had not disclosed to me in a timely manner, it was made clear that I was not as important as their bottom line and that "Business was Business" per Jerrod in Management. It was 3 weeks before I was due to close. I think Jerrod would have been happy if I just walked away. It left a really bad impression and undermined all of the good things and good people that work for KB. Kudos to Darin, Sarah, and Tom. They were great. Amanda in closing was slow to respond which made doing a cash deal reallydifficult. I've been in my home 2 months now. I am still waiting on some "blue tape" items identified at final walk through to be completed.

title: Great homes but encountered issues during purchasing process
review_text: The overall home buying experience was okay! There is absolutely no problem with the home but, I wish they treated the customers good enough because we faced issues with the sales person, who was very rude and lacked customer service skills. Despite multiple requests, certain tasks were only completed after persistent follow-ups, leaving a less than satisfactory impression on the customer service front.

title: HOA non-responsive
review_text: exterior home lights do not work. keep getting the run-around and have work order placed. cabinet has crack in it. tub has dent. no power or way to activate vent in 2nd bathroom on 3rd floor.

title: Ok
review_text: Ok ok ok ok ok

title: Paying attention to detail
review_text: The purchasing process was easy and without any issues. The problem was seeing the poor quality of workmanship and materials used. There is definitely room for improvement when it comes to responding to issues that you run into once you move in.

title: Owner
review_text: The sales person was very helpful. I was disappointed with the fencing. The ground was not level so the fencing between neighbors is not even. They made the fence gates between neighbors higher then the other. The heights of the fences between the houses is high and low. Like the ground is uneven. There are gaps below the fence. I can’t even let the dog out for fear he will slip under the gate and get out. And the space on one side of the house is average and the other side is very narrow. The smaller side has the adjacent fence higher so we HAVE to put a retaining wall otherwise when it rainthe mud will roll down and wash the dirt from under the fence and it will leave a gap. Some of the walls in the bathroom are flat paint while others are glossy. The carpet was supposed to be an upgrade but it looks cheap. More money was spent on padding for carpet than the carpet itself. The washroom did not come with any cabinets at ALL. The plants they put in the front yard were sporadically placed. And just random plants put in. I was told The home owners association was supposed to keep up the bark and weeds. We are paying almost $200 and yet the bark has no weed blocker laid down prior to placing the bark. And no one has come around to pull weeds. I have had to pull them myself. Disappointed for the amount I paid.

title: Expected better service than I got
review_text: I had major issues with KB since about half way through the home buying process. I was getting minimal updates and my initial time frame was pushed by months. Finally, at the end, my closing kept moving all around which made getting an appropriate private inspection difficult to do. KB stated it needed to be done a week before closing but when my inspector did come, so much still needed to be done and then closing got pushed. I mentioned from many months ahead that it could not be on a certain date as I would be out of the state, but still that's the time frame they had to do it to meetquarter numbers. Then as we get to that date, it needed to be pushed out once again. We get to walkthrough and while there is a vast improvement, so much still needed to get fixed. We'll, we decided to close anyways. Now, I'm finding things done so poorly that some bedroom doors can't even be closed properly, paint jobs are needed and even caulking near showers were incomplete. While I've had a great experience with the construction manager, he can only help so much while still working on getting other houses completed. Now, I'm trying to go through warranty for a water heater beeping that has been doing on for over a month and while I someone has gotten a hold of me, the person who would actually reach out (my community's representative) has not. So now we learned to live with the beep. Overall, these builders had so much potential to have a beautiful home without any issues if it wasn't so rushed and disorganized, but it's hard to be happy when I'm still dealing with so many issues.

title: Average process from initial purchase to final move in.
review_text: The entire process from initial purchase to final move was average. one major item in our kitchen that we were never told about and was not in the model is the exhaust fan above the microwave oven that just doesn't look right and takes away the consistence I the model had above the kitchen cabinet it appears like an after thought. After moving in we had issues with the bathroom vents in one case water entered the bathroom after it rained and in the other bathroom the ceiling started to form bubbles in both cases the galvanized vents were not installed on the roof tops. The builders teamwere responsive to our calls depending on the issue we were calling them about. My recommendation to any potential buyer is that all the work is being performed by subcontractors and not directly KB workers thus builders commitment is not as I would imagine or have seen with KB homes in California. If you are planning on buying a home from KB I recommend frequently visit to your new home as it is being built, take picture, report and document everything to the builder that your dissatisfied with. One more recommendation I have for KB leave extra paint with every new house you sell so that the owner can perform touchups to the wall, front door and so-forth. One more request please remove the outhouse that sits across my home, it is disappointing to see every morning I leave my home to go to work.

title: Not what I expected
review_text: We love our new home, but we do not like our yard. KB needs to put more effort into how they sod and seed the outside. They left rocks everywhere and the sod is awful

title: Good not great
review_text: The experience was good but not great. The sales rep Petra was really responsive and was answering questions great until the sale was finalizing. Toward the closing of the property, only information given was delays after delays with no explanation. Had to get construction supervisor involved to get information that was supposedly passed on to the rep. On closing day, sales rep was no where to be found and we had to ask another rep for a sign and take our own picture. It’s been almost 2 months since closing and not a single follow up other than automated emails. For a huge multi millionbuilder, please do better in the sales department and have people who actually care about people.

title: Rushed to get us in
review_text: They rushed to get us in to meet their quota.

title: Average
review_text: Few minor problems got fix but took time more than we expected. Couple major problems, some were already fixed, one looks like won't be able to get fixed. Construction next door leaves debris, splashes, etc. to our property.

title: Kb home starter home
review_text: Please make sure you are in contact with the staff and have items prepared. Don't be afraid to ask questions of any sort.

title: Craftsmanship was not the highest priority
review_text: Attention to detail isn’t there. Holes hidden behind light fixtures, drywall not texted and covered with a mirror, ect.

title: Delays and workmanship fell short.
review_text: We had to wait an additional 5 months due to PG&E transformer. Then when the transformer showed up, no one was on site to direct PG&E on which home to install the electric meter. So the first 10 home that were completed ended up being one of the last home getting moved in due to meters were installed to homes that recently got built. But “Upper” management didn’t think it was necessary to provide any additional compensation because they didn’t want to show any “favoritism”. But yet, home buyer that only waiting 3-4 months got to moved in first in reverse order. Fully inspect your homeduring your final walk before move in. Plenty of mistak/short cut that needed to be addressed. 1. Corner of the walls have crack due to “settlement”, (address this before the customer sees it then). 2. “Upgraded” kitchen sink had a manufacturing defect, they tried to cover it up with paint….now they have to rip the entire counter top to replace the sink and I how to wait 10 days without being able to use my kitchen sink for a family of 6… 3. Inspect all area that should receive caulk, they missed plenty of area. 4. Garage door belt was tighten so tight that the motor was thinking that something was hitting the door every other time we tried closing the garage door. I had to adjust myself. 5.restroom came equipped with “humidity exhaust fan” that doesn’t turn on when the restroom has 100% humidity. Smh

title: Had a good experience. Builder gets back in a good timely manner when issues happen.
review_text: Love the new home , thank you KB Homes ......

title: They built a house
review_text: The house is adequate. It is a cookie cutter house in a development. All the houses look the same and feel the same. I am satisfied that they did the job expected of them. Employees of KB are competent. Since I've never dealt with another new home builder, I don't know if the are better or worse than other.

title: Not a purchase that I am proud of.
review_text: We were provided with incorrect information prior to purchase. Were told that there was only one home left to build in this area and that is why they were selling this display home at reduced price. Three more homes have been built across the street since we moved in. Home insurance company notified us that they were canceling our insurance due to a roofing issue. The shingle were rolling or curling. KB Homes said this was not under warranty (2 months after purchasing home) but they would repair it out of gratuity. Still have to provide proof to the insurance company. Now we have discoveredthat blown in insulation was not completed in the attic. Wonder how KB will react to this. Almost wish we had not purchased this home.

title: Builder was best part of the process, the rest of it was underwhelming at best.
review_text: Price, options, and the builder Adrian was the best part of the process, we didn't have to chase him for answers, dates, etc. and he even helped reach out on our behalf for the closing date and time since the seller and the loan officer were not responding and assisted changing those times when the original times were not approved or even discussed with us. Hope y'all keep Adrian and give him a raise for sure. The loan officer, seller, and closing were all a pain. Loan officer and seller never reached out and we had to chase them for any answers we needed for months, it got so bad I ratherjust contact Adrian (the builder) and have him help me with any questions I had about anything than chase the other 2. At the closing they told us it would take an hour but due to our papers being incorrect (names and address on multiple papers) we had to stay for 3+ hours.

title: Very Responsive to Issues
review_text: Our home was completed ahead of schedule. Sales personnel were helpful during the process. The construction supervisors, were responsive to concerns during and after construction, and communication with them was very good. They have a challenging job, as the skill-of-the-craft is not what it used to be within the trades, so followup issues kept them quite busy.

title: KB Home Review
review_text: Quality of workmanship is lacking specifically painting of the doors, accent windows, etc. Still see paint drips and paint of glass of glass doors and windows. Make sure to fully understand what specials are being offered. Some of the sales rep are not familiar with the specials and can provide conflicting information. Have these resolve as soon as possible or you will lose out on the special.

title: Lacks New House appeal
review_text: Personally I am happy with the staff and the responsiveness. But overall the Community doesnt have a new house appeal. When you look from outside, there is a big difference between the New houses from other builders (for e.g HeadWaters) and K.B Homes. K.B Homes needs an upgrade with it looks. I bought it here because I had no other option for new Construction. There are limited option in Upgrades and most of them are outdated. Totally dissatisfied with Home Pro. They do not know anything about Customer Service. I would have given 4 Stars even with limited options for Upgrades. But I gave 3stars because of Home Pro.

title: Melissa is great! The actual builder and subcontractors, not very good.
review_text: Our direct contact with the builder was through Melissa. She was great in responding to our concerns and issues. The fact that there were (and continue to be) so many issues with this home is disappointing. The craftsmanship and materials (painting, woodwork, even cement) used for this home leaves a lot to be desired. Less than a month inside the home and basic installation issues continue. We are on our 3rd refrigerator, the cement in the garage is a joke. The sod for landscaping is full of weeds. The rear screen door has already been replaced (apparently damaged when they resurfaced part ofthe patio cement for a 3rd time) and we just found another hole in one of the window screens. The bathtubs had rust stains that had to be removed and the HVAC installation was flawed. Again, let me be clear, Melissa has been great in addressing these issues, but she cannot complete the necessary delivery and service herself. When the 2nd refrigerator was removed, they damaged the front door, which now needs to be repainted. I have purchased 3 new homes in my life and NEVER have I had to have so many call-backs to repair faulty installation. I have never seen such shotty painting (paint drops on the screens and outdoor shrubs). I have only referenced some of the issues we have faced. To make matters worse, I have talked to some of the neighbors who have faced similar issues. Melissa is great, but where is the quality control during the actual building and construction and installation processes?

title: Bang for buck but not high quality
review_text: We bought from KBHomes due to the cheaper price compared to other builders but it was a definitely a "you get what you pay for" experience. The house just looked okay from afar but when you get closer and closer you see the poor work done, the inconsistencies, the broken stuff underneath the surface, etc.

title: not so good and not so bad
review_text: just so so normal yes normal

title: Overall expected better experience as first time buyer
review_text: KB home seems to be more about quantity & production vs quality & customer satisfaction. Had multiple issues with house & community since home purchase. Workload applied to management team seems to be a lot to be able for them to address all homeowners concerns, repairs & issues in timely manner.

title: Communication from loan officer (Victoria) was terrible
review_text: My overall experience buying thisnhome started out amazing. In fact i didnt even think i was going to be buying a KB Home but the salesperson michael was amazing and when we saw the model home it was everything we wanted and the peefect price. The construction team was very communicative and during the build and kept me updated. My experience went south when my loan officer Victoria got involved. There was zero communication on locking in my interest rate so when it came time to close the interest rate had gone up! The only way we could get it back to the original rate is to do a adjustablerate mortgage and because of her bad communication i now had to roll the dice and hope i can refinance at some point in the next 5 years or take on future intrest rates which could cost us thousands of dollars in paying interest. I originally wanted 30 year fixed loan but the amaount of money it would have cost us to get the rate down was far outside what i budgeted. That was just the first experience. The next was no communication around the closing day! Yes the most important day of a homeowners journey she failed to give me accurate dates costing me even more money in extending my lease for a couple days and staying in a hotel for a couple days. The closing day wasnt just misquoted once not twice but three times. Keep in mind i have all my stuff packed up in a uhaul and am now having to pay extended uhaul rental days. I was basically homeless for a week with my fiance and a new born. When it came time for a manager to take care of the situation the response was slow and took forever. They told me multiple times “there is nothing we can do”. I finally got a hold of the regional manager and he was professional and able to offer me $1000 towards my closing. Though that didnt event slightly cover the cost of adjustments i had to make. I didnt really care about the money it was more that my feedback was heard to prevent anyone else from having this same experience. He also was able to help arrange a closing day on a saturday which i am appreciative even though it was still my loan officers fault for skipping over major details in the process that caused this. The icing on the cake was when we finally closed and got to our new home. The loan officer never told the construction manager about us closing on saturday so he could have the house cleaned and ready for us. So we came in to a home that didnt have its finishing touch. The construction manager brayden was able to get a crew out asap and have it cleaned but the breakdown in communication was unfortunate and again we just had to roll with the punches. As a homeowner i would now never want to buy a home again because of how tramatizing this experience was. As a loan officer you basically have the customers finacial future in the palm of your hands. The least you could do is slow down when it comes to MAJOR details and make sure the customer is caught up. My experience with KB was great from everyone else but i felt the need to write this review to warn others about my experience with my loan officer.

title: Just "okay"
review_text: The sales staff that I had was quite aggressive and pushy during the search and closing periods. There was a tile that was broken and was replaced with a different color. They were about to give me the house with a solo different colored tile in the master bathroom. The closing date was then pushed back as they had to redo the full master bathroom. There are still issues with the house that have not been fixed. Quality of work could be better.

title: Not a great experience with KB Homes
review_text: My experience with the sales guys was great, but not so with KB management who seems invisible. They only use the small guys on the ground for issues that require leadership to address. Please ensure you ask all questions before construction begins, I assumed sprinkler system was part of the home, only to discover last minute, it's an additional expense, it was not disclosed in the beginning.

title: I'd rather not say
review_text: I'd rather not say .

title: Overall experience is okay
review_text: 1. It is expensive 2. Quality is not that great 3. Homes are low to medium quality may be good for first home buyers 4. Not many options

title: Be involved throughout the process.
review_text: Signing on with KB Homes came with a promise of “weekly” updates; as well as 5 site visits. We were disappointed with the fact we didn’t get weekly updates. The reason was usually that there was a monthly Monday meeting. If that is the schedule then call on Tuesday, don’t skip a week. The site visits were as expected for the first 3 but then they were compacted together. Lastly, the amount of trash left on site (both on top of and under the bark) is unacceptable. I have found the expected nails & staples; however chunks of twisted metal, sheets of plastic and particularly a 8ft rebar (underthe bark) is a bit much and I would expect respect for the property, not a “hurry and move on”. Because that is how it represents itself. After the move in stay in contact with the customer service rep to take care of issues. We have encountered 15+ blue tape items and our rep has been very responsive.

title: Current Homeowner
review_text: Too many delays with construction and closing. Financer did not use all options available to purchaser.

title: ok
review_text: I have been called emailed very frequently and asked to provide the survey -we have not moved in so don't know yet since could not handle the frequent calls so completing

title: Unhappy Owner
review_text: When we started out with KB it felt like there was a rush to get things done from the start to the finish. The day before the closing was a rush as documents that should have been submitted to us for final review weeks before was still not completed for review and they were constantly running the numbers even though nothing had changed. Mortgage Department one person she knows herself left me and my husband off the deed. Now I have been left with the headache of getting that sorted out. When we did the Design Studio appointment had one person doubled up on our upgrades twice . Onceduring the video appointment and the other time on the final document. I pointed it out to her and she told me their was no error. She changed it on the system overnight and submitted it the next day without acknowledging her error. I print all documents before so I can review before i signing. She needs more training. KB needs to put up more and clearer signs so that people do not get lost trying to find this community. I am Constantly on the phone directing Uber, delivery company and fast food companies as the GPS is sending them to the neighboring community . Mail Boxes are located 15 minutes away walking. That does not make any sense. Each community has a cul-de-sac why not put them at the end of each community. There is no recycle or garbage pick up in this community as it still under construction. I have a very long list of things to fix. I have biting ants in my kitchen no food left out they were also on the carpet and outside on the patio. Shower pipe in master bathroom the water pressure is so low i can hardly take a shower. Too many things to speak on. Now we have moved into the area. Angie and Richard has always been very nice throughout this process. I have been here a week and no one has come to welcome us to the area. I have met a few of the neighbors really very nice who mentioned they have received gift baskets and gift cards. I am still waiting on mine not feeling very appreciated after spending all that money with KB. KB All your buyer's should be treated the same. We should all feel welcome. I hope this review will help KB improve customers overall buying experience.

title: Not very happy after I find a lot of bad details around the house!!!
review_text: This a great house overall but comes with a lot of issues low quality materials and some really bad details from construction contractors !! Once I start looking around everything I find a lot of ugly things I have being fixing myself since I don’t trust them at all !!

title: Good start, not so great finish
review_text: My initial experience with KB was quite good. The sales, design studio, mortgage lending and construction manager would be rated 5 stars to work with but after closing, my experience has been much different. Pretty much, once you close on the house and try to get responsiveness from the warranty department to correct sloppy work completed along the way during the construction of your 'new' home, you can expect at least a month of back and forth to try to get anything accomplished. Severely disappointed based on my original high expectations from KB. Their motto of "Built on Relationships"is a complete farce in my experience.

title: Closing Process
review_text: Closing process was such a headache, documentation process is so messy that it delayed us from closing for about 3 months. Loan processor was changed mid process and we had to gather all documentation all over again. Overall not a very simple process for closing. Review in regards to the house: Everything is working well, happy to finally have settled into my new home.

title: Material information left out/misleading from KB home
review_text: Material information was left out. After I took the keys, a few weeks later I had a qualified plumber and a selected electrician look at the work. The electrician questioned why my circuit breakers tripped off every other day, as soon as I plug my electric car into the 110 the breaker trips. The plumber didn’t understand why the drainage pipe for the air conditioner is above the 3rd FL bathroom on the room, since no other house nearby has this pipe sticking out. Also because the AC unit is on the side year on 1st floor ground. When I asked the asst super if KB hime he told me “everyhome has this” which is incorrect. Because sure to check the specs

title: Not in the standard
review_text: I was disappointed since I bought my first house here in USA 10 years ago with KB Home so I really trusted this time.
