title: Nice looking homes, but some quality control issues
review_text: The KB homes at Ventana on Lincoln CA, are nice. We where one of tge first families to move in, and everyone we've met have been nice. The office staff, and the kb homes construction supervisors have been great as well, even through this covid 19 challenged time. Now the excitement of taking possession of the home, and finalizing mounds of paper work, and contracts, time for the details. If you buy a home, take your time, inspect every inch, dont be rushed, bring a trained eye. We had a damaged microwave, unfinished paint, drawers that hit the custom counters and dont close right, damagedvanity counter tops, over spray, really bad seams in the carpet, and you can feel lumps here and there, automatic bath fans that would not shut off, they had to come and make them manually operated with the switch to turn off, the sensitivity adjustments i guess didnt work, or we had that much humidity behind the walls. Many of our upgrades hadn't been done, we had to bring it to there attention several times, with calls and emails. We paid for a sidewalk, that even being overlooked, had some problems getting installed, for instance the stucco bubbled out on the side where the sidewalk went, causing further delays because of repairs and paint. We had a issue with the landscaping, they only do the front, but one tree was hitting our gutter and to close to the sidewalk, they had it moved. Some other big ones we had problems with was the loft was supposed to have open railing, that we paid for, not the pony wall they initially started, our vanity light fixtures, and shower doors where supposed to be color changed, we caught it, they did swap it out for the correct fixtures. The dryer outlet didnt work, a breaker needed replaced. There's a few other on going issues, but with covid19, they're not able to address them all. John, Jim, and Devin are all great to work with, and try to fix everything they can. Point is before you get too caught up in the moment, before you take possession of those keys, inspect every single aspect of that home, ever inch, drawer, outlet, breaker, every window, one of ours was cracked. Carpet seems doors, yes we had a hole in a new door as well. Every knob, blind, door handle, outlet, carpet seem, light fixture, everything. Look real close with a flashlight, glasses whatever it takes, you're the one paying over half a million dollars for a new house, not almost, or repaired house, yes we've found those too, examples, shaker cabinet faces had been touched up with spray paint, thats still there. A counter top they sanded and buffed after damaging it, we caught it, and it was replaced. Also there's a huge pest problem with springtails, it got worse when they brought in the mulch, millions of them all over the area, our property, garage, and now our new home. Once you take possession its all on you, so before you take keys, id look for that, hundreds of dollars later its still a big problem. Overall the community is great we moved from 2 miles down the road, and have lived here for a long time, lincoln is a great community. Kb staff have been great, and although there has been quality control issues, the have and will continue to address them. I can't express enough how important the walk thru and inspections are, see something say something. Its your money, and dreams, not the carpet installer, not the roofers, not the painters etc. Its yours, get your moneys worth, and quality you expect, if you do that, you'll love your kb home, and enjoy working with the staff.

title: Many things falling apart
review_text: We are excited to own our 1st home , the price/value is great but then when we landed in home it started falling apart , there are number of issues like Gas leakage , Tank-less water heater not working , ceiling lights popping out , gaps on edges of stairs etc . The person who did our home orientation did not take things right way to address them and always we need to claim to south bay homes and depend on their availability. I did not expect these kind of issues happen with new home which is not pleasant. our Thermostat was not working , when i tried to replace with new one it went down andthey claim its not under warranty .. the new home less than 30 days how does it dont function well. overall happy only with purchase and community but not the amount of issues happening .

title: Sales staff are great! Getting things fixed? Not so much.
review_text: Have nothing but good things to say about KB Home sales staff! Helpful from beginning to end. That being said, it's been nothing but frustration getting things fixed and fixed correctly. Poor workmanship and no response are the main issues.

title: Not the best customer service!
review_text: Overall I think KB Homes are good homes to buy. This is my second time buying a KB Home. The first home I purchased was in CA 30 years ago. Great experience! This is my second KB Home. It is in AZ. Not a great experience! I was mistakenly expecting the same customer service as my CA experience. I will only mention the major problem because of time. The home I purchased was completed in June 2019. I looked at the house in December 2019 and decided to purchase it. We moved in at the beginning of February 2020. There were two electrical outlets in the second floor that did not work and four onthe first floor. It is now April and I am still waiting for the work to be completed. My main concern regarding the customer service is why they did not check the house to ensure ALL of the electrical outlets were working properly BEFORE we moved into the house! There was plenty of time to do it. My son slept through one of the appointments since he works during the night. It was delayed for three weeks to be completed because of that! The day of the appointment to return, the customer service representative did not call to let me know the appointment was cancelled because of the COVID-19 pandemic! So, my son was up all night and day because of his inconsideration! We should NEVER have been inconvenienced by the repairs since this should have been checked BEFORE we moved in! Now, here we wait to be rescheduled. How’s that for customer service?

title: KB Home - Be more involved in building process.
review_text: Be more involved in building process...

title: KB Home - Overall Good Experience.
review_text: Overall Good Experience. Kb has good customer service team.

title: Fencing
review_text: Been here for almost 2 months and still don’t have my fence up because HOA won’t get back to me. I’m honestly super disappointed with KBHS for not answering quickly. I have my back fence and side fence with the hoa and both are on my property. Been trying to get this figured out so I can get my fence up

title: Mortgage Company
review_text: I went with the one stop shopping with KB and thought that was a great idea and it was up until the end. The mortgage company stinks! They don’t communicate in anyway. I was very nervous with making my first mtg pyt, setting up their online payment and through the whole process they never communicated once. A good thing I kept checking the site then I had to email them to see something wasn’t set up right. I went through every other company (utility) and they were awesome, but not this mtg company. What a sad way to end what was a good experience.

title: Good, but could be better
review_text: My only complaints have to do with the quality of the build. I've had trouble and continue to have trouble with the white tile flooring that I had installed. There were red stains all over the house (and there still is some), but I just gave up after the 5th time they sent someone out to clean it. Also, our dishwasher and washer have already flooded the house causing damage to the floorboards. There's patches in the paint on the exterior and paint in some of the outlets inside. Other than that, it's just been minor fixes, but easily prevented issues.

title: Beautiful home
review_text: Quality of appliances is great! Few things I’d improve for the price paid, paint quality on walls, insulation in walls so that the house is not paper thin.

title: The whole unfiltered truth
review_text: Sales staff was amazing with helping find a lot and floor plan that fits my needs. After that we took a wrong turn somewhere. Once the slab was poured, I identified an issue missing with a missing the rewire for the floor outlet (contract stated before slab). Brought it to the builders attention, his response was it should be there if it's on the contract. Fast forward a week or 2, they had to cut the slab for wiring, bit it was in. During the build had typical issues that were address prior to closing, normal finishing issues. Moving on to closing... So we had a closing date scheduled andthere was an issue with the carpet padding that prevented my final walk through, I was not notified until the day before the walk through that it would have to be rescheduled. Keep in mind these walk throughs are during my normally scheduled work day, which the builder knew I had to take the day off to attend. Move forward to the second scheduled walk through, they tried to cancel the night before yet again because the builder had a scheduling conflict, not sure why because he chose the date for the second walk through. My agent talked them into letting us do the walk through with the selling agent and have our inspector do his inspection. Inspection was good only a few minor issue they had already identified to be fixed, horribly laid sod and cosmetic issues which is not surprising since it was a new build. Addressed concerns with the builder and he was happy to "take care" of all the issues. Due to these issues they tried to push my closing date over 30 days due to a total of 4, yes 4 scratches. Glass was scratched in the shower and 3 scratches on the cabinets. We worked through it and closed in a reasonable time, but the lawn doesn't appear to have been fixed, there are large gaps in the sod which I am afraid could injure my kids ankles or pets. Figured I will end up redoing the entire yard so not a big deal. Now that we are moved in I receive a notice that I have a very minor HOA fee that is past due and will begin to be a big problem if it is not paid in 30 day. The information at closing was incorrect and that the closing agent, title company, or finance company someone from the builder provided incorrect information and all fees were not paid at closing (not a big issue, and very small amount of money but it should not be an issue at all since the people who created the closing contract either work for or are contracted by the builder's company). That is what happened to me and may or may not be an isolated iincident,I cannot speak for other residents. It is a nice community, and very quit minus the construction during the day, which is to be expected since they are still building.

title: Issues after moving in
review_text: I have been waiting for the issues in the house to be fixed

title: Good home but slow responses
review_text: Builder response to issues really slowed down immediately after closing, but would buy again.

title: Smart Home
review_text: I think that I need a min-course in how to use the Smart features in my home. Also the backyard seems sloppy because there are holes in the sand and it is uneven.

title: It’s an okay home
review_text: If you go with KB home, be really careful with your studio selections. Purchase as much of that as you can outside of the studio. And check on your home weekly as it’s being built.

title: Craftsmanship little sloppy
review_text: Quality at delivery time is slopy, could be more neatly delivery. Bricks could show better craftman ship.

title: KB Home - Some items in the home are poorly and cheaply made
review_text: Some items in the home are poorly and cheaply made

title: Experience Was Okay
review_text: They set the expectations high based on all the things they say/share and provide for documentation of the process yet it didn’t seem like they actually followed through with it. I guess it could’ve been worse but definitely not as great craftsmanship executed in the building of the home as they sale nor are they consistent. Make sure you use a realtor to keep you in the best interest and not just be another “Lot”. Overall the experience was okay but at the end of it all we had to “clean” a brand new home to move into.

title: Great customer service but little value
review_text: House construction was delayed by 4 months, which is understandable to some extend but it wasn't easy dealing with temporary place to stay and having your mortgage rate lock expire since KB kept sending inaccurate closing dates. The home quality itself is ok. For being a new home I was expecting better quality in terms of finish. To mention a few things: nails sticking out the middle of the carpet, some gaps between floor and wall base, uneven layout of floor tiles that make a room look like its longer on one end than the other. Customer service however is very good, they are responsive eventhough the work and quality are not great. After speaking with some friends, it seems like they also have not had the best experience with KB when dealing with new built homes. I would not buy another new KB home, not worth the price one pays for.

title: KB Home - For the most part we have been pleased with our new home.
review_text: For the most part we have been pleased with our new home. The part that we are unhappy with is that throughout the process we have been told many things and the timing of them getting done or getting them done the right way has been a pain. Especially with our front yard area. The plans always seem to change with KB and they don’t always seem to know what’s going on and their answer to solving a problem is that they will find out and let you know but then they never let you know.

title: KB Home Advise
review_text: Make sure you have a realtor when you buy your KB Home. They respect realtors and ignore homeowners. You'll need someone to ensure that KB does the job and someone who can get you discounts on mistakes they make. Also, spend the money on an inspector no matter what the sales people say.

title: Great location
review_text: Bike rider paradise. Responsive to requests for missing parts. Floor plan great.

title: KB Home - Value is not worth for the price paid
review_text: Value is not worth for the price paid

title: Average home for an above average price.
review_text: KB spends a great deal of time, money and effort “selling” you a great home, but only puts forth minimal effort finishing and delivering it. Mine is still not finished 60 days after closing.

title: Pretty decent experience
review_text: The house was beautiful when finished. There are items in the house that were unfinished and lots of design center issues that soured this experience. Advice: new hires need much better training. Me working in the real estate industry I really was able to see the lack of professionalism and experience and it didn’t make the process great. Wish I had worked with more veteran employees. The Design Center was by far the worse experience of it all. A lot of items were not explained and I missed out on configuring my house the way I would’ve liked. Overall, I’m okay but would rate the entireexperience a 4/10

title: customer service counselor sales agent needs training
review_text: customer service manager Rob is an excellent manager giving him 10 stars really concern with buyers and constructor supervisor Steven is the same thing 10 stars rate but the materials are cheap a lot of things to fix for a brand new house

title: Great House, Poor Employees
review_text: House is good. Great quality at a great price. However, the communications with the co structure crew and sales person was very poor. Would of been a great experience if they just listened to us versus fighting us. I would recommend KB to others with a different crew. Studio and mortgage process was amazing (even though kb asks for way to many documents).

title: Snow Removal Sucks
review_text: My home is nice. So far warranty issues are taken care of in a satisfactory manor. But: I live 3 doors down and across the street from the Model Homes. I have Lived in this soon to be nice community and THE Walk IS NOT SHOVELED. Here is the Beauty of My side of the Street not Getting Shoveled. The Snow removal starts at the Model Homes and they will NOT SHOVEL THE HOUSES ACROSE THE STREET FROM THE MODEL HOMES?

title: Great customer service Bad workmanship
review_text: We had a lot of issues with the house when we moved in, our Central air and heat did not work, we had electrical problems, paint clean up, exterior of the house was missing paint, just to name a few. But Gary our warranty guy made sure that we were taken care of right away and had all of these issues fixed. But we did expect the workmanship to be way better, to many unfinished details for a new built house.

title: KB Home - Customer service after my move in was bad and i would suggest
review_text: Customer service after my move in was bad and i would suggest the team at least to respond if customer is calling. Dry wall after fixing my bathroom pan is left for more than 20 days during the period of Thanksgiving. I have called customer service but no response yet.

title: Good price, regular workmanship, ok materials
review_text: You get an ok house for a good price, but be very careful with the builder contractors they will leave a mess and to make them to clean after is very difficult, other than that you get a good place to live for an affordable cost.

title: Recent purchase of former model townhome
review_text: Sales team was great. Finance team was ok. Service team was poor.

title: You get what you pay for
review_text: The final product is decent but to get there you have to spend a lot of time walking the house and working with the superintendent to get things corrected. It takes multiple times requesting corrections before they happen. We walked the houses around us that were not being checked up on and the quality was pretty poor. As long as you can stay on top of the build, the final product is a good value for the money.

title: Excellent customer service, poor quality product.
review_text: We had an outstanding experience with all KB staff, however the quality of craftsmanship and care of construction was very poor in our first home build experience. We had trash left in our walls and behind HVAC units, carpet laid over debris, flooring laid incorrectly, missing electrical components, missing plumbing components, and more which has led to an unpleasant month of constant repairs and fixes after moving in. The KB team has been scrambling to make sure everything is fixed to our satisfaction but please make sure you are keeping an eye out on details that construction crews (a lotinexperienced) are doing incorrectly....also don’t rely on the “inspections” made by the construction manager... we have had a lot of issues that should have been taken care of during points of inspection and weren’t. Overall, we are satisfied with the home and the KB team for striving for 100% satisfaction.

title: Rushed and a little Disappointed
review_text: The builders were rushed and did a poor job on details. They wouldn't allow me an additional day. I had to close on August 20th. If they had held off for another month, the issue I have wouldn't exist. The paint job is poor, the cracks in almost all corners in the Master bathroom weren't sealed. Cracked tile in the master bath and instead of replacing it the put grout on it. Wood around the island is not flush, it puckers in the corners. All these thing couldn't be seen unless looking very closely. I've had three different contractors from the time the building of my house to now. The lasttwo have had to deal with all the issue that I've discovered. They're really good at helping with the repairs. I'm disappointed with the way my house was built. If I ever purchase another home I will be at the house everyday watching closely, unfortunately I wasn't in the same state. That was a downfall for me. The should be taking money off the amount I owe for the house, all the trouble i'm going through.

title: Many false promises delivered in a very sophisticated way
review_text: 1. Credit from standard options that can be applied towards to upgrades - This is really not true. They do make it look its true on little things like the faucets that you can check outside but anything that goes in thousands and the exact model is not found in the stores they added at least a 50% premium mixing up with poor quality items. 2. Design studio's misleading sampling technique - sample items they showed in the design studio looks intentionally misleading. Flooring sample they have shown did not represent what they have actually installed. A small percentage of the difference can beacceptable but almost 50% of the planks installed were not the sample shown. 3. The house was delayed by more than a month - it can be understood if the the house is delayed by a month from the beginning of the construction. Sales reps told us the house will be ready in 2 weeks but it actually took 5 weeks.

title: ok experience
review_text: KB offers affordable model homes, with decent workmanship. I did not give it 5 stars, because there was no transparency upfront about the additional costs involved in building the home like the lot premiums, or what was involved in the basic model. Their mortgage company was also difficult to contact and get a hold of.

title: Liked my previous KB Home prior
review_text: The quality of our new KB Home this go around was mediocre. Our final walk through was a mess and I have called KB several times to fix minor issues inside the home such as holes in the concrete foundation, bad wall patching jobs, and the door trim was not up to par either. Our first KB home we bought 7 years ago was superb but unfortunately this time around it felt like the construction was rushed and messy.

title: Water pipe issues
review_text: Busted pipe has caused continued water flow in backyard and front.

title: KB home was a great value.
review_text: As the saying goes, you get what you paid for. There are a lot of issues with the home and their employees seem to be let go pretty often. This has been frustrating as I was promised something only to find out someone was let go and I have to start the process all over again. Their worksmanship is horrible.

title: Decent home
review_text: I will commend KB Home for the speed at which the home was built. Sales person was very helpful and reachable. Few things to note. Insulation is very thin and the studio didn’t let us know how the insulation will be. I can literally whisper between walls. A lot of things in the house are upgrades and don’t come as standard. You have to spend a lot of money to build the house to your taste. It’s advisable to get an extra layer of carpet as you will feel the nails when climbing the stairs. In all, it’s a decent home.

title: KB Home -
review_text: craftsmanship could of definitely be better, unfortunately we did not notice the issues until we were all moved in, AC broke down within the first weeks of closing and it took 1 week to repair!, siding that had to be replaced outside still not painted and it has been over 2 weeks already!!. Definitely makes us reconsider our choice of builder...

title: Would probably choose another builder.
review_text: Happy to be in my first home. Overall very happy with my purchasing and design studio experience, but definitely see many issues with the workmanship of the home—painting, tile work, etc. very sloppily thrown together. Sales and design team are great!

title: Re-work
review_text: They had so many re-work to be done.. many mistake. some work are not done professionally... expensive with less choices

title: Beautiful house but lack of detail
review_text: Our house is beautiful and we love it, however, there were a lot of issues with the small details of our home - very poor quality of work. The toilet paper holders would not hold a roll of toilet paper, the slider door had gouges and scrape marks, the garage door was extremely sounded like it was going to fall off the hinges. The list goes on and on. This is a brand new home and to have so many issues upon move in was quite disappointing. It sure was upsetting to have so many issues in a brand new home that we paid a lot of money for.

title: Poor communication
review_text: Unresponsive to call or text

title: Not satisfied
review_text: Attention to detail with a new home was not there

title: New home buyer
review_text: On your final walk through make sure everything works. So, there is no hassle for any maintenance issue.

title: Poor Workmanship
review_text: When we moved in we had multiple electrical problems that were not found until wenhad an electrician install fans and GFI's in the garage for us. We also found problems with the plastering job when we were painting the interior of the house. We found paint/plaster on the underside of multiple doorknobs. The construction manager was quick to respond to the issues but still.

title: KB Home - Generally speaking, all the personnel are very kind and helpful.
review_text: Generally speaking, all the personnel are very kind and helpful. The only thing made me somewhat upset is your loan company was not very efficient, Jose is nice but the whole process is not a pleasant experience.

title: Beautiful home
review_text: The home is absolutely beautiful. The entire staff from the construction team to the sales team are professional and friendly. There are some issues with its corporate management. They are non-responsive. Happy with the home but not happy with how certain concerns are handled or should I say ignored.

title: met their goals but quick and not too many bonus as all upgrades
review_text: We bought another new home and their customer service wasnt nearly as good as KB but their home was built better. wasnt all upgrades. Disappointed in landscape although i know some of it is controlled by the area and water shortage but more trees and color would be nice especially with a corner lot and premium lot

title: Wish it was a better experience
review_text: Matt, Peter and Roger are great at what they do and very pleasant to work with. They are the team that build your house from ground up. Unfortunately for them, the sales team and K.B.H.S, fell significantly short in providing satisfactory customer service. My wife and I felt that K.B.H.S and the sales team were unprofessional and careless. They had their own agenda and did not work with our needs. K.B.H.S told us that we were asking too many questions and it was starting to upset their upper management team. Keep in mind, the questions we were asking were as follows: 1.What is thedifference in rate between 15 year fix and 30 year fix? 2. Will they match interest rate with a competitor? 3. Please provide a breakdown of interest between the 2 options? 4. Would it be better if we do a jumbo loan or a conventional? Apparently, these four questions were enough to upset the upper management of K.B.H.S. One week prior to our closing date, K.B.H.S told us we do not qualify for the loan and there was nothing they could do about it. At this time, my wife and I were forced to look into other loan companies and got approved with Cherry Creek Mortgage. As soon as we got approved my Cherry Creek Mortgage company, K.B.H.S said, "We have great news for you, we can match what Cherry Creek Mortgage is offering you." However, K.B.H.S was forcing us to close with them because, they told us that if we can not close with them on May 24th, each additional day will cost my wife and I $600 in penalties. Anyways, I can go on and on. In short, do not use K.B.H.S and make sure you get your own real estate agent involved. Do not give them the entire control of the sale, cause they are not worthy of such trust. The rest of the team was spectacular though. Truly one of a kind. Thanks again to Peter, Matt and Roger. Keep up the great work.

title: Not what i expected
review_text: Going into buying a home with KB, I was very excited. I was not ready for the inconsistencies with the project manager as well as him not meeting our expectations. There are still issues that need to be resolved and we have been in the house for almost 2 months.

title: Feedback
review_text: Consider hiring a good home inspector. Pay attention to construction process and contractor workmanship.

title: New Buyer
review_text: Not bad.

title: Average
review_text: Builder bless non/negotiable in price for faults they did during construction and didn’t want to take any accountability.

title: Middle of the Road
review_text: Keep double triple check on the items that you requested to have repaired or fixed. Follow up is not good

title: Unbiased opinion.
review_text: Make sure to get a reputable home inspector before buying. Be willing to walk a way if the price you’re willing to pay is denied. Also it’s a good idea to buy a book that tells you what to look for and what to be aware of before buying a home.

title: Better work
review_text: Better work on the backyard and fence.

title: Not too bad
review_text: Not bad builders just have to spend a lot of time looking after the craftsmanship.

title: Terrible wear and tear; unresponsive warranty service
review_text: My home was delivered to me with substantial wear and tear, and KB insists that things are normal and will not fix all problems before closing. Some issues are just impossible to fix after you've moved in. They also seemed to have used low quality materials--I have windows that do not block sound at all, and a super loud ventilation fan system. To make things worse, my KB Home customer service rep is unresponsive, and does not answer email for weeks. This has been a frustrating experience for me.

title: New home
review_text: Very bad on communication. They almost never get back with you on anything. We closed two months ago an still cant get paint and things fixed that they promised would be done if we closed. They want you in so that they don't have to do any more work.

title: Love our home, but tough to get here.
review_text: Design center experience was very good, but the building process was the opposite. We had 4 different project managers due to them moving to different communities, and at one time our house didn't have anyone work on it for 6 weeks. At a few different times, the PM actually lied to us (which was confirmed). It's been 5 weeks since we closed, and there are a ton of punch list/warranty items that are now being worked on, but it's progress. The house is becoming what we hoped for, but getting here was tough.

title: new KB home review
review_text: this is my first purchase from KB Home. the quality is average and the price is above average. Understood that the overall house-buying market in the bay area is crazy so I don't blame this on KB home. Cannot review on trustworthiness and responsiveness since I've just moved in for couple of days. I'm giving 3/5 just to be able to submit the result.

title: Pleased but with issues
review_text: Craftsmanship, damaged cabinets, caulking, damaged drywall, damaged wall trim, unfinished garage was agreed on but the drywall was damaged. Planks on the brand new fence were popping off the rails. I spent time and money repairing items in the a brand new house I paid over 330 thousand for. I didnt expect this from a brand new home, maybe I expected more. We are going on 2 months in the house and I cant tell you how many work orders were put in for repairs on top of the work I put in. I also had the sprinkler system break and now I have a 2000 dollar water bill KB is trying to deal with.I just dont know what part of that bill I'm going to be stuck with. It's just stressful to deal, especially with such a busy personal life. There are other things, such as no mailbox setup,etc that just makes me shake my head.

title: KB Home - On original walk through we tagged spot to be repair and six
review_text: On original walk through we tagged spot to be repair and six weeks later not resolved.

title: KB Home - The staff of KB Homes are very courteous and humble
review_text: The staff of KB Homes are very courteous and humble

title: poor
review_text: I am not happy with any of the process... noises from plumbing above my office, water settling in the yard, tiny little plants in the front yards, as well as its been 2 months and our sidewalk still has huge cracks with no indication when it will be fixed..the drywall, when the light hits it has wave shadows.....as well as my garage door was so noisy If I hadn't caught some of this it would have just been left with very little quality assurance.

title: Watch them like a hawk
review_text: Our home was not clean when we moved in, and there were multiple things that needed repair. There was construction debris thrown on top of our kitchen cabinets, trash in the yard, saw dust coating the backs of all our doors and trim, drywall dust stuck to all the window frames, paint on the floors and the carpet, gobs of caulking dripping out of the window panes and the list goes on and on. We had our house mold tested before drywall was installed, and sure enough, there was mold in half a dozen places on the framing. KB was great about having a reputable company remediate all the mold.KB’s warranty department is great about having stuff fixed in a timely manner. But be warned, it still takes up a lot of your time and is very frustrating to pay this much money for a home and have such shoddy workmanship in so many different areas. KB will do good work but you will have to work for it by keeping a close eye on them and insisting that they do things right and fixing the things that are wrong.

title: Subs
review_text: Very disappointed in the sub contractors. Especially the tile/flooring company. Also the painters. We moved in 1-1/2 months ago and we are still waiting on issues to be fixed

title: Little disappointed on my new house
review_text: I guess im one of the unlucky one that the house built close to oak tree. Backyard now fill with leaves and eventually my roof will be, too.

title: So far so good. Not super responsive, but that's how things work in the bay area I guess
review_text: They were not super responsive after the contract was signed. Also, once the home closed, the responsiveness dropped to 0. There was no guidance on whom to contact in case of unforseen situations. However, it's been just a few months since the home was ready. Let's see how things go as the home ages.

title: KB Home - The cement in my driveway and sidewalk in front of my house did
review_text: The cement in my driveway and sidewalk in front of my house did not cure property. It is gray and speckled when every house on the block and around the corner is white. It’s not right.

title: Great location
review_text: The upstairs floor squeak due to improper installation. the paint is cheap. the windows don't slide up or down and comes off the bracket.

title: KB Villas at The Meadows
review_text: We had been in the new house 3 weeks and had two separate leaks in the Master shower. The gutters are not even joined at the corners. The quality of the drywall finishing is fair at best.

title: Not what we expected
review_text: The appliances and toilets are shoddy, the cheapest that they could put in. Toilets have to be flushed twice, Plumber came out and said it was the cheapest model of toilet. Bad water pressure. Back wall is 6 feet tall outside, but we can see into each others houses. Going to have to pay to have 2 more rows added (after HOA approval). Should of been added by KB Homes, since there is no privacy and considering we had to pay $5,000 more for our lot location. The garage was set up too high and there was a sharp incline, not able to get cars into the garage. That since has been fixed, butshould of been done correctly the first time.

title: Disappointed
review_text: We are still waiting for our shower floor to be fixed, our final termite inspection and I have a paint issue we would like to discuss.

title: great, supportive sales team.....very average quality
review_text: Basement flooded before closing, touch up paint throughout the house with wrong color, pipes froze in the first month

title: Workmanship
review_text: Do not rush. Give them plenty of time.

title: Somewhat disappointing
review_text: Home seems well build.. we have an outlet that should be half hot that is not, so we have a wall switch that does nothing. We never got a 60 day walk thru we were told we would get..

title: KB Home - We have lived in our new KB home for 2 months.
review_text: We have lived in our new KB home for 2 months. We have noticed and continue to notice things that weren’t done correctly or were/are damaged during the construction process. What we do appreciate is the responsiveness of Jodi over at KB. He makes sure that whatever issues arise get resolve in a timely manner.

title: KB Home - Good price.
review_text: Good price. Cheap materials

title: Superintendent
review_text: Superintendent was the person who stand out the most. Price of home was decent. But starting to see cheap materials were used

title: Bowed roof on brand new home???
review_text: Bought a brand new home in Falling Water 2 and the build quality is not as good as I would expect with a new home and under the brand of KB Homes. The roof above my garage is bowed and they keep telling me it is a cosmetic defect... I am hiring roofing company to give me a second opinion

title: poor customer care service
review_text: ensure that all problems are fully resolved before you receive the house. Never sign the papers after walk-through if you have any issues in the house,they will say they gonna fix it just sign the papers but it will take them forever to fix it! Once the turnover is done, it is very hard to fix these issues, particularly when dealing with customer care service

title: KB Home
review_text: The overall finishing work of the house construction is unsatisfactory, and there are many areas where the work has been left incomplete or done in a careless and substandard manner.

title: Dissapointing and stressful
review_text: My overall experience with KB Home was challenging from start to finish. I did not feel fully valued as a customer at any point in the process. My concerns were often dismissed or addressed only after repeated follow-ups. The communication between sales, construction, and the design studio was inconsistent and caused avoidable delays and mistakes. I also faced financial stress because of incorrect information from the loan officer, which forced me to seek new financing at the last minute to avoid losing my earnest money. The quality of construction felt rushed and left me with several issuesin my new home. Overall, the process lacked transparency, urgency, and the level of care expected for such a significant purchase.

title: Room for Improvement in Quality and Service
review_text: I was disappointed with several aspects of the homebuying process and felt it did not meet my expectations. Some finishes and materials did not meet the standard I anticipated for the price paid, and there were minor issues that needed attention. Communication was sometimes unclear, and I felt that updates or timelines could have been more transparent. Given the issues I encountered, I did not feel the home fully reflected the value promised. While some concerns were addressed, there were delays in responses and resolution that caused frustration. I hope KB Home takes this feedback seriouslyto improve the experience for future homeowners.

title: Refrigerator
review_text: We have been trying to get our refrigerator replaced or refunded since we paid for it ourselves thru the design studio for 1100.00. It looks second hand and runs loud enough to hear 7 out of 8 hrs. They ordered doors without hearing our side of issue. This has been ongoing before we closed on house, said go ahead and close all issues will be taken care of, at who’s satisfaction. Had a range problem got that solved. Still have issue with cabinet inserts, put on order to replace. Essentially everything we paid extra for has had issues. Everyone who has told us they will come here to talkto us about this issue has not shown up. Wait all day and nothing. KB advertising says 100% satisfaction guaranteed. Not happening yet. We closed on 10/03/25. Would like for someone with some clout from KB to respond to this Thanks Unhappy homebuyers Paul & Diana W.

title: quick move in less than a month
review_text: it was a quick move in expirence, lots of issues though for a brand new home, not great customer service

title: Difficult build
review_text: I have only been in the home for 30 days and have already had to submit a warranty work order for a broken window. Overall the house is acceptable after much distress during the build with builder not wanting to fix damages caused by trades. Also please check your ac and insulation as mine were damaged, ductwork not taped properly or suspended properly. Insulation was only 3-4 inches in some areas of the attic and contract stated 10 inches throughout. Builder had more than 4 weeks to correct the issue and they did nothing until the last 24 hours before closing even though they stated to methat all was addressed and corrected. Workmanship was messy and not up to standard. All I asked was just do their job correctly and don’t cause more damage with every trade that entered the house. Overall I found a lack of respect for the client and no respect from the trades for the finishes in the house. Millwork in the house messy and substandard and black of attention to detail. I recommend that all clients hire a home inspector to check for things that are not up to code and have items professionally documented for your own security. My only saving grace was the project manager Dave, otherwise we would have had to spend another $10k to fix all the issues that LB homes refused to address.

title: After sales, you are on your own.
review_text: Sales person was very helpful and seemed very honest. When calling customer service of issues noted, we were told they would relay the information and have items corrected. No call back for a couple of months. I was told there would be a final walk through to review all systems of the house. This never happened. Post sales customer service is poor.

title: Poor quality materials
review_text: KB uses the cheapest materials and the GC does not seem to care or know how important is the appropriate installation. Warranty and repair process works fine, but only for stuff that is broken, not for wrong installation. In this case, GC will answer that it is installed per their standards even when it’s not installed correctly.

title: After purchase/move-in response
review_text: After final walk through and move-in the customer service rep has been terrible!!! This rep sent the after closing email with a few attachments which included a performance review that he completed giving himself the highest possible rating, could not believe this! Extremely slow to respond (if he even responds), seems very inexperienced answers are always “oh just look on line” or “we don’t do that” even if the KBHomes indicates you do! One plumber he sent out said “oh he is the laziest person! Very sad and not a good representation of KBHomes! Dissatisfied with after purchaseresponsiveness! This reps attitude, lack of customer service makes me question the actual KBHomes warranty, appears to be no way to get a different customer service rep to work with. He’ll say he will get someone here or let us know answer to question and it rarely happens, we have to follow-up with him! You’ve hired a person who is not reliable and lacks responsiveness,unfortunately have heard same issues with this person by many neighbors in this new community.

title: Poor Quality and Unresolved Issues
review_text: From the day I moved in, many things were already broken. The materials used feel very cheap, and everything seems to break easily. Even months later, the problems remain unresolved, and responsiveness to issues has been very poor.

title: Neal
review_text: I am not really pleased with the response of the warranty issues that we’ve had with the house

title: Terrible
review_text: Quality of the homes isn’t the best and checks are not properly completed prior to submitting the homes to the buyers. When issues arise, you won’t get a response. Emergency hotline is terrible as well.

title: Poor customer service once you home is funded
review_text: Do not close till ALL issues are resolve. They will do bare minimum once you are in home.

title: Still waiting on repairs that where supposed to be done during my initial walk through before getting my key
review_text: I understand this is a business but I feel as if things were rushed in order for everyone to get paid.

title: Fighting buyers remorse because of poor workmanship of home.
review_text: Not a great experience. So many issues with the construction. The subcontractors were terrible. This house has been one gigantic punch list. I have had to hire my own people to fix issues because I do not trust the subcontractors who would have to come back and fix their poor workmanship in the first place.

title: Home was put together in a hurry! Had many issues that had to be addressed!
review_text: Home was overpriced! A lot of broken promises!

title: Lack quality control and responsiveness
review_text: Sales and design ppl were great. However, construction manager T was not on top of ensuring house was built to standard. Many times he ensured me home was ready for my inspector, though it wasn't ( utilities off, major fixes needed, etc.) I had to put off closing many times cause of this. When I did close their were still issues needing repair. Building manager rushed me to sign ppwk so I could close, ensuring me the items would be fixed that same day. They weren't. It took almost a month for all things to get fixed, with me having to take many days off from work. Moral of the story,don't close until everything is to your satisfaction, as any repairs will take weeks to complete.

title: 10-Day Punch
review_text: The drywall, paint, landscapers, and tile/flooring companies are NO GOOD!! Plumbers & electrical are exceptional! AC company is ok. Brand new build my roof is already leaking due to the ridiculous open vents on roof. Do not allow them to put these open vents on your roof. Every time there is wind and rain you will see water leaking from your ceiling inside your home!! BEWARE!!

title: Lack of understanding the buyer
review_text: Lack of understanding the buyer

title: KB Homes
review_text: If you decide to build with KB do not under any circumstances move in until EVERYTHING is complete, no matter how tiny. Give yourself some wiggle room before you have to be out of your current home and into your new home so issues can be taken care of before you close. Please compare the workmanship of other home builders with whomever you decide to build with.

title: Jay Homeowner
review_text: I was really excited with the completion of my home but upon moving in, I feel the house was cheaply built. You can hear water going through the pipes when the bathroom and toilet is used. Some of the phone and cable wiring wasn't completed they just left it out. I have only one phone and internet port which was in the bedroom kind of ridiculous. The design studio should do a better job explaining upgrades. I would have loved to have higher ceilings and a bigger entry way rather than the standard to make moving furniture and appliances easier. Also main office team do not want to engage youin negotiating how you would want certain aspects of the house done if you have concerns. They just delay and dodge your emails for days. I really wish I had a better experience. But I must say the building supervisors have really nice personality but they are restricted to how much help they can offer you.

title: Micro Manage
review_text: Close to two months after closing and still getting things fixed. Getting YES to death and wanting issues resolved. I still cannot give a good review with things still needing repaired.

title: Not satisfied with Landscaping quality
review_text: I am not at all satisfied with Landscaping and missing few things by construction people. Construction people they are not installing what we have chosen in kb designer studios properly.

title: A Janky Sloppy Dirty Mess
review_text: Everyone I worked with one-on-one at KB Home was kind and helpful. This would include my sales agent, interior design specialist, loan processor and construction manager. But these kind people where not the ones assigned to do the actual physical building of my home. On a good note, My home seemed to have a solid foundation, sturdy frame construction, plumbing and electric that were up to par.. But after that the cosmetic work on my home left a lot to be desired. The paint/caulking, trim, carpet, tile/grout and landscaping where overall a sloppy sloppy disappointing mess that souredthe joy and usual personal satisfaction associated with building and buying a retirement home. There was over spray on cabinets. There was split trim and short cut trim with half inch gaps that were filled in and smoothed over by fat fingered caulking, Caulk and paint was smeared over dirty surfaces and even onto and into the carpeting. Poorly laid carpet had fraying gaps and holes. The new carpet was soiled with mud stains to the point that all the carpeting had to be steam cleaned. I think it should have been replaced. Both painted and stained surfaces had runs the size of an 18inch ruler. Over-all I paid hard earned money, saved over my lifetime, to KB Home who built me a new house with a"janky" interior. My yard looks like a sod remnant sale. There are two or three different kinds of grasses patched together from the front to the back and from side to side. When asked what type of grass is planted in my yard, I simply say in embarrassment, "it is green grass". Like the inside of the house my lawn is also a sloppy "janky" mess. In closing, I should mention that my home loan was originated through KB Home Loans. and I was constantly finding errors and mistakes in documents that had to be corrected and reworked throughout the process. And, because of that, I lost confidence in my loan originator and the loan process.

title: Mediocre built house
review_text: First time buying new home and I would not buy from KB Home if knew quality of house is this low. Mediocre built at best.

title: Home
review_text: Wish you could put in more quality products. Kind of cheap.

title: Wouldn’t recommend
review_text: Home buying is not an easy process. There’s always issues. The main problem I had was that communication with the loan officer was absolutely terrible. It took days to get a response, but then I was called several times and rushed to submit items. I had two different loan officers, one was great (I believe his name was Josh) and the other is the one I had issues with. My main loan officer, Dais, would call me at the end of the business day and even if I called back 2 minutes after missing her call, I would get no response until end of business day the next day. My realtor would reach out toher and she got a response, but I wouldn’t. The processor was even worse. She made several mistakes caught by others, did not apologize for any of them, and made me jump through extra hoops that were not necessary. As far as the construction, several items were wrong. Some were fixed, others weren’t and it took forever to get them fixed. I do not believe the home was worth the price. I received little to no amenities. For example, other companies provide a garage door opener, window blinds, and even a basic refrigerator for the same price that I purchased this home for. I did not receive any of those items. Other homes are going for the same price, in the same location, are larger and come with those amenities. Unfortunately, I felt like I was too far along in the process to go with another company. The reason why I gave this review two stars is because Val, the sales rep, was/is awesome! He always got back to me in a timely response. Helped me out with whatever I needed and helped escalate the issues I faced. Overall, I do not recommend this company

title: Nothing is being completed, but mor interested in getting 10’s on the evaluation
review_text: Same as above read and read understand our frustrations

title: Not the greatest
review_text: Building too fast, taking shortcuts to save in cost, cheap labor and materials for an overpriced home. Had issues in every phase of the process..

title: Bridson
review_text: Slow to get things repaired correctly

title: KB Homes needs work
review_text: As a general builder, my KB Homes experience was good. Great salesmen. Very personable and knowledgeable. The experience was not the same after the closing. The lack of communication was the most frustrating part. With mw knowing how the work with contractors can be, it was exhausting trying to get things resolved because of the lack of follow-through. The only saving grace was the salesman (Jim) having to get involved to try and assist in coordinating things to be resolved. If it wasn't for him, I would give KB a zero!!!!!!!!!

title: Not what I expected.
review_text: Homebuying experience was fair, could have been better in my opinion. Quality of workmanship & materials are not the greatest. I've notice a lot of paint overspray around the house and especially on my floors! I don't have time to be scrubbing paint off my new floors! I've noticed cracks and nicks on my cabinets, and nicks on my shower doors as well. The list goes on... I don't believe the house is worth for what I paid for it.

title: Ask about drainage issues with lot before you purchase
review_text: I was not informed when I was choosing my lot that there would be a drainage issue when it rains in my back yard. The backyard floods whereby the water stands making it unusable for several days. Several attempts to alleviate this issue have gone unsuccessful. This problem has tarnished my new home building experience.

title: Construction was done with different material than what was showed.
review_text: KB Homes builds the houses their way even after providing upgrade options. They replace the displayed material with cheaper material for cost savings. They do not care to fix that even upon customer's request. As you are locked in contract, you become liability.

title: Poor workmanship
review_text: Poor workmanship (paints, door and door locks)

title: It was an okay experience but I'm happy to have a brand new house in LA
review_text: I bought a new construction home from KB Homes and I have mixed feelings about it. On the positive side, the staff at the office was pleasant to work with and they tried their best to accommodate my needs. The rates were high, but that’s the market and I don’t blame KB for that. I did have some issues during underwriting, but they were not KB’s fault. On the negative side, I was disappointed with the standard items included in the home. They did not match the price I paid for the home and I felt like I was getting a low-quality product. I think KB should have included more features in thestandard package, such as a smart doorbell, higher quality finishes, and better lighting. All those things had an extra cost and I had to pay more to upgrade them. The most frustrating part was the post-closing process. It was difficult to get items addressed after closing and I had to follow up multiple times to get things fixed. The only person who was helpful and responsive was Donald, who is my favorite person at KB Homes. He was professional, courteous, and efficient. My advice to anyone who is considering buying a new home from KB Homes is to not rush your closing and to inspect everything carefully BEFORE you sign off. Make sure you get everything you want and need in your home and don’t settle for less. You are paying a lot of money for your home and you deserve to get the best quality possible.

title: Just like other production builders; good at the start, bad at the end.
review_text: Record all conversations and take pictures/videos of all stages of construction. Do not close and give them your money until everything is fully completed, including touch ups, repairs and replacements.

review_text: The quality of the finishings in the house is quite poor! The interior walls are of very poor quality, and the backsplash finishes are of very low quality!

Yard grading is terrible. Landscaping installed half dead. Was made to close way to fast and early. House should of been completed before closing

title: Too many issues
review_text: Too many issues that still need to be fixed after receiving home

title: There are still pending issues that we need them to be addressed from warrant team
review_text: Workmanship is not good at all. There are many poor quality works that we have found at many places of the home. Even when we asked them to be fixed the persons who came to fix did the same kind of poor quality repairs making no difference. At the time of closing we noted some issues that needed to be addressed. But due to closing timeline, we were told to go ahead get them fixed by warranty. There are two bigger items that are not addressed till date, that we definitely want them to be addressed. Chipped portion of counter top in kitchen that was just glued in, Drive way replacement as ithas cracks. In fact prior this year, I dropped from other home in East village - Classic collection for the drive way crack issue and they said they will not fix it. But after we dropped from that home, we saw the driveway was getting replaced. If we were told that will be fixed we would have very weel bought that home instead earlier this year. But now the same drive way crack happened here. Earlier when we signed the offer, there was no crack. But towards the closing it started showing and before closing we said this is not something that we are comfortable with. But we were told it is very minor and due to lack of time for closing date, it is better to close first and get them addressed by warranty later. Now we see that it evolved bigger and warranty team is saying it is in tolerable limits for them. But that is not aceptable answer for us as we had dropped from earlier hiome also for the same reason.

title: Construction Site Supervisor
review_text: All I can say is, have your builder fix everything before closing, after closing the trades are slow to respond. Holding up closing is the only way to hold the builder accountable for the repairs. After closing you are a low priority, they want the other houses to be built as fast as possible.

title: Never working with KB again
review_text: Overall we had to jump through hurdles to get the home. It has been almost two months and we still have not had all the things fixed in our house after living here for almost two months. According to the city, they have not paid their fees and finished the paperwork so we still do not have our certificate of occupancy meaning we can’t get trash cans, and I can’t put utilities in my name. Craftsmanship in the overall build is subpar. I regret not going with another builder and lender. I fell for the low interest rates which at 6.5% still isn’t that great. Overall this is been a terriblehomebuying process. I will not be using anything related to KB again.

title: Need to work on improving the communication
review_text: My experience is need to work on communication when it comes down in fixing things in my house.

title: We have been in our home for almost 2 months and are STILL waiting on many blue tape items to be fixed. Lots of promised that "we" will be here this afternoon or tomorrow. NOPE!
review_text: Trust me, you do not want my husband to write a review.

title: Mixed Bag: A Review of My KB Homes Experience
review_text: My experience with KB Homes has been a mixed bag. While there have been some positive aspects, the overall journey has been marred by several significant issues that are yet to be resolved. One of the standout problems with our KB Homes experience was the lack of transparency when it came to crucial information about our lot and an adjacent construction project. This lack of disclosure was a major disappointment, as it left us feeling like we were not adequately informed to make a decision about our purchase. Had we been given all the necessary details, we might have reconsidered ourchoice. Another area where KB Homes fell short of expectations was in the craftsmanship of our new home. We have experienced more issues in this brand-new home than we did with our previous builder, Meritage. This speaks to a potential lapse in quality control and attention to detail during the construction process, which is concerning when investing in a new property. On a more positive note, we do appreciate the layout and design of our KB home. It's clear that thought went into creating a functional and appealing living space, and this aspect of the home has been a redeeming factor in an otherwise lackluster experience. In conclusion, our experience with KB Homes has been characterized by a lack of transparency, disappointing craftsmanship, and ongoing issues. While the layout and design of our home are pleasing, these positives are overshadowed by the negatives we have encountered throughout our journey with KB Homes. It is my hope that the company can address these issues promptly and improve its overall customer experience moving forward.

title: Poor quality
review_text: Do your research before buying a new built from KB Homes. The quality of materials used to build these homes is cheap. So many issues with our new built, what a disappointment.

title: N/a
review_text: Nothing to say about anything

title: Maybe 7/10 (Definitely NOT 10/10)
review_text: Model homes looked great, but after moving into the actual home, I realized the quality of KB Home is not top notch. You can tell people who painted and did the construction didn’t pay attention to details. You can hear impact noise from your next door neighbor. KB Home’s frontline employees (sales, superintendent) are great, but sr. leadership must drive quality for the whole company so it can trickle down.

title: Problems with workmanship and quality!
review_text: Been here a little over a month and have had a bunch of issues with the house from electrical to plumbing to paint. Thought I was buying a quality home from a quality builder. Sense of urgency is just not where it should be to get issues resolved. Not too happy.

title: Disappointed
review_text: Sales and finance were excellent. Rolanda and Brad did a great job. Disappointed with interior craftsmanship and finish. Sloppy work and finishes feel cheap. Multiple issues with the home. Resolution of these issues being accomplished slowly.

title: Sales and design
review_text: We’re proud of our new home however I’m ashamed of how things were handled in the last week or 2 of the process

title: Poor follow up on things
review_text: Finance took too long with certain documents which caused the closing to be delayed several times. The house is not fully complete and there were many flaws in the home. Still waiting on items to be taken care of even though several months have gone by since living in the home. Majority of the items owner had call the contractors for each job directly. It felt like I was doing someone else’s job.

title: Not my typical experience with KB
review_text: Less than average experience so far in this new community.

title: So many work orders pending within 2 months of moving in.
review_text: Within mere 2 months of moving in the new built home we have already faced so many issues in the working capacity of the house. Listing a few here - patio screen door came off, no electricity in the laundry room, kitchen cabinets are coming off, master bedroom door lock is installed opposite way , 2 bulbs are plunging out of the wall, and so on. We put in work orders and only 1 work order was done and rest all are remaining. TOTALLY DISAPPOINTING. We bought 625,000 home for this,????? Need answers.

title: Take too long to fix the problem
review_text: The subfloor on the second floor is completely loose, whenever you walk on some spots you can feel it moving and making noise cracking. When we moved in, we have bees right above our front porch, 2 months pass they still trying to figure out how to remove them. Small details they are fast to fix, but some more complicated problems they take time. Poor job on details. Food materials

title: Sherri
review_text: Poor communication Poor follow thru Poor quality workmanship

title: Poor communication
review_text: The start of the buying process was great, everyone was nice, friendly, and communicative, but once we moved into the house, people stopped communicating with us and now we have to deal with a jerk who is our rep named Brett. I requested a new rep and the response is that we can't have anyone else. That's great customer service, make us have to deal with a jerk who has no interest in actually helping us or answering emails. We had large mistakes happen in the house and had to have them all fixed once we moved in. If you plan on adding an AC unit with KB homes, you'll want to check it whenit's ran because they do a bad job at running it so that in just a few years, it'll go out.

title: Good at first…
review_text: At first, Kb Homes was great. So much communication. It was great. However, towards the closing, I was worried. They rushed us to close by the quarter deadline saying that they’ll fix the things later. Well, I’m still waiting: floors to be relaxed because of scratches; a fridge to be replaced because it is dented in two places; 1/4 of my sprinkler heads don’t pop up; my garage door just broke; my shutters need to be punted; and I never received my Wi-Fi AC box that I was told was standard. I’ve only lived here two weeks. Needless to say, I’m extremely disappointed. At first, I told everyonethat KB was great, but now I will be sure to caution everyone. DO NOT SIGN UNTIL THEY FIX EVERYTHING!

title: Not the best
review_text: Bad experience with part of KB Home employes and quality of work

title: Review
review_text: The home buying as far as the loan and sales were ok. The quality of the workmanship on the home is subpar. No quality control. The vendors used needs to be held more accountable for their sloppy workmanship. Still awaiting response for my squeaking floors. Placed a workorder several weeks ago and no one has responded.

title: Good buying experience until water leak was discovered!
review_text: Seller’s agent Tanika had been great but I had some bad experience with a couple of others after discovering a water leak with about a week after moving in. I picked KB because of some friends who had a good experience at another city. Maybe it was the pandemic, the supplies shortages, the shortage in workers but they could really do better.

title: Rushed and Poor Craftsmanship
review_text: It took 15 months to build our home start to finish and it wasn’t even fully completed when we were pressured to close at the end of April 2023 so corporate could meet their goals, and we are now facing ongoing repairs and issues with the craftsmanship inside the home. KB REALLY REALLY REALLY needs to pay better workers to come in and build the homes. You guys would save so much money in the long run. Stop thinking so short-term with your costs and just think what a glowing review we’d have left you if your craftsmanship would’ve been better. Also, if the home would’ve actually been completedin a timely fashion instead of thrown together at the last minute so you could make your closing goal for the month. We love our homesite and community, and the repairs will be completed in time, but we just wish KB would care more about the homes they are building. Get rid of your cheap labor and pay your people better so you get better quality workers in your homes. Foreign workers or not, don’t scrape the bottom of the barrel to save a buck, you’re now going to pay for it on the back end anyway. Not smart business, and not at all good for customer service. Your Superintendent Grant and Project Manager Justin have been awesome and responsive to our concerns, but they can’t fix shoddy craftsmanship because they can’t watch every homesite daily to catch the bad work. STOP SACRIFICING QUALITY TO SAVE A BUCK. It’ll be what I tell anyone who asks me if they should consider KB Homes; they sacrifice quality to make money. Build with them at your own risk.

title: KB reps lied
review_text: There was an existing drainage issue. KB's representative and her manager assured us that the drainage would be addressed in a couple of months last November. It has been seven months, and my driveway was flooded with water last night after the rain. I tried to follow up, but there was no response.

title: Quality Build Issues
review_text: Quality of work is kind of substandard. Stucco paint fell off in my front of the house. Laundry door is not shutting properly and misalign. A few items that was not being install during the walkthrough I have to go back to my list contract to provide those item. Nails found in our backyard . carpet installation not install properly and some other issues not to mention. Noted that this is my 2nd KB home that I purchase probably my last one. In fairness Troy, Chad and Klay are very helpful and professional.

title: Overpriced homes
review_text: Homes are worth maybe $400k from a value perspective, advertise starting at $540k with low grade fixtures, appliances, cheap lighting, cheap counter tops, other than kitchen, everything is low quality unless you pay for upgrades. After $120k in upgrades, you still have an unfinished basement, and lack things you should get standard. Lipstick on a pig best describes KB Homes value proposition.

title: Ana and Jesus
review_text: Material used for home are cheap. Blue tape was taken lightly and not fixed appropriately. Claim calls have been great! Response has been fast.

title: Issues not fixed
review_text: Issues that were address at the beginning have not been fixed. I get told dates and still no one comes out. Would be nice to at least get an update.

title: Poor workmanship
review_text: Do not do your closing until the house is exactly ready and to your satisfaction they promise to do the outstanding work necessary and never have time once they get your money you are in your own.

title: Depressing
review_text: Moving into a new house should be a wonderful experience. It's been a nightmare. I referred 2 people and now feel bad for them. I tell everyone I know to stay away. I still have issues after 2 months because KB hires the cheapest laborers amd then wonders why we as residents always call

title: Rushed experience, lacking customer-first mindset.
review_text: For anyone looking to purchase a KBHome, buyer beware that you are about to embark on a rushed, confusing, and pressured experience. Before the purchase of our home and for an extremely short period thereafter, the responsiveness and ability to make promises made to assist, carry out requests, and fix issues were satisfactory. However, the follow-through on promises and communication regarding the status of issues have been extremely frustrating. It is clear that is important through the rushed purchase process that the company places utmost priority in getting a customer through to thefinish line of purchasing but afterward, responsiveness, service, and customer-first attitude falls off a cliff. I had to escalate an email to upper management to receive any attention.. and it still not completed to date. The package is pretty at the surface, but the deeper you dig, the harder it is to stay in love with the deal you made. Also, please be weary of the $10,000 upfront that you need to put in that they don‘t tell you about to secure your purchase!! If you choose to back out, you lose $10,000. Ouch.

title: Jose m
review_text: I think they need better supervision on the house works especially at the terminations that's ones are very bad

title: Average
review_text: I get an astronomical amount of automated junk mail and calls from KB home. Representatives begging for survey scores rather than offering solutions. I get contacted more about surveys than I do from the post sale representative that is suppose to take care of my punch list.

title: Some good some bad some unacceptable
review_text: I bought the home while still living in California which was a challenge but luckily I had friends in San Antonio that filled in for me on some important dates the first was the walkthrough on my new home my friends drove an hour to meet with the employees from KB homes they pointed out a few things and we're assured that they would be corrected. On the second and final walkthrough my friends again drove an hour to meet with KB homes employees some of the things that they had pointed out during the first walkthrough were corrected others were not like the refrigerator door was dinged upscratched and was unacceptable they were told it was on order another thing that wasn't taking care of was the painting that was noticeable in the entryway of the home they were assured it would be taken care of another thing that was not corrected was the water pressure was still very low they we're told that it was the way it is because of the rain type shower head that was installed in the home. I moved in a week later my friends met me here at the house because they had my keys garage door opener etc and they pointed out the things that were needed to be corrected and hadn't been the next day I met with Juan the project manager who came to my home and I pointed out the things that should have been taken care of prior to my arrival he told me that it wasn't him that met with my friends it was his employee who I can't remember his name my friends told me that it was one and the other guy that they met with the second time I was told then that the refrigerator doors were on back order and that they would be arriving soon it's been 2 weeks and the second door just arrived when I looked at the packing slip it said that they were not ordered until April 13th which was after I got here there's still spots that need to be retouched with paint and I keep getting promises that someone will show no one has taken care of it since. I'm a little disheartened at the response that I'm getting from Juan who apparently is the project manager of these things I've also pointed out a few other things to one that are unacceptable and I keep getting another promise I just hope that these things will be taken care of before the warranty expires. I just noticed and reported to one that there's condensation in the study windows and he assured me that it was still under warranty and would make note of it. I understand that with new homes little things like this come up but I'm not getting the response from KB homes that I should be in my opinion. I don't want excuses I want action and they seem to have failed on that level. I also appreciate someone taking responsibility instead of blaming someone else again I just hope that this is all taken care of soon as I'm settling into my new home it'd be nice to not have to worry about warranty expiration dates and response from a project manager.

title: Have been having the worst experience after closing.
review_text: I've had a great experience with the salesperson - Joel and the loan originator - Michelle. They were easy to talk to and work with. However, I only got to know the construction superintendent a couple of days before my closing while I should have been in contact with him throughout the building process. After closing, I came to my house and noticed numerous issues such as dirty windows and bath tabs, and paint on the floor. I reported that but no one cared. My windows and bathtubs are still not clean. There was mold in my kitchen, I reported that but it's been a month and a half since theyremoved my cabinets, and haven't installed them back yet saying that we are waiting for the cabinets to arrive.
