review_text: Don't use KBhome. The workmanship is very messy. They build fast and that leaves a lot of room for mistakes and carelessness

title: KB Home - The worst construction crew (Hector)Ernesto was helpful.
review_text: The worst construction crew (Hector) Ernesto was helpful.

title: I still have issues with my home.
review_text: I would like for the contractor to come finish my house

title: KB Home - The house was not ready by closing and my great room and flex
review_text: The house was not ready by closing and my great room and flex room electric outlets were not working. It took them almost a month to fix my front door frame and paint it. In the master bedroom the floor is making a sound because the plywood was not tightened before carpet was installed. Everything about my first home buying experience went wrong

title: Builder was great himself.
review_text: The “brand new” home as well as the whole process was rushed and not completed by the time we were moved in. It took longer than expected to build the home as well which made this process a little frustrating. The house looks nice though given everything else.

title: KB Home - Have current issues that were pointed out & not taken care of.
review_text: Have current issues that were pointed out & not taken care of. Which is frustrating considering the builders are in my area. As a matter of fact right across the street from me. That’s really upsetting! I shouldn’t have to hunt you down just to get my issue resolved. I’m very disappointed.

title: Warranty department unresponsive
review_text: Not getting initial issues and workmanship defects addressed. Terrible communication. Unresponsive representatives.

title: Details matter
review_text: Overall just another large builder that slaps things together. Not much attention to the details, which build on each other, and the finish product reflects that. A common statement is 'you won't notice once blind/pictures/etc are installed'... that's covering up shotty work, which should have just been done correctly to start with. Wouldn't go with same builder again.

title: Mixed feelings, it all started good and finished really bad. Our sales rep was great and then it went downhill from there
review_text: I don't know if the workmanship went down because of the Covid19 and we ended up with 3 different contractors working on our house. We did not get many chance to walk the house until then end. We had brought up some issues and was told it was part of the build. I had also pointed out some stuff to be fixed in our final walk and because the walk was the afternoon before the signing I could not verify that they were not fixed. We had to rush the closing because of the pandemic because there were so many uncertainties and felt like we were threatened to cancel the sale if we did not do certainthings. It actually was an awful experience in the end, I wish i had waited.

title: Not what I expected
review_text: Things had been resolved from the pressure of closing and not having my appliances in my house at the time of closing but there has been so much turnover in superintendents that issues I had just fell through the cracks. There is no follow-up anymore. Once you close - it's like you don't matter anymore.

title: Poor workmanship
review_text: The more I walk through the home , the more I find needing to be fixed. Most of what need to be fixed is from poor workmanship and what appears to be lack of oversight. Walls that curve, carpet with lumps, squeaky floors I have gathered 3 pages so far. KB has a good product . They really need to watch the contractors closer. KB has been challenged with the fact that Covid-19 hit and they had to lay off numerous employees. They did still push the house to market to achieve their financial goal . Unfortunately for us, we are the ones paying for it.

title: NEEDS IMPROVEMENT!!!
review_text: WHAT WAS PROMISED WAS NOT GIVEN TO US!!! THE AMOUNT ON UPGRADES WE SPENT AND THE PRODUCT THAT WAS GIVEN TO US (DAMAGED) AND THEY KEEP ON TELLING US THAT WE WILL FIX A BRAND NEW PRODUCT IS UNACCEPTABLE AND BTW STILL NOT FIXED WAITING ON THEM TO CALL

title: Disappointed
review_text: Would love to say my new home was everything I hoped it would be but unfortunately that is not the case. Things that were taped during our final walkthrough were never repaired, tons of cracks in the grout along walls and doors which has led to chunks of grout coming out during sweeping and mopping, closet doors were damaged upon move in in every room, "privacy" upgraded insulation offers less "sound dampening" than the regular insulation in our last house, no peephole in front door, sliding screen door lock does not work, door knobs were installed incorrectly, door to garage does not latch,I could go on and on. I'm extremely disappointed.

title: Terrible workmanship and followup
review_text: Many problems with floors, paint, tile, cabinet and more have made what could have been a great buying experience. The home has energy saving devices and insulation that can save a lot over the years but the nagging problems that remain makes me warn people I talk with to look somewhere else. Punch list items will not be taken care of. So if you don't mind getting irritated every day when you look around at all the problems that have been ignored by KB Homes, you might like their townhomes in Ormond Beach. Couple things to think about if you look at their townhomes, the units on the outsideof the street have two parking spaces in their driveway whereas the units on the inside only have one because the sidewalk is in front of the units on the inside of the street. Also, units on the outside can park in the street and partially on their lot for a third space where the units in the inside cannot again because of the sidewalk. One of those things you don't even think about until you move in and notice your neighbor across the street has 3 cars parked there and you can only park one.

title: Not finished
review_text: Have many issue that need to be finished

title: Not the best
review_text: Lots to be said. The quality for what I paid for is less than great.

title: Nice home but after being in it for a month quality control was obviously lacking
review_text: I purchased a new home and after moving in it became obvious that the quality control was poorly managed or nonexistent. The painting was poorly completed and the subsequent touchups that were done were done in a different finish. There are other issues as well, including the dishwasher not being installed properly which resulted in it flooding the kitchen. They respond quickly to the issues but it is obvious that no one tested anything before I purchased the home and I wonder what else was not properly tested. It seems to me that they slap that houses up as fast and cheaply as possible andthen deal with the fallout later.

title: Not as good as we hoped
review_text: Cody said he would replace our driveway and power wash our garage that’s covered in paint. I’ll be happy to change this after that is done but it’s now been just about 2 months and we are getting the run around we feel like with no direct answer. Our furnace acts ups time to time. The paint inside the house is super cheap and if you accidentally rub your shoulder in the wall it scratches. We love the house as a whole just want the driveway replaced and garage power washed and I’ll be happy. Oh we also had construction workers steal water from our hose which isn’t normal.

title: New Home
review_text: Well...still waiting on major repairs for poor craftsmanship.

title: Not the best house for the money
review_text: For the money paid for house, I think the quality and workmanship is a bit lacking. Also, we picked a larger lot to install a pool in the future. But due to the extreme excavation to the back yard (which we discovered was necessary after the house build was well underway) to facilitate drainage. We may not be able to install a pool or even afford to install a pool. Not too happy with KB Homes right now.

title: Very dissatisfied
review_text: Very dissatisfied... before moving in things were already breaking. Front door handle fell apart, fire extinguisher fell off and broke when someone walked over it upstairs, windowsill paint/drywall mushed when it rained. I HATE that you can hear everything if someone is walking upstairs, talking the walls are to thin, and the fact that everything is an upgrade $600 K for a poorly made cookie cutter home is such a big disappointment.

title: KB is Horrible and I am very dissatisfied with the completion of my new built
review_text: Horrible communication and customer loyalty! KB does not care about anything but the sale.

title: Quantity over Quality
review_text: The tradesmen are majority unqualified. Framers are terrible, painters lazy, masons were good, roofers try to get too much done to quickly and the shingles are loose because nails are missing to anchor them down. Siding is not smooth. The bath in the master constantly creeks and squeaks. The front door light kicks the breaker every time we turn it on which kills the light and fan in the powder room. Drywall is not level in window frames from top to bottom some also from side to side. The fence blew over in a storm. The flood light already stopped working on the back of the house. Thelight switches have no attempt of coordination in sequence of what light turns on in the home, you just keep flipping them on and off till you get the desired light to illuminate.

title: Extremely disappointed
review_text: For the cost of our new home we are extremely disappointed in the overall process. Very poor quality workmanship. Still having issues 30+ days after move in.

title: Low quality and service after purchase.
review_text: Quality of work is very poor.

title: The List of Issues is Long.
review_text: Complete disregard and lack of expected quality craftsmanship for a $550K home. Lots of cutting corners to push towards a short-deadline closing. Lots of pressure put on building superintendents and managers to close the books on several homes at the same time. First night in the house the thermostat went bonkers and started the A/C when it was 19 degrees outside. Brand new whirlpool oven didn't work for nearly two weeks. Rather than bring us a new one like we had asked they deferred us to their home warranty department. Well over 20 items still awaiting fixes and or repairs after beingin the home for almost two months now. We are very concerned about the long-term probability of greater unseen (aka under-the-skin/below the surface issues and concerns arising from the obvious poor craftsmanship and rush to close. Easy to put some of the blame on us the buyers for accepting after the final inspection, but not something taken lightly when you've been living in a hotel and air BnB for a month while getting promise after promise by the builder that everything's going to be taken care of to 100 percent of your satisfaction, and that your home will as good as or better build quality than their best model home. Yes, that's precisely what we were told on several occasions. Couldn't have been farther from the truth. In hindsight I never should have accepted/signed or gone to closing, but instead played hardball and insisted on them refunding me my $10K earnest money and took my business elsewhere. My only concern with this thought is that I think the lack of quality craftsmanship in the new homes being built in this area is more wide-spread than I can imagine. I wonder who's really passing the various inspections of these homes at the city level? Lastly, our poor neighbors. We consider ourselves fortunate the we are not having to deal with the plethora/myriad of issues they're having to deal with as I speak. These poor folks have been having no-kidding deplorable and unimaginable issues. To the point where they are about to go after them with a class action lawsuit demanding compensation for their time or the full refund/buy back of their homes. It's really unacceptable how KB Home has treated these retired folks who have worked hard their entire lives only to have to deal with this kind of stuff.

title: KB Home - Throughout the process I have been let down by Kb homes every
review_text: Throughout the process I have been let down by Kb homes every step of the way. If I was to do it all over I would not purchase a home with KB.

Water comes thru our door. Paint still on our carpet and floor. It has been a bad experience since day 1. From loan process to moving into home.

title: Kb review
review_text: Workmanship not up to par, shoddy finish.

title: My what should have been new home
review_text: Walls are not straight water got under my home my bathroom is jacked up cracks in my ceiling something about the drywall you can here people using the bathroom you just have no privacy in the home the room on north side is just nuts when someone uses the microwave or bathroom I have real questions with the quality of the material I can get a nail and push it into the drywall no hammer needed my windows have damage and nobody seems to give a

title: Quality is ok; Service is severely lacking
review_text: The value for the price we paid for our new home is okay, despite the continued issues before after move in. However, the communication and service from the construction team and lender were very disappointing. I would highly recommend that future homebuyers stay far far away from KB.

title: If I could turn back the hands of time...
review_text: Asked to resubmit my first review bc “doesn’t meet website guidelines “ ... laughable smh... Here’s a summary: Nice staff/responsiveness... 4 stars Value for price .... 3 stars Trustworthiness... 1 star.. lost trust from the very beginning to the end. A pure nightmare Quality... 1 star .. never moved into a new home that was presented new ... (sloppy, debts, splatters, wires etc)

title: Contractors and studio workers were great but.
review_text: Here's my experience, I never had a issue with the contractors. They were probably the shinning star of this company. Everything we talked to them about was done promptly and handled accordingly. We had issues come up with framing and they were promptly handled with the proper care I would expect from a company bidding for my business. The design studio gets a A+ also. I forget the name of the woman who helped us, but she was also a rockstar. She helped us explaining the cost differences and what to expect down the road. Now the face of the company was horrible and will make me neverbuy from this company again. Becky was rude at times, short and was never very sympathetic for issues that came up. Some of the issues: I had questions about the contract and she talked over me instead of discussing it with me, we had issues with our first Lender and had to start the whole process over again, Becky continued to hound my wife and I about our lender, eventually cc'ing us in a very rude and unprofessional email, which was sent to our lender. This letter was so rude my lender vowed that his company will never work with KB Homes again. At the very end of it Becky threatened us with our lender, saying that if our lender couldn't come up with a loan agreement that our contract would be voided out, unless we went with KB Homes lenders. When it came down to my family doing our final walkthrough, my wife and I had scheduled a trip to Texas for my work conference. We made it very well known in the beginning to middle of the project these dates were a no go for us to close due to that (my wife had paid 600ish for the conference and plus plane tickets, rental cars, hotel rooms etc.). Becky insisted that we need to be there on this date which was a few days before we were due back, couldn't even work with us about pushing it out a few days. After numerous days of arguing about it Becky, asked us to have our father-in-law who just had a heart attack (which Becky knew), who couldn't even drive and was not mentally capable at that moment (due to his health) to come to get the keys and do the final walkthrough. After a long time arguing, complete frustration, and Hopelessness that this would never get resolved we finally were able to get our date pushed out. I am beyond frustrated with the whole process besides the three (1 design studio, 2 contractors) people I spoke about above. As for the pricing, I have remodeled my home before and I have a good idea how much labor and materials cost. You guys would of raked me over the coals, with flooring if I had not done the flooring in my old home.. The up charge on the materials alone made me reconsider adding anything extra through you guys. It would of been nice to have a home ready to move into at the end of the process instead of having to do construction for the first two weeks of living there. I much rather have the thousands of dollars in my pocket that were the difference in the prices quoted by you guys instead of the actual cost. The home Is beautiful and was well made. That's why I gave the for stars.... everything else would of been a 1 due to becky, except for the people I mentioned above.

title: Responsiveness of issues after moving in.
review_text: Before signing on the closing day there were several issues that were done at last minute like the grading on the left hand side in between houses. By now KB home had worked on this matter several times and stilll not resolve. Also, the were a crack on the ceiling by the dining room area that three different people have tried to fix. The biggest issue is the Grading in between the house. I keep being told that has to do with the city. I know it doesn’t has to do anything with the city. The last crew that was here broke the cable for spectrum and taped it together and didn’t say anything aboutit. I really like to have the grading done right.

title: price increase
review_text: they did not respect the agreed initial price, they made me waste a lot of time

title: All talk and nothing done
review_text: We did our first walk through and we found some things ( scratch tub, wire sticking out of stucco, roof tile missing nails, dead plants and some texture and painting issues). Also the superintendent showed up 30 min late. He said that he would add trim in the garage for being late. A week later was our closing date and it rain 2 days during that week. We had another walk through and the only thin was fixed was the paint and texture issues. He said the they can fix the issues while we are living their. I did not want to slow anything down so I said okay. It took them 2 weeks after I saidsomething again to fix the issues the only thing they did was fixed the tub. Now almost 2 months later the super called me and ask how everything is doing. I told he that our plants still need to be replaced, fix the roof, stucco, 2 doors need fixed ( 1 does not latch and the other does not closed), we saw a hole in the bottom of the cabinet in the bathroom, and I put a new faucet in the kitchen and it looks like it had some water damage on the bottom shelf of the cabinet. He told me he would schedule me for the nexted friday and nobody called me and nobody showed.

title: Not totally happy.
review_text: Everyone here is friendly and pleasant.. Terry has been responsive, and has tried to help. However some issues are still unresolved. Eric told me to wait 10 months and everything would get taken care of then. Joe came over and looked at te things that needed addressed and gave me an appointment over a month away.

title: Experience
review_text: Steve and Katrina were great. That’s did all the could to make things right. The builder are sloppy, missing paint, holes in door, A C went out 3 times. Door off hinges. I could go on and on. The sales team is awesome. Need hire better builder that cares.

title: Kb home works with many companies to build 1 home
review_text: I don't like that KB home works with so many companies to build 1 home. Quality is not good, Do to this theirs so many cracks and holes. When reporting issues to the superintendent all he does is send you to the customer service number?, which no one ever answers or returns phone calls.

title: Hit many bumps during the whole process
review_text: I wasn't impressed with my sales rep. Buying a new house is a lot of money and I expected top customer service quality (took several days to get a response, did not meet deadlines). Lot of the upgrades are overpriced to the point where it's still cheaper to rip out KB installed finishings and install new ones after closing. Lot of the base finishings is very low quality and that's how they keep prices competitive but get you to pay more to upgrade to something more reasonable. After closing, our After Sales Customer Service representative was also very hard to get in touch with and ignoresmost of my emails asking for an update. KB subcontracts a lot of the final stages of work (carpet, counters, appliances, etc). In my experience, it was all sub-par (counter overhang turned out to be a underhang meaning the closed drawers extended past the counter edge, holes in shower wall slabs, missing door pins, missing dead bolt screws). I'm pretty easy going but my new KB home purchase left a lot to be desired.

title: KB Home - Having lot of post closing issues and those are not addressed
review_text: Having lot of post closing issues and those are not addressed properly. No point of having 1 month check in of everything has to go through the product warranty.

title: KB Home - Bad quality.
review_text: Bad quality. Bad painting so thin came off easily. Everything damage easy

title: Professionalism
review_text: The initial process with KB Loan Officers and builder was unprofessional; consistently lacking in communication. Neither the loan officers or builder were on the same page on status or updates. The overall construction was okay but responsiveness needs improvement.

title: Great Sales/Finance Staff, Poor Quality Builder
review_text: Overall, very disappointed with our new home. Model homes look great, but the actual build on all the houses in our neighborhood are very poor quality. Cheap materials and even cheaper labor -- skill level of the contractors is abysmal. If you're looking for a quality home in the $325K+ range, look elsewhere.

title: so many issues!
review_text: So many issues to mention...

title: Maybe reviews are legit online about kB homes.
review_text: Sales person Arron was the only bright spot

title: Overall construction quality is not good enough!
review_text: Responsiveness to issues by the construction team was slow and unsatisfactory.

title: Paint
review_text: The interior paint job is extremely sloppy! There are already several cracks in the walls and chipped paint that was not caused from move

title: Poor attention to details.
review_text: Several annoying issues such as wrong appliance installation, cracks in the driveway pavement, and hanging electrical wires that impede the closure of kitchen cabinet drawers.

title: Not a happy homeowner
review_text: In short, everything was just done to get the house off the market. Everything was mediocre. Thankful for our sales person Gina and superintendent Kevin for hearing us and doing their best to correct the errors. Tips: DO NOT sign off or accept keys till everything and I mean everything is completely done right. From paint jobs to working light bulbs. From flooring to any dents and holes around the house!

title: Room for improvement needed
review_text: Our sales Rep Warren was amazing. He kept us in the loop the whole process. He gets a 5 star. The lending/processor/underwriter were terrible. Lacked communication, follow up, made promises and commitments that were never kept. Do not use KBHS.

title: Exhausting
review_text: The process was exhausting. Although everyone had excellent personalities and reached out, there was a comedy (and by comedy I mean incredibly Frustrating & unprofessional) of errors that gave us anxiety, stress & ended up costing us money due to delay. Although the home is beautiful now that it's complete, I would find it difficult to provide a recommendation in the future.

title: KB Home - KB homes worked hard to sell the house but once I got into the
review_text: KB homes worked hard to sell the house but once I got into the house I have ID'd a lot of flaws that were identified during the walk through and were not fixed. During acceptance I was told they would get fixed after signing. When I called customer service to correct it I ran into a dead end so i went to the the office where I was told they dont handle issues like that once the house is excepted. I would not reccomend kb homes to anyone.

title: Nice Home. Questionable Staff.
review_text: Problems from the day one. Decided to continue homebuying after they worked with us to fix problems. Then problems arose again after our walk through. Tried to sell us a home with broken cabinets. Had to write multiple emails up to vice president to get our problems fixed. Staff is untrustworthy and unprofessional. We love our home but what it took to get it right was ridiculous.

title: KB Home - Communicate communicate communicate and do no settle.
review_text: Communicate communicate communicate and do no settle.

title: Build Elsewhere
review_text: KB uses the cheapest sub contractors possible and it clearly shows in their workmanship. KB employees will try their best to make best of a situation but the work actually being completed during construction is horrendous.

title: New house
review_text: Many small issues and couple of big things we found and let the kB know but no one comes to fix it

title: Poor quality and slow to fix
review_text: KB is quick to force you into the new home before it's ready, then takes forever to resolve issues under warranty. We wait weeks for resolution, as things get pushed back or no one shows up for scheduled repair visits. Extremely dissatisfied with KB currently.

title: Nice home with many, many repairs to be done.
review_text: It's a nice community with the understanding that the building of a new home would be a pleasant one. However, after the home was completed, it was left with numerous repairs and careless errors. There were some individuals who took care of the situation mindfully and thank goodness. But it's mind boggling that there were so many repairs to be done 2-3 months after the house was closed on.

title: Not happy with KB
review_text: My home was not clean/ready before I moved in.Wrong keys given(I am not kidding..!!) I called for some repair at home, did not get any response for two months.Will not recommend KB to my friends.

title: Not Great
review_text: Partly my fault for not making sure what was getting. Asked about screen for back porch but no one bothered to tell me there was no porch on this house changed the color of cabinets t hey picked and asked for different counter top in kitchen which they did not change after picking different cabinets. At fist very responsive to any problems or flaws but soon started not responding very well if at all. still have issues not taken care of.

title: KB Home - Dont get any upgrades.
review_text: Dont get any upgrades. The materials are cheap and fit and finish is horrible.

title: Disappointed
review_text: Management at KB Homes were very unhelpful and inefficient. Problems still persist with home more than 3 months after closing and the company is slow and inefficient in their resolution.

title: KB Home - We bought house more than month ago & master bathroom sink is
review_text: We bought house more than month ago & master bathroom sink is leaking already & have water puddles underneath. Due to that issue, the cabinet got damaged too. I think KB home use very cheap materials to build home. How can we trust KB home, since we already have major leak issue after one month. Very disappointed & price we paid is way more because the materials used are very cheap.

title: Commitment
review_text: What’s committed at the time of closing is not yet done . I am still waiting for that . And also no body contacted me for 1 month inspection.

title: Love the place, Not so much the Customer Service or responsiveness.
review_text: It is like jumping through hurdles to get someone to tell you what, when and how to repair something. There is so much red tape. You go through many people trying to find out if your issue is even in the pipeline. If your issue is a small fix, the repair is sloppy, causing other damage. Make sure every little thing is fixed and checked prior to closing to avoid these unnecessary headaches. I am still waiting for the new working keys to the front gate. It is a shame, as I like the property.

title: Remember, KB is a tract home builder
review_text: Workmanship is as a traditional tract home builder. Maybe even sub par against that benchmark and the associated competition. Limited attention to detail. Lower end components for the money; faucets, appliances, toilets, and carpet. Interior painters are terrible. I don't think they know what blue tape is or what it used for. Other than that, the house seems to be solidly built, so far.

title: Avoid upgrade and do it you own
review_text: Material used is cheap, upgrade is limited and expensive. Not very good build quality either.

title: Hurriedly built home with poor QA/QC
review_text: Trades unsupervised. Home not delivered on time as promised. 2 months trying to get issues resolved that should have been done correctly before closing. Representatives of KB relaying incorrect information.

title: Very unorganized company
review_text: We moved in less than 3 months ago and the walls and ceilings are already starting to separate and show visible holes. There are 4 additional issues that we have been waiting to get addressed as well.

title: Lying and terrible workmanship
review_text: Save yourself some headache and just go buy a CBH. Superintendent and tradesman don’t care about you or what they’re doing. I have missing splotches of paint in my house. I had a 3’x3’ drywall patch upon move in that looked like I did myself. Not a single straight line anywhere. Carpet is already peeling up. Terrible workmanship and an absolute lack of craftsmanship.

title: Charles D
review_text: Actually, this is the third home I have purchased in the last 20 years. My first home was bought by my ex-wife, who added me to it. We then built a second home together. The quality of the homes was satisfactory, and the builders was accurate. They suggested a meeting shortly thereafter. However, I am quite dissatisfied with the responses I have received regarding at least eight or nine issues I raised directly with the builder. I will refrain from naming anyone, but I am sincerely disappointed.

title: Not a good experience
review_text: Just to summarize from the beginning, our loan officer for some reason switch to another loan officer, information wasn't input correctly had to redo paper. We didnt have confidence after that. Once the building started some concerns came up, which they handled it but just the workmanship was questionable, luckily I did research on what question to ask. And now there are still some unfinished things that said would be completed but nothing has been done and these are small things that was said to be done within the 10 day walk through and now its past the 30 day. So very unsatisfied. Thesewhole process felt rushed, especially the build process.

title: KB Home Builder poor workmanship
review_text: My home backyard drainage system is not properly installed by KB Home Builder. When it is raining weather conditions the lawn grasses is waterlogged and not draining. My home Backyard is a total Disgrace to KB Homes Builder during raining weather conditions. People cannot hangout in this Backyard. My investment to this home is a waste of money.

title: Painful Process
review_text: There is little communication between departments. Project schedules don’t appear to exist and failing to meet dates 7 times over doesn’t matter. Punch items take months to get to and not everything is resolved max

title: Sooo Many Issues!
review_text: Be sure to research the area your community is being or was built on. Get third party inspections cause the KB construction workers don’t take pride in there work. Keep a list of every repair and how long KB takes to fix it. Tiles started cracking in the second day of moving in the house. I been living here two months now and there are cracks all over the floors and walls in the house, not to mention varies other build issues. The construction manager will submit repair request for the first 45 days after you move in but after that good luck on getting anyone to come repair things. Icurrently got blue tape all over my walls, unpainted areas, and cracked tiles to name a few things while I wait for KB to give me a date in when the repairs will take place. I can’t even decorate for the holidays cause I will have to move it all when the repair guys come over. Go with a different builder if you have not already signed the contract

title: Poor Communication and multiple follow ups
review_text: There were multiple inspections with inadequate follow-ups and delayed communication. Due to several issues, we had to initiate follow-ups each time, as there appeared to be a lack of interest and initiative from the builder’s side.

title: Looks pretty… but falls apart quickly
review_text: Buying this home is absolutely the biggest regret I have because it’s been nothing but constant headaches since the day I did my inspection. I was told when to schedule a third party inspection and both times the construction team was not prepared for it. I was told by the project manager that my expectations were to high and no house would ever be perfect, and I needed to have realistic expectations when trying to get the crooked wall, chunks of missing cabinets, and exposed nails fixed. I was told several times this is above model standard, yet I have no ability to do laundry, water sittingin walls growing mold, broken window, and it’s taken weeks to get a solution. My realtor was gaslit and ignored when addressing concerns before I showed up after closing and was told she couldn’t address the issues. I’ll never buy another KB home and I won’t recommend anyone I care about to either. I’ve had more issues in the brand new home than I ever did in a rental property.

title: Flagler Beach
review_text: We closed two months ago and are still awaiting major repairs, roof, driveway, flooring etc. maybe buy a fixer as the work would be done more timely and to a better standard.

title: Homebuying experience was awful
review_text: Before we sign the contracts for the new home we told the KB agent that we will be adding a second name to the tile and was told that we could add that person during the signing at the title company. When the signing day came was told by the title company that KB has to amendment our contract. So we went back to the KB agent told them what happen and was told by the agent that they can not provide amendment or change the contract. Which will create additional cost for us to hire a lawyer to deed our title . Also we were require 50k in earnest money deposit compare to our other homepurchase that was 7-10k by a different builder. We have to tell KB during the walk through there are lots of crack 2x4. that needed fix.

title: KB homes not a best builder to choose from
review_text: It was a hectic process to get your home closed. Few false promises made during the process. Workmanship is not at best and at the same time it is not worth for the money you pay.

title: Good until you close
review_text: Everything was good until we closed. Now there's minimal response to issues. Repaired a window and they said they would come back out to do the paint. Been waiting on paint for over a month. Still no word on when the people are coming to paint the window that they did repairs on. Oven quit working less than 2 months in the house. The number left to me for warranty repairs isn't valid anymore. Just like most other companies they'll do what it takes until the sale closes then you're on your own.

title: Veteran’s First Home
review_text: I was warned not to buy a KB Home and now I’m warning you. The craftsmanship is horrible. The sales agent and general contractors grossly misleads buyers. They have completely ruined my first home buying experience. You cannot get them to honor parts of the warranty. Purchase with a Pulte, Lennar or other builder. Trust me!!!

title: Not to happy
review_text: Just a heads up once you pass your 30 days in the home and have to deal with the warranty department you will sit and wait for them to get those items fix. You also do not get any updates so you are kept in the dark on the items that you submitted. So beware.

title: Interior designer
review_text: Quality control is a problem

title: Not good
review_text: We are very disappointed. We made a list of items that need to be fixed. We were told contractors were coming but we had mostly no shows. We would text and make calls and no response. Painters came in to do touch ups. No drop cloths and got paint on our rugs. My wife had to get paint out. There are no transition strips down and the rug is fraying. Never have I seen rugs installed and transition strips not put down. We only been in the house 3 months. The ceiling in the lanai is wavy due to the trusses/rafters being bowed. Garage floor is full of stains. Ledge in garage is crumbing. Tilein kitchen is crooked by 2 inches and the wall is not leveled. Tiles in master bathroom are beveled. The cement outside the house is sloppy workmanship. We had to go back to Pennsylvania for several weeks and we were promised that the work was going to get done. So far we been home for 3 weeks. As far as we can tell since we have cameras we only saw two people outside our house. We saw no one go in. We were promised pictures as the work was completed. So far we haven't received anything. Apparently every person we spoke to at Whiteview Village is experiencing the same thing. Some have been living there for almost a year and half and still have problems.

title: Terrible
review_text: Call me if there are any questions

title: Worst
review_text: The whole building process was kind of fustrating. Loan and all financial process was smooth and easy. To be honest, I wouldn't recommend KB to anyone.

title: Total facade
review_text: I purchased this home as to be our last home. We have had nothing but issues with loud Mexican music played by construction workers. Fire alarms that come on randomly for no reason. Door seels that are improperly installed. Expensive filters for the home, four different types. Carpet is standard in the dining area, in other words you're eating on carpeted floors; so 70's. Poor home construction, walls feel like paint on cardboard. Do not buy a home here! I'm looking to sell and only been here two months, yes, it's that bad! I moved from the ghetto to the ghetto, a complete facade put on by KBhomes.

title: Quality, price and workmanship poor, communication and follow up failed miserably.
review_text: I have lived in my house for 3 months now and I still have items that have not been fixed. Zero communication and the complaints that I have submitted have been tossed aside. They don’t care, they have their money and screw the consumer. Very disappointed in this company.

title: Worst Decision Ever…….
review_text: Date given for closing -house wasn’t ready. Once closing was done a multitude of inappropriate installments were noted.. For example: shower pan was installed it had a crack and empty spaces underfoot. Requested a new one and the gentleman stated it was malfunction from the manufacturer and they didn’t understand why it was installed. The shower door leaked due to little to no silicone per gentleman removing it. After replacing shower pan plastic cover not removed because it was no one’s job. So we removed it and caulking around tile and pan has yet to be redone 3 weeks later. The groutbetween pan and tile floor also has not been done. The walls had broken Sheetrock installed bulging the walls. Still waiting for that repair. The grounds was never leveled and lowest grade of grass applied so the landscape is uneven and a hazard. They totally made a mess of the garage floor and took it upon themselves to epoxy it to cover the screw up without consulting the buyer. The door frames appear to be the rejects so are the cheapest you can buy as you see the dents at different parts of the wood. Paint is watered down so if you pass a rag you see the sheetrock The cabinets ill painted and toe kicks 1” short throughout the house. Kyle- superintendent Chris warranty manager Johnny brought in to take over for Kyle all talk the talk but don’t walk the walk. All they are concerned with is how I was going to relate them. Looking for a ten I gave them an opportunity to make the corrections. They would call one company at a time and then book 2-3 weeks out. When asked for phone numbers for warranty department it has never been provided. Requested all companies be called simultaneously since they didn’t interfere with one another. That never happened it wasn’t even acknowledged. These were my major concerns. Advice to future KB HOME buyers- Don’t buy from KB Avoid the stress, headache and waste of time for services needed to be completed. I was told KB ‘s tactics is to waste time until your warranty is over. I believe in seeing the good in people but I guess I should have listened to the ones I trusted. Lesson learned.

title: Emperor
review_text: Don't buy one is my advice.

title: Not Satisfied with the Home we moved-in
review_text: We moved into a Model home which was a sales Office earlier. Prior to moving-in (at the time of purchase), we were provided a lot of assurances but when close to the move-in date, sales representative was not agreeing to none of the items agreed upon. For Ex: the carpet was really Bad and nothing was done to address the issue. Similarly with the doors. The only saving grace was the construction manager Joaquin. He ensured all the defects highlighted on moving-in were fixed. Over all did not have a positive experience and also felt I had paid 20K more than the market value.

title: Frustrating Overall
review_text: I expected some problems when building a new house but was shocked at the subpar craftsmanship and communication I experienced during this process. I still have many items on my to-do list that I brought up before closing that haven't been addressed four months later. After moving in, I was rushed to take my 30-day survey early and pressured to give a good review, even though one was not deserved, and then literally forgotten about. This whole time, I have been patient and understanding as I know they are busy and overworked, but sadly, that has only gotten me pushed around and overlooked.

title: OVER RATED. OVER INFLATED.
review_text: STAY AWAY WHILE YOU CAN. DOWNSTAIR RESTROOM IS FLAWED.....44 DEGREES DURING THE WINTER? KB BLAMES THIS ON THE BUYER FOR SELECTING TILES..BECAUSE THAT IS WHAT MAKES IT COLD.

title: The construction team member Lorenzo you need to fire him. He only talk, give promises to do work for you but never put it in to work.
review_text: Really bad experiences. Once they get their reviews you never gonna see them

title: Very poorly built home
review_text: Going on 2 months now and none of the issues have been addressed or even made appointments. Doing business with KB is like going on a dating site. All nice and good at the beginning but once you discovered issues KB ghosts you. My sprinkler system is flooding, so I’m wondering if KB is going to replace my grass. Dishwasher and Stove need to be replaced. One of the bedrooms has a dead switch. Where my walls meet the ceiling are not even straight. I also had the carpets removed. While removing the carpet, nails and trash was discovered under the pad. Good thing I’ve had my tetanusshot. I would like to get these issues fixed asap so I can sell the home. Very disappointed in the poor build of a “quality home”.

title: Disappointing
review_text: House wasn’t even completed on closing day and kb homes didn’t even have paperwork ready to close when they picked the date to close. Craftsman ship is disappointing. Got my microwave stolen while in building phase. Landscaping is dying and uneven and mostly dirt and grass. Sodding isn’t even up to code. Cracks In driveways and inside garage and only lived in for 2 months. They said the settling is “normal” but the cracks will only get worse down the road.

title: Not the best but it's quick
review_text: KB homes is straight-forward to deal with. You know the prices and options you will get. They move fast in construction! But, that may be at the cost of quality. They do not use the highest quality materials and some of the labor is sloppy. It is nice to have a home and they price it so that you can buy it, but they are not necessarily a good value. Quality just isn't there. And then when you want something fixed.... everything should be smooth and quick but you get a million run-arounds which is unfortunate. Probably would be better to just do things correctly the first time.

title: Very bad build quality, materials and workmanship
review_text: The build quality is not at all good, the materials used are sub par. The workmanship is not at all good. Seems like some armature who has no experience has built the home. There are scratches on the marbles in washrooms and kitchen sink. Kitchen cabinets have so many damages like literally looks as if it is 10 years old. Dry wall putty is not put properly and chips off and cracks everywhere angles were not put properly for doors and windows. Door frames have damages everywhere even before they were handed over. The sales team literally said if you don’t want to purchase then you can back outand we will sell it to someone else asap when asked to fix these issues.

title: Kb home
review_text: Materials used are cheap and coming apart. KB waits until day before 30 day inspection to try and fix things. They never came back to fix the rest of our issues

title: Sloppy
review_text: House looks like it was built in a rush. No quality control was implemented though the building process; lots of details were overlooked. Response to repair issues with the house extremely slow to none.

title: No
review_text: Too bad for warranty,I have problems on countertop and then I wait long time ,nobody help

title: Terrible experience
review_text: I haven’t been in my house for two months and things are falling apart. Very cheap materials and poor construction. Very unimpressive.

title: I was warned but I gave KB the benefit of doubt which is a huge mistake!
review_text: To begin with, the initial experience was all great where we could pick a lot, pick the floor plan, elevation and choose the structural changes and select the interiors of our liking. Good Things: 1. We are happy with the masonry work and how the elevation turned out. 2. Plenty of options for the selection of base floor plan and elevation Bad Things and areas where the quality is poor: 1. The craftsman ship for the interiors is sub par and we are not happy at all with the finish of drywalls, how the corners appear, the materials used for the doors and windows. 2. Drywalls: There aremany areas of the house where the coffered ceilings, drywalls, kitchen island walls looks crooked, finish is not good and its not aesthetically pleasing. 3. Painters have not used tapes to avoid paint bleeding on to the edges of cabinets, countertops, other colored walls on the outside and on the garage opener etc 4. Cabinets - Had scratches, paint drops, smudges at many places, we documented everything and shared it with the construction manager before closing but we were promised that this will be taken care after closing by scheduling for a cabinet punch. When the cabinets guy arrived he is blaming it on painters/construction workers and did not fix all the issues. 4. Flooring - We chose Vinyl planking but beware of the floating types, we saw the model homes which was glued however for us they installed a floating type which is shaky at places and makes thumping sound when we walk on some areas, its not a great experience walking on it. We were not explained about this type at the time of selection in design studio and issues we reported were not fixed. 5. Poor quality electric outlets and switches, gaps all around the switch boards/outlets developed within few months. 6. The window sills are not even, the drywall where it meets the sill and windows are crooked, finish of the wood used for the sills looks bad but no quality check is performed and installed without caring for the end product and the for the aesthetic appeal. 7. Garage door is not having a proper seal and lot of mice are entering often which has become a big headache for us. 8. The concrete floor in the garage, patio and driveway has lot of imperfections and holes made due to improper care during construction Also beware of the sales team, some are nice but the one we got was not honest and hid a lot of information and we learnt he lied blatantly and discriminated between different buyers.

title: HORRIBLE EXPERIENCE POST MOVE IN
review_text: Since I have moved into my home (and even before closing for that matter) I have had a laundry list of things that were incomplete or not working. These issues range in size from small to large (broken cabinet all the way to leaky irrigation). The lack of communication between KB and their partner companies has been atrocious. Not only that, but the lack of communication to me has been horrible. I get that everyone is busy and y'all are trying to get up as many houses as possible but this has been a horrendous experience and I would advise people to stay away. Let me give you a couple ofspecific examples: 1. When I did my final walkthrough on May 25th (closing date of May 26th) there was a broken cabinet that I pointed out to my construction supervisor. It was finally taken care of today (7/19) after multiple outreaches from myself. 2. I didn't end up moving into the house until June 10th and on June 12th I noticed a leak in the irrigation system. I texted my construction supervisor on June 12th. I heard back from him on June 14th that he was going to swing by. By that point I had already received notice from the City of Austin that I had a leak for 30+ hours at 10 gallons an hour. The proposed solution was to turn off the irrigation system until their people could come out. So that's what we did. Their people didn't come out until July 12th when they told me "no leak" with no further explanation so we turned the water back on to the irrigation system. Low and behold, 3 days later I get ANOTHER email from the City of Austin saying I've had a leak for 48 hours at 11 gallons an hour. Keep in mind, I didn't receive a single notification about a leak while the water to the irrigation was turned off. Fast forward to today and the same guy comes out and tells me again, "no leak". Unbelievable 3. One of my issues since before I moved in was the fact that there was a 3 inch gap between the fence and the ground. The proposed solution was to have them come lay another layer of sod to lessen the gap. Well, 2 months later and the guys came out today to do that. EXCEPT, they didn't bother to knock on my door or confirm what needed to be done. Instead, I walk out of my office to see 2 guys in my back yard ripping up the grass in the middle of the yard! Nowhere near the fence. They clearly thought they were there to replace all the dead grass because my water hasn't been getting watered because, as you will remember, MY IRRIGATION SYSTEM IS STILL BROKEN!!!!! I send a picture of 2 men with shovels digging up my yard to my construction manager, warranty manager, and the sales person with no response. Again, this has been such a nightmare of an experience. I have tried to be cool and understanding but the lack of communication between all parties and lack of timeliness has me ready to jump out of a window!

title: Issue after issue but it’s decent
review_text: I love my house but it’s low quality work

title: DEALING WITH IT
review_text: The overall experience was a shade above terrible. As expected, the home took approximately 12 months from signing the contract to closing. For nearly 8-9 months we dealt with an unresponsive Construction Manager - named Scott and a similarly unresponsive Sales Rep named Bob (Ok. Bob was a tad-bit more responsive but very unreliable). Not only were they unresponsive, they were unclear in their communications creating much heartache and the potential loss of a sell. The only reason we remained on contract with the purchase of this home, is because of Brandon. Unbeknownst to us, Brandon wasassigned as our new Construction Manager the last two-three months of this process drastically improving our experience. I feel confident in saying that had Brandon been our Construction Manager from the onset, KB would have gotten better ratings. Brandon was professional, honest, and understanding of our concerns and issues. He immediately addressed issues that had been outstanding for nearly 6 months (i.e. when there's a hole in your brand new roof during the winter and spring months, that's an issue that should be taken care off immediately). Brandon single handedly saved this sell. The KB's closing process (not included in the ratings) was also a mess, but because it's not on this rating scale I won't go into those issues.

title: Worse response time I’ve ever seen.
review_text: I want to start off by saying Ryan has been trying his best during this time however I’m still extremely dissatisfied with the follow up with issues regarding the home itself. The buying process was not a problem, I am satisfied with that part of this process. Tyler has been absolutely abysmal with responding to issues that have been constantly happening, such as a light in my kitchen that has been left unscrewed since 2 days off buying this home. During the last week of the purchase I asked Ryan if I could get my private inspector to come in and check the home after the closing and heconfirmed I could so I went ahead and did that and the inspector found things that need immediate immediate attention. So I message Tyler and Ryan a text message and long story short I haven’t received anything but a phone call from Tyler that I missed. At this point I would like to work with someone else.

title: I WOULDN'T RECOMMEND KB HOMES TO ANYONE, WORST BUILDERS, VERY POOR QUALITY WORK
review_text: I WOULDN'T RECOMMEND KB HOMES TO ANYONE, IT'S THE WORST BUILDERS, VERY CHEAP AND POOR QUALITY!!! IM VERY DISAPPOINTED IN MY HOME, WISH I WOULD HAVE WENT WITH ANOTHER BUILDER!!!

title: Choose a different builder
review_text: The process wasn't delightful. I was given false hope several times and was offered things I didn't receive. If I could do it all over, I wouldn't choose KB homes.

title: Dan is the worst
review_text: The house I purchased was not new but broken, ruined, damaged.... I overpaid for this home based on the quality and workmanship. I have been requesting repairs for months. These repairs should have been made prior to me purchasing this home. I will never buy a KB home again. I asked for you to take your home back but you refuse to do that. I do not want a KB Home. My opinion is just getting worse not better with each person or issue.

title: Overall experience
review_text: Beginning with getting pre-approval through KB Homes the rep failed to do diligent work and could not get me pre-qualified for VA loan. However, outside loan servicer was able to easily do a qualification for the loan then incentives through Kb Homes were promised and it took multiple levels of leadership to approve this. Closing was pushed twice because Supertindent made promises to complete all work identified in the final walkthrough prior to closing and that did not happen. Currently still trying to get the work done from issues identified in final walkthrough prior to closing. All workthat was completed prior to closing was very poor and the overall construction of the house has terrible finishing work that illustrates laziness in contractors and a bad process for ensuring quality assurance. Overall I would rate the experience as terrible at every level of service besides Adrian being polite, professional and proficient at his duties

title: Rob was most helpful the last day before we settled.
review_text: We felt rushed to close, when the house was not even completed. Also the workmanship left a lot to be desired. We still have things that need to be fixed due to the shotty workmanship.

title: Low quality of construction !
review_text: Low quality of construction ! Sales service needs to be improved. No follow up or updates on next steps upon entering into agreement. KB home needs business, not customer satisfaction.Post buying experience and responsiveness to issues is satisfactory.

title: Terrible workmanship
review_text: Over all its just another track home with low quality material & poor workmanship. There are plenty of hairline cracks acros the drywall joints where the tape didn’t have enough mud behind it . The Construction Superintendent tryed to charge me cash for a stone design that was already included in the blue prints. Matt The warranty fellow is terrible at responding the phone, text & emails so far not a great experience. A few things need to be repaired such as my front door being unsquared, my dryer braker is completely out , and also the GFCI outside patio outlet trips every time i plugsomething in . BEAWARE kb does not care about quality.

title: Horrible
review_text: The time to build was too long for the size of the house. The quality of work is horrible. The neighborhood has a lot of self centered residents Wish I would have known. I would have looked elsewhere

title: Horrible
review_text: My experience was not great at all. Everything was rushed to close hearing from other workers around working on the home. I was told that KB rushes homes to get #’s skipping big quality checks. employees get treated like numbers no value to the home buyers just searching for good surveys to getting popularity numbers and land to keep building. I’ll be selling my home due to so many things to fix and chasing warranty and kb superiors to resolve once I finished my survey I was let go and no one reached out. I will suggest buy else we’re kb Home isn’t the same 3 years back they were number 1!Now they need a change.

title: Terrible
review_text: Been in the house only a few months and we've already decided at the end of the year we're going to sell. Multiple issues including leaks, countertop issues, issues with appliances, paint issues , quality issues, customer service issues, landscaping issues, and more. Customer service has been extremely useless with the exception of one or two helpful incidents. I will never purchase another KB home in my life. What was supposed to be our forever home has now just turned into a short-term living situation.

title: Horrible service post closing
review_text: After closing customer service has been horrible. 10-day visit work has been pending for 8 months. Hate how you deal with customers post-closing

title: The American Dream Deferred: A Homeowner's Nightmare with KB Home
review_text: I recently had the unfortunate experience of building a home with KB Home in the Winn Ridge community in Aubrey, Texas, and I would not recommend this builder to anyone. From the start, our project was plagued with delays and issues, from the pause in construction for three months to allow for resources to be devoted to other projects, to struggling to pass pre-dry wall inspections, and ordering materials short of what was needed. The project team seemed unable to deliver on reasonable estimate timelines, causing great inconvenience to my family, and we ultimately missed a 120 daylock rate agreement with KB Home Loans. The lack of professionalism and empathy from the project team was also a major issue, and it felt like they needed help to get the job done. We were left feeling disappointed and stressed, and it has been over two months since we moved in and our sentiment has not changed. In conclusion, my experience with KB Home was far from the American Dream and I would not recommend this builder to anyone.

title: Stress every step of the way - Price changes, Poor Quality, Fights with Contractors - UGH
review_text: This was extremely stressful - starting with the price I was quoted going up $3k bc my salesman went on a vacation. Ktichen Tile was wrong had to be pulled out, wrong sales sheets, had to fight for the deposit I gave to Design Center to show on sales closing sheet, lots of errors financially I had to keep on top of. Contractor refused to fix problems pointed out my home inspector. They insisted I fly in for closing on 11/30 and the home was NOT even CLOSE to being able to close. Finally (still with problems left to fix) we closed 12/16 - Problems and stress every step of the way. Even ClosingPapers had cross outs with money changes; a nightmare and very unprofessional Title Company. I would hesitate to recommend anyone to buy from this home manufacture - all they were concerned about was getting their money. We had a cash deal - we held out until the errors were corrected, but I had to return so we finally closed still with things left wrong in the house. This is a 1/2 million dollar home; we should not have been treated like this.

title: Don’t do it !!
review_text: This is the worst quality of work. The worst response times, and the most problem I have ever had w a new build ever. I would never buy another KB home. Never

title: Don’t close until absolutely everything is fixed!!!
review_text: The build was late. 2 months after living in the house and we have NEVER had a working microwave. Issues have been outstanding since moving in, week after week we’re told ‘it will be done this week’ and nothing ever ends up happening. It makes it worse that these are minor issues that could all be done within an hour or 2. It’s annoying as it is a prime example that KB hire works with poor workmanship that obviously don’t take pride in their work, these items were obviously also overlooked by the management who should have picked it up.

title: Much promised, some delivered
review_text: While the home KB delivered is certainly a fine home, I'd wager there are ways to get a fine home with less headache. After being in the home a while, it's clear where some costs were cut. Certain things were unfinished or wrong: a chandelier with no lightbulbs, the door to the garage was installed backward, a nail sticking out of the stairs, a shrub promised in the front that has never been installed, several dozen large nails left on the deck/backyard. Many of these left undone after I was told they would all be fixed. A free additional bucket of paint was promised that I've never received.Additionally, it has been very difficult to get ahold of people and to hear back. As I mentioned, it's a fine home, but I'm sure there are many other companies that can build a fine home and have better communication.

title: Disrespectful, disappointing
review_text: Sales was awesome, knowledgeable, helpful, present. Rest: refer to title.

title: Very bad Response and False promise
review_text: I would not recommend to my friends to buy KB Home and totally frustrating with those false statements. they will not be considered once we have closed down homes.

title: 100% paid, partially delivered
review_text: There was a long list of issues that were documented prior to closing that I am still struggling to remediate. Some of the issues were dealt. However, big ticket items like kitchen cabinet is still pending with no visibility to resolution. The Sales staff, Construction Staff, and Customer Service staff are NOT the problem. The Staff are all super nice. But the process doesn't lead to resolutions. Had I known that the warranty claims process would be this time-consuming, I would have never closed on the house. Buying a KB home feels like I am dealing with an insurance firm, where gettingclaims dealt takes forever with zero visibility to issue resolution.

title: Terribly rushed and resulting bad quality
review_text: KB had a lot of supply chain and labor problems as did all builders. So they rushed the inspection process to get to closing and get my money. After that it has been like pulling teeth to get them to finish the items that they PROMISED to take care of. Now that winter has set in I expect most items will not be addressed until spring. In retrospect, I would recommend have your own inspector submit a report before closing and to not close until the items are addressed. Don't count on KB to fix things after you close. And they continually harassed me top submit this review, so here it is.

title: Check EVERYTHING at Orientation
review_text: Do not let them bully you into signing if you're not satisfied.

title: Poor quality / Workmanship / Responsiveness
review_text: WoW, the home build quality is as low as it can get. Exemplifies that the company is having severe trouble engaging skilled workers compared to its competitors. Every. corner of the house displays extremely poor workmanship (walls, ceilings, paint, baseboards, doors etc.). I am hoping that the framing, electrical work, plumbing is ok). Will only find out after some time elapses. The biggest culprit though is poor responsiveness to get the issues addressed by the construction operations. They are busy building homes and then closing them as soon as possible to get money without any sincereintention to ensure homes are delivered with quality nor on providing good customer service. No wonder, survey conducted for home owners (who closed on their Gomes since 2021) shows that Kbhome is ranked at the rock bottom of the 20 builders based on the quality of its delivered home and customer satisfaction. I hope that company looks past it old glory and start focusing on quality over quantity.

title: house delivered unfinished
review_text: The house was not ready at the time of delivery, it was even closed two months ago and the house still needs things to fix, the bathroom window, drywall, cabinets with damage, which are supposed to be new and they went and patched it up. I think that when you buy something new it is new and not patched up since I paid for a completely new house It's been two months since I moved and it hasn't been 100% complete I only had 1 walk and 1 day before closing I think it is disrespectful to the buyer that we pay for good quality and service which has not been provided to me.

There is a crack in my ceiling, 2 doors don't close completely, water pools in the backyard, porch is missing bricks, roof has issues, siding, large space between the cabinet and wall, no caulking in areas, flooring is cut poorly (it doesn't even meet the wall in some areas). In the amount of time it took to complete this house, its shameful to have so many issues. But I digress... I'm actually really sad about thinking aboutit. The new Superintendent (I think that's his title), Duane is been made aware and is working to rectify issues... I hope he doesn't leave or get moved. He's been helpful. Had to extensively screw drywall to 2x4s as ceiling was sagging. Huge crack in our hallway he was suppose to finished it was stopped. We believe he was reprimanded for doing it right, hallway not finished, now no one sent to paint over seams, just a complete stop. Cracked tiles, one loose. Driveway and garage door never power-washed, as well driveway looks mismatched in color, dirt brown, and garage door filthy. Left hole in yard, did not fill in just put sod over it. Gravel, not dirt under areas of sod. Door seal, large gap front door 2. back door seals looks old and used, looks bad. Btw was told that would be taken care of it. Mismatched baseboards kitchen, living room, which we paid extra for. No foam insulation around stove vent or sink. title: Dissatisfied
review_text: So we bought a house 7 Oct. we did not like the experience with KB. When they offered a high APR & wanted us to buy down the rate. We went to our lender that offered way lower APR but then KB wanted to match it but wanted my lender to do a promise letter. Neither lenders wanted to do this so this took up time. We had refused to go with KB if they were going to match it because of they could match it now then they could have matched it in the beginning. Unfortunately we had to go with KB because they refused to give us the $4000 rebate and closing cost. Then it was a rush to do the walkthrough of the house. We closed on 7 Oct. walk through was 4 Oct. 2nd walk through was 6 Oct & did this not give them enough time to fix thing we found or that the inspector found. We got, “yes, that got fixed” when it didn’t. The AC gate was bent and that didn’t get replaced during the initial, 2nd walk through or the 30 day walk through. So this makes me doubt that they got any of the other things fixed. We are still waiting for things to be fixed and we are two months into living in this house.

title: They're just trying to get you into a house
review_text: So if you're a 1st time homebuyer with no options, then this may be the right builder for you. Here's my process from beginning to end. Go into the sales office and take a look at the houses and decide if you want to buy or not. After that you get put on a waiting list where their mortgage officer takes at least a whole month to reach out to you after being told week after week from your sales rep that someone should be reaching out to you. Finally, the mortgage officer sees if you're qualified by giving you a ridiculous loan that sticks with you till closing day (so go with another lender).Then months go by before your sales rep finally reaches out to you to setup design studio appointments. The first appointment is virtual, so you'll have to go off your best guess if you want something or not based on pictures. Then second appointment is where you can see the material in your house in person; once all your choices, and you've paid up; you can't change anything. Even if they haven't started construction. Now the best part is the construction where you're excited to see how your house will turn out. You'll have to watch them build because they'll forget the things you've picked, so it's very important that you watch them closely. You get 2 reviews to see how far the house has come, and make suggestions/improvements. Now here comes to the closing process. Once you've signed off on your incomplete house (that they promise to fix); you get to close on it. After living in the house for 2 months you've come to find out that they won't fix the things they've promised to fix. For example, I've been out of a microwave for 2 months. There's some minor details I wish that were taken care of, but hey it was my mistake for trusting them. The house is nice, so that's why there's 2 stars instead of 1.

title: Poor Quality Control and Lack of Follow Through
review_text: The sales and design team were great, we designed a home we were looking forward to. We got word of our closing date and would have the new home orientation the day before and the home was not finished with many issues. We were asked to come back the next day before our closing and we were promised the house would be finished with a final clean completed. The next day we arrived to much the same home. The house was missing pantry shelves, the countertop had screw holes in it and wasn’t secure to the cabinets, the flooring transitions were not secured, outlets we asked to be moved more than amonth prior we’re still not moved, garage door opener was not installed, the house had not been fully cleaned and a full host of other issues. We were promised that while we were at the closing the shelves would be installed, same with the garage door opener, the electrician would move the outlet and the countertop would be fixed as well as a final clean. We were lied too, none of these things happened. To get these issues fixed we had to go through the warranty service. Yes, to get items that should have been installed we had to go to the warranty service. It took over a month after closing to get our shelves in the pantry. It is now almost 2 months after we closed and the list of issues is long and have yet to be addressed by KB Homes. I would not purchase a KB Home again.

title: KB Home - The initial completion date was not respected due to Covid-19
review_text: The initial completion date was not respected due to Covid-19 related issues, but once i moved in, i noticed minor issues with the property, such as the concrete slab that we paid for, which was not installed and other structural issues with the property. We were also told of a 10 day follow up review, which never took place. Some knobs are loose, and all of this is happening as we just moved in.

title: Luxury price points, but non-luxury homebuying experience
review_text: Even though the homes at Solano were priced at $1.5M-$1.7M, which are pretty nice single family homes even for Irvine, we felt like the KB homebuying experience was more in line with what one would expect from condos/townhomes well below the $1M mark. It was NOT a luxury homebuying experience. DESIGN CENTER The design options at the Design Center were way too limited for homes at this price point and not high quality or at the level of luxury that is warranted by homes at this price point. Also, the Design Center was understaffed on the Leonard's front and the process not too manual. Thisled to numerous mistakes that carried over into the installation of our upgraded materials. How about using a bar code system for our upgrade selections rather than manually entering them into the computer / relaying it to another person? CONSTRUCTION There were numerous mistakes made by both KB installers, as well as the sub-contractors (Leonard's, Excel Cabinets). The builder grade materials are not much better than what a buyer could get a Home Depot. There's no pride in the workmanship. Many walls aren't straight. The painting was rushed and there are marks in the walls. The edges of doors are not smooth. Items like the toilets and toilet paper holders were not installed in the optimal spot in the bathrooms. There are gaps in the wood floors and on the kitchen backsplash. Grout and caulking are applied sloppily. My kitchen island countertop was initially installed at a diagonal. It looks like the installers came back and straightened it out, BUT in so doing, they simply removed cabinet panels and re-installed them leaving nail marks very visible. COMMUNICATIONS I believe that the Solano team (Sales, Design Center, Superintendent) met every Monday, but for some reason, the communications were not always aligned on timelines. This led to us paying for an appraisal too early in the process and that was wasted money. We were kept apprised of all timelines moving, and appreciated the KB team's flexibility in terms of fixing items in time for us to close, but we went all the way down to the wire with our lender and closed on the last possible day once final inspections were done.

title: Our dream home disaster

We were in the house for nearly a month, then found out we had mold. We have since been removed from the home awaiting remediation. This has been an absolute nightmare, but we are working through the remaining issues. Additionally, the overall build quality was average at best with some areas still needing repairs or rework. All of this after almost two months after closing. I wouldn't do it again.

review_text: Got my house with things missing, the house was dirty, appliances, hardware, missing. They miss calculated the amount of closing, had to pull extra money in. It was a hot mess.. still waiting for things to be resolved.

title: Terrible experience
review_text: KB Home and its subcontractors did not install the flooring and portions of the baseboard in my new home correctly, within industry standards or within manufacturers recommendations/specifications. Throughout the build process the original superintendent and regional supervisor were dismissive and patronizing. They disregarded everything I said, they ignored all the information I provided them that supported my claims and they tried to make me feel like I was being unreasonable. They even questioned the validity of my research and supporting documentation, stating “[I] don’t know where yougot your information from”, when I clearly provided source information in multiple communications. They also stated that they had spoken with the Contractors Board and the tile manufacturer and there were “no standards they were required to follow”. However, they was not able to provide anything to substantiate this claim. KB Home flat out disregarded standards set by an ANSI-accredited organization, the ICC and multiple manufacturer’s recommendations/specifications; the result is egregiously substandard workmanship and a brand-new home that insights anger and frustration instead of happiness and comfort.

title: Don’t Do It
review_text: The model home was impeccable, your home will not be. We paid thousands in upgrading almost everything that you could in their base model home, and our home looks like a college kid slapped everything together with a hammer and some nails. Cabinets are crooked, chipped, cracked, and missing hardware. Vinyl floors are warped like you are walking on an uneven gravel road, same experience with carpet (the subfloors are not settling correctly). Paint throughout the home is uneven, finishes are horrible (it looks like a college kid painted the home in a few hours) paint easily scuffs and chips, sodon’t plan to even touch the walls because you will either chip or blemish the paint. Upgraded canned lights are not flush with the ceilings and have paint all over them (according to the superintendent, this is my responsibility to get them flush, he claims they cannot get it perfect). Window seals are horrible and have tons of draft, and patio slider does not close properly and has a lot of draft as well. Upgraded kitchenaid appliances are malfunctioning and installed crooked, but it is your responsibility to get it fixed, KB Homes will not help with that. Door frames are crooked (KB Homes has to come in and reframe/reset many doors throughout the house because they do not close now). Trim work is horrible, you will have large gaps between floors and trim (the excuse is they use “floating floors” but I am in real-estate, and I have seen much higher quality installs with “floating floors” I suspect the sub floors are either settling wrong or installed incorrectly. These are just a few of the issues I could think of. Spend your money elsewhere.

review_text: We had many items on our list from the walkthrough that needed to be addressed. Some minor and some major flaws. 6 weeks later nothing done or committed to being fixed. Spent $$$ on upgrades. Upgraded wood floors have defective boards, whitish color where stain is missing, many scratches and holes. Upgraded cabinets, inconsistent stain. whitish stain on several doors, scratches and doors falling off their hinges.
